Create `core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-retention.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
runtime_latest_checkpoint() {
  local latest latest_cursor latest_generation candidate cursor generation
  latest=''; latest_cursor=-1; latest_generation=-1
  for candidate in "$RUNTIME_DIR"/checkpoints/*.json; do
    [ -f "$candidate" ] || continue
    cursor=$(jq -r '.cursor // -1' "$candidate" 2>/dev/null || printf '%s' -1)
    case $cursor in ''|*[!0-9]*) continue;; esac
    generation=$(jq -r '.checkpoint_generation // 0' "$candidate")
    if [ "$cursor" -gt "$latest_cursor" ] || {
      [ "$cursor" -eq "$latest_cursor" ] && [ "$generation" -gt "$latest_generation" ];
    }; then latest=$candidate; latest_cursor=$cursor; latest_generation=$generation; fi
  done
  [ -z "$latest" ] || printf '%s\n' "$latest"
}
runtime_verify_checkpoint() {
  local checkpoint expected state_hash
  checkpoint=$1
  [ -f "$checkpoint" ] && [ ! -L "$checkpoint" ] || return 5
  expected=${checkpoint##*-}; expected=sha256:${expected%.json}
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$checkpoint")" = "$expected" ] || return 5
  state_hash=$(jq -r .state_hash "$checkpoint")
  jq -Sc .state "$checkpoint" >"$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-state.$$"
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-state.$$")" = "$state_hash" ] || {
    rm -f "$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-state.$$"; return 5; }
  rm -f "$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-state.$$"
}
runtime_verify_raw_chain() {
  local first first_sequence previous expected anchored current current_cursor current_hash
  local checkpoint anchor_sequence anchor_previous event sequence
  first=$(find "$RUNTIME_DIR/events" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.json' | LC_ALL=C sort | sed -n '1p')
  [ -n "$first" ] || return 0
  first_sequence=$(jq -r .sequence "$first")
  previous=$(jq -r '.previous_event_hash // ""' "$first"); expected=$first_sequence; anchored=0
  [ "$first_sequence" -ne 1 ] || { [ -z "$previous" ] || return 5; anchored=1; }
  current=$(runtime_current_dir) || return 5
  current_cursor=$(jq -r .last_event_sequence "$current/state.json")
  current_hash=$(jq -r '.last_event_hash // ""' "$current/state.json")
  checkpoint=$(runtime_latest_checkpoint); anchor_sequence=0; anchor_previous=''
  if [ -n "$checkpoint" ]; then
    runtime_verify_checkpoint "$checkpoint" || return 5
    anchor_sequence=$(jq -r '.retained_chain.first_sequence // 0' "$checkpoint")
    anchor_previous=$(jq -r '.retained_chain.previous_event_hash // ""' "$checkpoint")
  fi
  if [ "$first_sequence" -eq $((current_cursor + 1)) ] && [ "$previous" = "$current_hash" ]; then anchored=1; fi
  while IFS= read -r event; do
    if [ "$anchor_sequence" = "$expected" ] && [ "$anchor_previous" = "$previous" ]; then anchored=1; fi
    runtime_verify_event_file "$event" "$expected" "$previous" || return 5
    previous=$(jq -r .event_hash "$event"); expected=$((expected + 1))
    sequence=$(jq -r .sequence "$event")
    if [ "$sequence" = "$current_cursor" ]; then [ "$previous" = "$current_hash" ] || return 5; anchored=1; fi
  done < <(find "$RUNTIME_DIR/events" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.json' | LC_ALL=C sort)
  [ "$anchored" -eq 1 ] || return 5
}
runtime_replay() {
  local work checkpoint cursor previous event sequence revision
  work=$RUNTIME_DIR/.replay.$$
  rm -rf "$work"; mkdir "$work"
  checkpoint=$(runtime_latest_checkpoint)
  if [ -n "$checkpoint" ]; then
    runtime_verify_checkpoint "$checkpoint" || { rm -rf "$work"; return 5; }
    jq '.state' "$checkpoint" >"$work/state.json"
  else
    [ -f "$RUNTIME_DIR/snapshots/1/state.json" ] || { rm -rf "$work"; return 5; }
    cp "$RUNTIME_DIR/snapshots/1/state.json" "$work/state.json"
  fi
  cursor=$(jq -r .last_event_sequence "$work/state.json")
  previous=$(jq -r '.last_event_hash // ""' "$work/state.json")
  while IFS= read -r event; do
    sequence=$(jq -r .sequence "$event"); [ "$sequence" -gt "$cursor" ] || continue
    runtime_verify_event_file "$event" "$((cursor + 1))" "$previous" || { rm -rf "$work"; return 5; }
    runtime_apply_event "$work/state.json" "$event" "$work/next.json"
    mv "$work/next.json" "$work/state.json"
    cursor=$sequence; previous=$(jq -r .event_hash "$event")
  done < <(find "$RUNTIME_DIR/events" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.json' | LC_ALL=C sort)
  revision=$((cursor + 1))
  jq --argjson revision "$revision" '.revision=$revision' "$work/state.json" >"$work/final.json"
  cat "$work/final.json"
  rm -rf "$work"
}
runtime_compact() {
  local retain_snapshots retain_events lock events_lock current cursor previous previous_hash
  local checkpoint_generation event_list total first_index first_retained first_sequence first_previous
  local temporary state_hash critical event checkpoint_hash checkpoint prune_count pruned old_event
  local current_revision snapshots snapshot_total remove removed revision
  retain_snapshots=3 retain_events=1000
  while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
    case $1 in
      --retain-snapshots) retain_snapshots=$2; shift 2 ;;
      --retain-events) retain_events=$2; shift 2 ;;
      *) return 2 ;;
    esac
  done
  lock=$RUNTIME_DIR/compaction.lock
  runtime_lock_acquire "$lock"
  events_lock=$RUNTIME_DIR/events.lock
  trap 'if runtime_lock_assert "$events_lock"; then runtime_lock_release "$events_lock"; fi;
    if runtime_lock_assert "$lock"; then runtime_lock_release "$lock"; fi' RETURN
  runtime_lock_acquire "$events_lock"
  runtime_reduce
  current=$(runtime_current_dir) || return 5
  runtime_verify_raw_chain || return 5
  cursor=$(jq -r '.last_event_sequence' "$current/state.json")
  previous=$(runtime_latest_checkpoint)
  previous_hash=null
  [ -z "$previous" ] || previous_hash=$(jq -Rn --arg v "$(runtime_sha256 "$previous")" '$v')
  checkpoint_generation=1
  [ -z "$previous" ] || checkpoint_generation=$(( $(jq -r '.checkpoint_generation // 0' "$previous") + 1 ))
  event_list=$RUNTIME_DIR/.compact-events.$$
  find "$RUNTIME_DIR/events" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name '*.json' | LC_ALL=C sort >"$event_list"
  total=$(wc -l <"$event_list" | tr -d ' '); first_index=$((total - retain_events + 1))
  [ "$first_index" -ge 1 ] || first_index=1
  first_retained=$(awk -v target="$first_index" 'NR==target{print;exit}' "$event_list")
  if [ -n "$first_retained" ]; then
    first_sequence=$(jq -r .sequence "$first_retained")
    first_previous=$(jq -c .previous_event_hash "$first_retained")
  else first_sequence=null; first_previous=null; fi
  temporary=$RUNTIME_DIR/checkpoints/.tmp.$$
  jq -Sc . "$current/state.json" >"$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-canonical.$$"
  state_hash=$(runtime_sha256 "$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-canonical.$$")
  critical=$RUNTIME_DIR/.critical.$$
  printf '%s\n' '[]' >"$critical"
  while IFS= read -r event; do
    jq -e '.type|IN("terminal_transaction","input_required","disk_pressure","recovery_action",
      "shutdown_advance","cycle_result","council_member_start","council_member_complete")' \
      "$event" >/dev/null || continue
    jq --arg file_hash "$(runtime_sha256 "$event")" --slurpfile event "$event" \
      '. + [{sequence:$event[0].sequence,type:$event[0].type,action_id:$event[0].action_id,
        event_hash:$event[0].event_hash,event_file_hash:$file_hash,event:$event[0]}]' "$critical" >"$critical.next"
    mv "$critical.next" "$critical"
  done <"$event_list"
  jq -n --argjson cursor "$cursor" --argjson checkpoint_generation "$checkpoint_generation" \
    --arg last_hash "$(jq -r '.last_event_hash // ""' "$current/state.json")" \
    --arg state_hash "$state_hash" --argjson previous_hash "$previous_hash" \
    --argjson first_sequence "$first_sequence" --argjson first_previous "$first_previous" \
    --slurpfile critical "$critical" --slurpfile state "$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-canonical.$$" \
    '{schema_version:1,checkpoint_generation:$checkpoint_generation,cursor:$cursor,
      last_event_hash:$last_hash,state_hash:$state_hash,
      previous_checkpoint_hash:$previous_hash,
      retained_chain:{first_sequence:$first_sequence,previous_event_hash:$first_previous},
      critical_events:$critical[0],state:$state[0]}' \
    >"$temporary"
  rm -f "$RUNTIME_DIR/.checkpoint-canonical.$$" "$critical"
  if [ "${POLYLANE_TEST_FAULT:-}" = compact-before-checkpoint ]; then
    runtime_lock_release "$events_lock"; runtime_lock_release "$lock"; trap - RETURN; return 99
  fi
  checkpoint_hash=$(runtime_sha256 "$temporary" | sed 's/^sha256://')
  checkpoint=$RUNTIME_DIR/checkpoints/$cursor-$checkpoint_hash.json
  chmod 400 "$temporary"; mv "$temporary" "$checkpoint"
  runtime_verify_checkpoint "$checkpoint" || return 5
  runtime_replay >"$RUNTIME_DIR/.replay-proof.$$"
  [ "$(jq -Sc . "$RUNTIME_DIR/.replay-proof.$$")" = "$(jq -Sc . "$current/state.json")" ] || return 5
  rm -f "$RUNTIME_DIR/.replay-proof.$$"
  if [ "${POLYLANE_TEST_FAULT:-}" = compact-after-checkpoint ]; then
    runtime_lock_release "$events_lock"; runtime_lock_release "$lock"; trap - RETURN; return 99
  fi
  prune_count=$((total - retain_events)); [ "$prune_count" -ge 0 ] || prune_count=0
  pruned=0
  while IFS= read -r old_event; do
    [ "$pruned" -lt "$prune_count" ] || break
    rm -f "$old_event"; pruned=$((pruned + 1))
    if [ "${POLYLANE_TEST_FAULT:-}" = compact-mid-event-prune ] && [ "$pruned" -eq 1 ]; then
      runtime_lock_release "$events_lock"; runtime_lock_release "$lock"; trap - RETURN; return 99
    fi
  done <"$event_list"
  rm -f "$event_list"
  current_revision=$(jq -r '.revision' "$current/state.json")
  snapshots=$RUNTIME_DIR/.compact-snapshots.$$
  find "$RUNTIME_DIR/snapshots" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -exec basename {} \; | LC_ALL=C sort -n >"$snapshots"
  snapshot_total=$(wc -l <"$snapshots" | tr -d ' '); remove=$((snapshot_total - retain_snapshots))
  [ "$remove" -ge 0 ] || remove=0
  removed=0
  while IFS= read -r revision; do
    [ "$removed" -lt "$remove" ] || break
    [ "$revision" = "$current_revision" ] && continue
    rm -rf "$RUNTIME_DIR/snapshots/$revision"; removed=$((removed + 1))
  done <"$snapshots"
  rm -f "$snapshots"
  find "$RUNTIME_DIR" -maxdepth 1 \( -name '.reduce.*' -o -name '.replay.*' \) -exec rm -rf {} +
  find "$RUNTIME_DIR/events" -maxdepth 1 -name '.tmp.*' -type f -delete
  dd if=/dev/zero of="$RUNTIME_DIR/recovery-reserve" bs=1048576 count=1 2>/dev/null
  runtime_lock_release "$events_lock"
  runtime_lock_release "$lock"
  trap - RETURN
}

runtime_wake() {
  local current pid token role generation live
  current=$(runtime_current_dir) || return 0
  pid=$(jq -r '.guardian.pid // 0' "$current/state.json")
  token=$(jq -r '.guardian.start_token // ""' "$current/state.json")
  role=$(jq -r '.guardian.role // ""' "$current/state.json")
  generation=$(jq -r '.guardian.generation // -1' "$current/state.json")
  case $role in guardian|launcher) : ;; *) return 0 ;; esac
  case $generation in ''|*[!0-9]*) return 0 ;; esac
  live=$(runtime_start_token "$pid" || true)
  [ -n "$live" ] && [ "$live" = "$token" ] || return 0
  # Never open the FIFO here: a FIFO write without a reader can block forever. USR1 is
  # coalescible; the durable event plus the guardian fallback tick guarantee no lost work.
  kill -USR1 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
}

runtime_run_with_deadline() {
  local seconds child started cadence now
  seconds=$1; shift
  [ "${1:-}" = -- ] || return 2
  shift
  "$@" & child=$!
  started=$(runtime_now)
  cadence=${POLYLANE_DEADLINE_POLL:-1}
  while kill -0 "$child" 2>/dev/null; do
    now=$(runtime_now)
    if [ $((now - started)) -ge "$seconds" ]; then
      kill -TERM "$child" 2>/dev/null || true
      sleep "$cadence"
      kill -KILL "$child" 2>/dev/null || true
      wait "$child" 2>/dev/null || true
      return 124
    fi
    sleep "$cadence"
  done
  wait "$child"
}
```

Create `core/scripts/polylane-runtime.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
umask 077
SELF=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd -P)
[ "${1:-}" = --dir ] || { printf 'expected --dir <runtime>\n' >&2; exit 2; }
RUNTIME_DIR=$2
shift 2
case $RUNTIME_DIR in /*) : ;; *) printf 'runtime path must be absolute\n' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac
. "$SELF/lib/polylane-runtime-common.sh"
. "$SELF/lib/polylane-runtime-events.sh"
. "$SELF/lib/polylane-runtime-reducer.sh"
. "$SELF/lib/polylane-runtime-retention.sh"
runtime_require_dir
command=${1:-}; shift || true
case $command in
  init) runtime_init "$@" ;;
  emit) runtime_emit "$@" ;;
  terminal-transaction) runtime_terminal_transaction "$@" ;;
  lookup-event) runtime_event_lookup "$@" ;;
  reduce) runtime_reduce "$@" ;;
  check-leases) runtime_check_leases "$@" ;;
  assert-invariant) runtime_assert_invariant "$@" ;;
  compact) runtime_compact "$@" ;;
  replay) runtime_replay "$@" ;;
  get) runtime_get "$@" ;;
  wake) runtime_wake "$@" ;;
  run-with-deadline) runtime_run_with_deadline "$@" ;;
  *) printf 'unknown runtime command: %s\n' "$command" >&2; exit 2 ;;
esac
```

Run:

```bash
chmod +x core/scripts/polylane-runtime.sh
bash -n core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-retention.sh
bash -n core/scripts/polylane-runtime.sh
```

Expected: both syntax checks exit 0 with no output.

**Contract — marker observation and runner liveness:**

Internal marker helpers emit changed marker fingerprints when `POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR` is set.
Because builders write marker files directly, the runner also fingerprints every observed
status/verdict file and emits exactly one progress event for each new valid fingerprint
before accepting it. A direct-file test writes the marker without calling the helper and
asserts the runner emits/wakes once; repeated polls emit nothing; a stale nonce emits
nothing. Legacy callers remain valid when runtime is unset.

On pane seed the runner creates `lane:<name>`/`integrator:<name>` leases. Its fast health
path computes `lane_progress_fingerprint` from pane output, piped log, git HEAD/status,
foreground command, and marker fingerprint. At agent start it records the foreground PID as
a quiet child with an explicit maximum from `POLYLANE_AGENT_MAX_QUIET` (default 300 seconds);
changed proof returns to the ordinary 90-second lease and never extends the hard quiet
deadline without new evidence.

- [ ] **Action 2.8 (2–5 minutes): Apply direct-marker observation and quiet-child patches**

Apply these exact hunks:

```diff
--- a/core/scripts/polylane-markers.sh
+++ b/core/scripts/polylane-markers.sh
@@
+marker_sha256() {
+  if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then sha256sum "$1" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'
+  else shasum -a 256 "$1" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; fi
+}
+
+emit_marker_progress() {
+  marker=$1 actor=$2 action=$3
+  [ -n "${POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR:-}" ] || return 0
+  runtime=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd -P)/polylane-runtime.sh
+  fingerprint=$(marker_sha256 "$marker")
+  "$runtime" --dir "$POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR" emit "$actor" marker_progress "$action" \
+    --fingerprint "$fingerprint"
+  "$runtime" --dir "$POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR" wake
+}
@@
 atomic_write_marker "$marker" "$content"
+emit_marker_progress "$marker" "$actor" "$action_id"
--- a/core/scripts/polylane-run.sh
+++ b/core/scripts/polylane-run.sh
@@
+file_sha256() {
+  if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then sha256sum "$1" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'
+  else shasum -a 256 "$1" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; fi
+}
+
+observe_marker_once() {
+  marker=${POLYLANE_TEST_MARKER:?}
+  run_id=${POLYLANE_TEST_RUN_ID:?}
+  [ -f "$marker" ] || return 0
+  grep -Eq "run=$run_id([[:space:]]|$)" "$marker" || return 0
+  fingerprint=$(file_sha256 "$marker")
+  seen=$POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR/marker-fingerprints
+  mkdir -p "$seen"
+  key=$(printf '%s' "$marker" | cksum | awk '{print $1}')
+  [ ! -f "$seen/$key" ] || [ "$(cat "$seen/$key")" != "$fingerprint" ] || return 0
+  runtime=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd -P)/polylane-runtime.sh
+  actor=lane:$(basename "$marker" | sed 's/^status-//')
+  "$runtime" --dir "$POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR" emit "$actor" marker_progress "$run_id" \
+    --fingerprint "$fingerprint"
+  printf '%s\n' "$fingerprint" >"$seen/$key.tmp.$$"
+  mv -f "$seen/$key.tmp.$$" "$seen/$key"
+  "$runtime" --dir "$POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR" wake
+}
+
+lane_progress_fingerprint() {
+  pane=$1 log=$2 worktree=$3 marker=$4
+  {
+    tmux capture-pane -p -t "$pane" -S -80 2>/dev/null || true
+    [ ! -f "$log" ] || tail -80 "$log"
+    git -C "$worktree" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || true
+    git -C "$worktree" status --porcelain=v1 2>/dev/null || true
+    tmux display-message -p -t "$pane" '#{pane_current_command}' 2>/dev/null || true
+    [ ! -f "$marker" ] || file_sha256 "$marker"
+  } | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}'
+}
@@
+if [ "${1:-}" = --observe-marker-once ]; then observe_marker_once; exit $?; fi
@@
 child_pid=$!
+quiet_seconds=${POLYLANE_AGENT_MAX_QUIET:-300}
+case $quiet_seconds in ''|*[!0-9]*) printf 'invalid POLYLANE_AGENT_MAX_QUIET\n' >&2; exit 2 ;; esac
+child_token=$(ps -o lstart= -p "$child_pid" | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')
+"$RUNTIME" --dir "$POLYLANE_RUNTIME_DIR" emit "$actor" quiet-child "$action_id" \
+  --deadline "$(( $(date +%s) + quiet_seconds ))" --child-pid "$child_pid" \
+  --child-start-token "$child_token"
```

Run:

```bash
bash -n core/scripts/polylane-markers.sh
bash -n core/scripts/polylane-run.sh
```

Expected: both commands exit 0 with no output.

```bash
bash core/tests/test-runtime-compaction.sh
```

### Task 3: Add a Typed Recovery Queue and Provider-Outage Work

**Files:**
- Create: `core/scripts/polylane-queue.sh`
- Create: `core/scripts/polylane-recovery.sh`
- Create: `core/scripts/polylane-worker.sh`
- Create: `core/scripts/polylane-dispatch.sh`
- Create: `core/scripts/lib/polylane-queue-reducer.sh`
- Create: `core/config/polylane-dispatch-v1.json`
- Create: `core/tests/test-recovery-queue.sh`
- Create: `core/tests/test-recovery-routing.sh`
- Create: `core/tests/test-provider-recovery.sh`
- Create: `core/tests/test-empty-queue-reconstruction.sh`
- Create: `core/tests/fixtures/provider/`
- Create: `core/tests/helpers/queue-contract.sh`
- Modify: `core/scripts/polylane-agent.sh`, `core/scripts/polylane-supervisor.sh`
- Modify: `codex/scripts/polylane-codex-agent.sh`,
  `claude-code/scripts/polylane-claude-agent.sh`

**Interfaces:**
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> enqueue <complete-item.json>`
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> ready <online|offline|probe> --limit <N> [--now <epoch> (test mode only)]` is read-only and prints
  candidates plus the source snapshot revision.
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> reserve-selected <selection.json> <guardian-pid> <guardian-generation>` atomically
  creates short starting reservations only if the expected revision, dependencies, and
  ownership constraints still match; a revision conflict exits 7 for rescheduling.
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> activate <id> <claim-token> <worker-pid> <worker-start-token> <expires-at>` changes
  a reservation to running only after the blocked worker validates its own identity.
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> renew-claim <id> <claim-token> <worker-pid> <worker-start-token> <proof-fingerprint> <expires-at>` renews only on a
  changed progress proof; heartbeat-only and duplicate fingerprints return 5.
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> complete <id> <claim-token> <worker-pid> <worker-start-token>`,
  `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> fail <id> <claim-token> <worker-pid> <worker-start-token> <reason> <strategy-hash> <retry-at>`
- `polylane-queue.sh --dir <runtime> ensure-progress <goal-state> [--now <epoch> (test mode only)]`
- `polylane-recovery.sh route <reason-class> <actor> <context.json>` enqueues the next
  untried recovery tactic.
- `polylane-recovery.sh run-offline <queue-item.json>` executes only allowlisted handlers.
- `polylane-dispatch.sh <queue-item.json> <claim-token>` maps every kind to a fixed installed
  helper/adapter entrypoint without evaluating queue text; a missing handler emits typed
  `handler_unavailable` recovery rather than leaving a reservation.
- `polylane-dispatch.sh audit <installed-skill-root>` resolves every allowed kind and calls
  each handler's `contract <kind>`; any missing/incompatible route exits 5.
- `polylane_adapter_probe <model> <deadline-seconds> <output.json>` is adapter-owned,
  bounded, non-mutating, and classifies available, transient/rate-limited, or user-action.
- `polylane_adapter_cycle_launcher <manifest.json> [<runner-argv>]` invokes the installed
  platform fail-closed launcher; `polylane_adapter_agent_job <prompt> <output> <model>
  <effort>` runs one normal noninteractive adapter job for research/council/suggestions.

**Contract — queue validation, ownership, claim-order, and dispatch tests:**

Allowed kinds are exactly:

```text
cycle digest skill_discovery research verification council certification
final_suggestions
checkpoint retry reflection_repair alternate_model recarve alternate_approach
local_test static_analysis diff_review merge_prepare prompt_prepare documentation
cleanup provider_probe state_reconcile session_recover
```

Reject arbitrary kinds and any `args` containing `command`, `shell`, or `script`. Enqueue
mixed online/offline jobs with dependencies and priorities; claim must choose the highest
priority due job whose dependencies are complete and whose mode matches provider state.
`provider_probe` uses mode `probe`: it remains eligible while the provider is unavailable,
whereas ordinary online work does not. Success emits provider-available and wakes online
selection at the same timestamp.

Every queue item follows:

```json
{
  "schema_version":1,
  "id":"queue-17",
  "kind":"local_test",
  "mode":"offline",
  "status":"ready",
  "priority":90,
  "goal_ref":"m2.1",
  "args":{"acceptance_id":"accept-m2.1"},
  "depends_on":[],
  "next_action_at":1000,
  "deadline_at":1300,
  "attempt":1,
  "strategy_hash":"abc",
  "ownership_globs":["src/api/**"],
  "resource_locks":["git:base"],
  "claim":null,
  "last_error":null
}
```

`ownership_globs` and `resource_locks` are required arrays on every item, including empty
arrays. Globs are sorted unique normalized project-relative patterns; reject absolute paths,
`..`, empty patterns, and patterns outside the frozen goal scope. Resource ids are sorted
unique values matching `[A-Za-z0-9._:-]+`. A selection and its starting reservation carry
`ownership_hash=sha256(canonical-json(ownership_globs,resource_locks))`. Reservation
recomputes that hash at the expected queue revision; any changed ownership or conflicting
running/reserved glob or resource rejects the whole selection before mutation.

A live claim contains worker, PID, guardian generation, claimed time, and `expires_at`.
Running work satisfies the liveness invariant only while all owner/generation/PID/deadline
checks pass. Kill the guardian during a claim and during an offline handler; reduction must
requeue the orphan with preserved checkpoint/evidence and a new attempt.

Test the real reservation handoff: the guardian reserves, starts
`polylane-worker.sh` blocked on its token, the worker calls `activate` with its actual PID
and start token, observes the acknowledged current snapshot, and only then runs the
allowlisted handler. Crash before spawn, before activate, and before acknowledgement; each
reservation must expire/requeue without handler side effects. The guardian PID is never
recorded as the running worker PID.

Run one healthy worker longer than its initial claim expiry while emitting changed proof;
`renew-claim` must extend the claim and prevent a duplicate. Repeat with heartbeats and a
duplicate proof only; the claim must expire, fence the old worker, and requeue exactly once.

Change queue revision and ownership globs after a scheduler selection;
`reserve-selected` must reject the stale selection without reserving a subset, then a fresh
selection succeeds. Install fixture helpers for every allowed kind and drive each through
selection, reservation, activation, dispatch, and completion. Cycle and research must
reach the selected normal adapter; other kinds map to
their named bundled helper; provider probe maps to the adapter probe hook. Remove each
fixture helper in turn and assert typed `handler_unavailable`, never a stuck reservation.
No queue field may choose an executable or append argv.
The fixture registry audit passes only when all kinds resolve and rejects a missing helper,
non-executable file, wrong contract version, and adapter hook absence.

**Contract — exact recovery-ladder tests:**

For one actor/reason, repeated `route` calls must enqueue in this order:

```text
checkpoint -> retry -> reflection_repair -> alternate_model -> recarve ->
alternate_approach -> provider_probe
```

Supply the same strategy hash twice and assert the duplicate is rejected and the next
tactic is selected. Retry counts have no terminal path. If approved models are exhausted,
the route moves to re-carving rather than failure.

After the final tactic, an incomplete non-provider failure enqueues `state_reconcile` with
an incremented `strategy_generation` and requires a newly derived strategy hash before the
ladder restarts. Provider failures continue bounded probes plus offline work. Simulate three
fully exhausted generations and assert no empty queue, repeated hash, or terminal exit.

**Contract — provider outage and empty-queue tests:**

Provider fixture progression is available → rate-limited → unavailable → available.
During outage, assert the controller phase is `RECOVERING`, offline jobs claim and complete,
provider probes have persisted `next_action_at`, and no `WAITING_FOR_USER` event appears.
An authentication fixture may request user input only after alternate approved models and
local repair routes are exhausted.

For an incomplete goal with no active actor or queue item, `ensure-progress` must enqueue
`state_reconcile` immediately with a goal reference. A completed goal or valid user-input
terminal must not reconstruct work.

Use fake Codex and Claude adapter probes to prove core dispatch contains no platform
command. The real Codex hook uses a bounded ephemeral read-only `codex exec` with
`approval_policy=never` and the approved probe model; it records only normalized status and
output hash. Probe timeout becomes provider-unavailable recovery, never a terminal state.

- [ ] **Action 3.1 (2–5 minutes): Add the complete queue/recovery contract driver**

Create `core/tests/helpers/queue-contract.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
scenario=${1:?scenario required}
REPO=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../.." && pwd -P)
RUNTIME=$REPO/core/scripts/polylane-runtime.sh
QUEUE=$REPO/core/scripts/polylane-queue.sh
RECOVERY=$REPO/core/scripts/polylane-recovery.sh
DISPATCH=$REPO/core/scripts/polylane-dispatch.sh
AUTONOMY_TMP=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-queue-test.XXXXXX")
export AUTONOMY_TMP POLYLANE_NOW_EPOCH=1000 POLYLANE_TEST_MODE=1
trap 'rm -rf "$AUTONOMY_TMP"' EXIT
. "$REPO/core/tests/helpers/autonomy-contract.sh"
project=$AUTONOMY_TMP/project
runtime=$project/.polylane/runtime
mkdir -p "$runtime" "$project"
"$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" init --loop-id pl1 --agent codex --project "$project"
adapter=$AUTONOMY_TMP/polylane-codex-agent.sh
cat >"$adapter" <<'ADAPTER'
polylane_adapter_cycle_launcher() { printf 'cycle:%s\n' "$1" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/adapter.called"; }
polylane_adapter_agent_job() { printf '{"ok":true}\n' >"$2"; }
polylane_adapter_probe() { "$POLYLANE_ADAPTER_PROBE" "$@"; }
polylane_adapter_dispatch_contract() { case $1 in cycle|research|provider_probe) printf '1\n';; *) return 5;; esac; }
ADAPTER
export POLYLANE_AGENT_ADAPTER=$adapter

item() {
  id=$1 kind=$2 mode=$3 priority=$4 goal=$5 globs=$6 resources=$7
  jq -n --arg id "$id" --arg kind "$kind" --arg mode "$mode" --argjson priority "$priority" \
    --arg goal "$goal" --argjson globs "$globs" --argjson resources "$resources" \
    '{schema_version:1,id:$id,kind:$kind,mode:$mode,status:"ready",priority:$priority,
      goal_ref:$goal,args:{acceptance_id:("accept-"+$goal)},depends_on:[],next_action_at:1000,
      deadline_at:1300,attempt:1,strategy_hash:("strategy-"+$id),ownership_globs:$globs,
      resource_locks:$resources,claim:null,last_error:null}'
}

enqueue() { item "$@" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/item.json"; "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/item.json"; }
current_queue() { "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" dump >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"; }

case $scenario in
  queue)
    item bad arbitrary offline 1 m1 '[]' '[]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/bad.json"
    assert_rc bad-kind 5 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/bad.json"
    item bad2 local_test offline 1 m1 '["../escape"]' '[]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/bad2.json"
    assert_rc bad-glob 5 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/bad2.json"
    item bad3 local_test offline 1 m1 '[]' '[]' |
      jq '.args.command="echo unsafe"' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/bad3.json"
    assert_rc command-field 5 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/bad3.json"
    enqueue q-low documentation offline 10 m1 '["docs/**"]' '[]'
    enqueue q-high local_test offline 90 m2 '["src/api/**"]' '["git:base"]'
    item q-child documentation offline 95 m3 '["src/api/internal/**"]' '[]' |
      jq '.depends_on=["q-high"]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/q-child.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/q-child.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 2 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/selection.json"
    assert_json highest-first '.items[0].id' q-high "$AUTONOMY_TMP/selection.json"
    assert_json dependency-blocked '[.items[].id]|index("q-child")==null' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/selection.json"
    assert_json ownership-hash '.items[0].ownership_hash|startswith("sha256:")' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/selection.json"
    guardian_token=$(ps -o lstart= -p $$ | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" emit guardian register queue-test --guardian-pid $$ \
      --guardian-start-token "$guardian_token" --guardian-generation 1 --guardian-role guardian
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    item q-lock-order documentation offline 1 m-lock '["lock-order/**"]' '[]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/q-lock-order.json"
    ( "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/q-lock-order.json" ) & queue_writer=$!
    ( "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" emit runtime proof lock-order --fingerprint event ) & event_writer=$!
    lock_deadline=$(( $(date +%s) + 3 ))
    while { kill -0 "$queue_writer" 2>/dev/null || kill -0 "$event_writer" 2>/dev/null; } &&
          [ "$(date +%s)" -lt "$lock_deadline" ]; do sleep 0.05; done
    if kill -0 "$queue_writer" 2>/dev/null || kill -0 "$event_writer" 2>/dev/null; then
      kill "$queue_writer" "$event_writer" 2>/dev/null || true; autonomy_fail queue-runtime-lock-deadlock
    fi
    wait "$queue_writer"; wait "$event_writer"
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 2 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/selection.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" reserve-selected "$AUTONOMY_TMP/selection.json" $$ 1
    current_queue
    token=$(jq -r '.items[]|select(.id=="q-high")|.claim.token' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json")
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" activate q-high "$token" $$ "$(ps -o lstart= -p $$ | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')" 1090
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" renew-claim q-high "$token" $$ "$guardian_token" proof-a 1140
    assert_rc duplicate-renewal 5 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" renew-claim q-high "$token" $$ "$guardian_token" proof-a 1190
    assert_rc wrong-worker 6 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" complete q-high "$token" 999999 missing
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" complete q-high "$token" $$ "$guardian_token"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 2 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/after-dependency.json"
    assert_json dependency-released '.items[0].id' q-child "$AUTONOMY_TMP/after-dependency.json"
    enqueue nested-running local_test offline 110 m4 '["src/**"]' '["git:base"]'
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 4 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested.json"
    jq '{schema_version,snapshot_revision,queue_revision,mode,selected_at,
      items:[.items[]|select(.id=="nested-running")],aggregate_ownership_proof,limit}' \
      "$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested.json" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.json"
    # Recompute the aggregate after narrowing the fixture selection.
    nested_proof=$(jq -Sc '[.items[].ownership_hash]|sort' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.json" | shasum -a 256 | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
    jq --arg proof "$nested_proof" '.aggregate_ownership_proof=$proof' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.json" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.next"
    mv "$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.next" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" reserve-selected "$AUTONOMY_TMP/nested-only.json" $$ 1
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 10 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/conflicted.json"
    assert_json nested-glob-excluded '[.items[].id]|index("q-child")==null' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/conflicted.json"
    enqueue same-resource documentation offline 99 m5 '["other/**"]' '["git:base"]'
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 10 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/resource-conflicted.json"
    assert_json resource-excluded '[.items[].id]|index("same-resource")==null' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/resource-conflicted.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" reclaim-expired --now 1016
    current_queue
    assert_json expired-requeued '.items[]|select(.id=="nested-running")|.status' scheduled "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    assert_json expired-attempt '.items[]|select(.id=="nested-running")|.attempt' 2 "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    jq '.queue_revision -= 1' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/resource-conflicted.json" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/stale.json"
    before=$("$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" dump | jq -Sc .)
    assert_rc stale-atomic 7 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" reserve-selected "$AUTONOMY_TMP/stale.json" $$ 1
    assert_eq stale-no-subset "$before" "$("$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" dump | jq -Sc .)"
    current_queue
    assert_json complete '.items[]|select(.id=="q-high")|.status' complete "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    ;;
  routing)
    context=$AUTONOMY_TMP/context.json
    printf '%s\n' '{"goal_ref":"m1","strategy_hash":"s0","ownership_globs":[],"resource_locks":[]}' >"$context"
    expected='checkpoint retry reflection_repair alternate_model recarve alternate_approach provider_probe'
    actual=''
    for ignored in $(seq 1 7); do
      "$RECOVERY" route transient controller "$context"
      current_queue
      kind=$(jq -r '.items|sort_by(.created_sequence)|last.kind' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json")
      actual="$actual${actual:+ }$kind"
    done
    assert_eq ladder "$expected" "$actual"
    assert_rc duplicate-strategy 5 "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/item.json"
    for ignored in 1 2 3; do "$RECOVERY" route exhausted controller "$context"; done
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ensure-progress "$AUTONOMY_TMP/context.json" --now 1000
    current_queue
    assert_json nonempty '.items|map(select(.status=="ready" or .status=="scheduled"))|length>0' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    ;;
  provider)
    mkdir -p "$AUTONOMY_TMP/adapter"
    cat >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/adapter/probe.sh" <<'PROBE'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
printf '%s\n' "${POLYLANE_PROBE_RESULT:-available}" >"$3"
PROBE
    chmod +x "$AUTONOMY_TMP/adapter/probe.sh"
    export POLYLANE_ADAPTER_PROBE="$AUTONOMY_TMP/adapter/probe.sh"
    enqueue offline local_test offline 80 m1 '[]' '[]'
    enqueue probe provider_probe probe 100 m1 '[]' '["provider:probe"]'
    export POLYLANE_PROBE_RESULT=unavailable
    "$DISPATCH" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/item.json" test-token || true
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 4 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/offline.json"
    assert_json offline-drains '.items|length>0' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/offline.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready online --limit 4 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/online.json"
    assert_json online-deferred '.items|length' 0 "$AUTONOMY_TMP/online.json"
    assert_eq no-waiting 0 "$(find "$runtime/events" -name '*.json' -exec jq -r 'select(.type=="terminal")|.type' {} + | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
    ;;
  reconstruct)
    printf '%s\n' '{"loop_id":"pl1","complete":false,"next_goal_ref":"m9"}' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/goal.json"
    item blocked local_test offline 40 m-blocked '[]' '[]' | jq '.depends_on=["missing"]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/blocked.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/blocked.json"
    item cycle-a local_test offline 30 m-cycle '[]' '[]' | jq '.depends_on=["cycle-b"]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/cycle-a.json"
    item cycle-b local_test offline 30 m-cycle '[]' '[]' | jq '.depends_on=["cycle-a"]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/cycle-b.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/cycle-a.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" enqueue "$AUTONOMY_TMP/cycle-b.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ensure-progress "$AUTONOMY_TMP/goal.json" --now 1000
    current_queue
    assert_json reconcile '.items[]|select(.kind=="state_reconcile")|.goal_ref' m9 "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    assert_json reconcile-deadline '.items[]|select(.kind=="state_reconcile")|.deadline_at' 1300 "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    before=$(jq -Sc . "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json")
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ensure-progress "$AUTONOMY_TMP/goal.json" --now 1000
    current_queue
    assert_eq idempotent "$before" "$(jq -Sc . "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json")"
    guardian_token=$(ps -o lstart= -p $$ | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" emit guardian register reconstruct --guardian-pid $$ \
      --guardian-start-token "$guardian_token" --guardian-generation 1 --guardian-role guardian
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ready offline --limit 1 --now 1000 >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/reconcile-selection.json"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" reserve-selected "$AUTONOMY_TMP/reconcile-selection.json" $$ 1
    current_queue; rid=$(jq -r '.items[]|select(.kind=="state_reconcile")|.id' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json")
    token=$(jq -r --arg id "$rid" '.items[]|select(.id==$id)|.claim.token' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json")
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" activate "$rid" "$token" $$ "$guardian_token" 1090
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" complete "$rid" "$token" $$ "$guardian_token"
    "$QUEUE" --dir "$runtime" ensure-progress "$AUTONOMY_TMP/goal.json" --now 1000
    current_queue
    assert_json next-generation '[.items[]|select(.kind=="state_reconcile")|.args.reconcile_generation]|max' 2 "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    assert_json unique-strategies '([.items[]|select(.kind=="state_reconcile")|.strategy_hash]) as $x |
      ($x|length)==($x|unique|length)' true "$AUTONOMY_TMP/queue.json"
    ;;
  *) printf 'unknown queue scenario: %s\n' "$scenario" >&2; exit 2 ;;
esac

finish_autonomy_test "queue-$scenario"
```

- [ ] **Action 3.3 (2–5 minutes): Run the queue/recovery tests and capture RED**

```bash

bash core/tests/test-recovery-routing.sh
bash core/tests/test-provider-recovery.sh

```

Expected RED: all four exit nonzero because the queue, recovery, worker, and dispatch
interfaces do not exist; the first test reports `polylane-queue.sh` missing.

**Contract — atomic typed queue operations and fixed dispatch:**

Queue mutations are immutable runtime events reduced into the same atomic generation as
state and leases. Item id is immutable and lexical id is the final claim tie-break.
Canonical ownership arrays and their hash are immutable for an attempt. Selection excludes
any item whose glob overlaps a reserved/running glob or whose resource id matches a
reserved/running resource; `cycle` derives its combined glob set from the validated
manifest and reserves `git:base` only for promotion, while reducer, cleanup, and publication
jobs use their named runtime/package locks.
`ready` is a pure query used by the scheduler. The guardian is the only reservation owner:
it calls `reserve-selected` with the scheduler's revision-tagged selection, reruns selection
on conflict, launches the fixed `polylane-worker.sh` wrapper with a registered kind/args file
and opaque token, and lets that child activate its actual PID before work.
Starting reservations have a short deadline and do not satisfy the liveness invariant by
themselves unless a live guardian lease covers the spawn action. Every reservation,
activation, proof-bound renewal, completion, failure, orphan reclaim, and schedule emits a
runtime proof event reduced into the atomic snapshot.

Offline handlers accept ids, file paths, and registered acceptance/lint commands from the
goal state; they never evaluate queue-provided shell text. `diff_review` writes a diffstat,
`merge_prepare` checks worktree/branch readiness, `prompt_prepare` runs prompt/skill lint,
`documentation` audits required docs, `cleanup` removes only Polylane-owned scratch,
`local_test` and `static_analysis` call pre-registered frozen checks.

`polylane-dispatch.sh` owns an exhaustive kind-to-handler `case`. It resolves executables
only relative to installed `BIN`; exactly one `cycle` item invokes the selected platform's
fail-closed cycle launcher for a nonce-tagged manifest, and that runner internally owns all
builder panes plus its dependent integrator. Research enters the selected adapter's managed
agent-job hook. Digest, skill discovery,
verification, council, certification, final suggestions, and recovery kinds call their
named bundled helpers with validated ids/files only. `provider_probe` calls
`polylane_adapter_probe` even while normal online work is deferred. A helper introduced by
a later plan task may be absent only before its kind is enqueued; if called early, the typed
missing-handler recovery keeps the queue live.
Every production handler exposes `contract <kind>` returning dispatch schema version 1;
the final installed-package audit permits no missing-handler route.

**Contract — visible bounded provider backoff:**

Use backoff `5, 10, 20, 40, 80, 160, 300` seconds capped at 300. Before any wait, persist a
`provider_probe` item with mode `probe` and `next_action_at`, emit an event, and drain ready
offline work. There is no unreported `sleep`; the guardian waits on the wake channel with
five seconds as a maximum fallback tick.

- [ ] **Action 3.5 (2–5 minutes): Add queue payloads to runtime reduction**

Create `core/scripts/lib/polylane-queue-reducer.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
polylane_queue_apply_event() {
  local state event output
  state=$1 event=$2 output=$3
  jq --slurpfile e "$event" '
    ($e[0]) as $x | ($x.payload) as $p |
    .queue=(.queue // {revision:0,provider_state:"available",items:[]}) |
    if $x.type=="queue_enqueue" then
      if any(.queue.items[]; .id==$p.id) then .
      else .queue.items += [$p + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] | .queue.revision += 1 end
    elif $x.type=="queue_reserve" then
      if .queue.revision!=$p.expected_queue_revision then
        .queue.last_reservation_conflict={expected:$p.expected_queue_revision,actual:.queue.revision,
          event_sequence:$x.sequence}
      else reduce $p.reservations[] as $r (.;
        .queue.items |= map(if .id==$r.id then
          .status="starting" | .claim=$r.claim
        else . end)) | .queue.revision += 1 end
    elif $x.type=="queue_activate" then
      .queue.items |= map(if .id==$p.id and .status=="starting" and .claim.token==$p.token and
        .claim.guardian_pid==$p.guardian_pid and
        .claim.guardian_start_token==$p.guardian_start_token and
        .claim.guardian_generation==$p.guardian_generation then
        .status="running" | .claim.worker_pid=$p.worker_pid |
        .claim.worker_start_token=$p.worker_start_token | .claim.expires_at=$p.expires_at
      else . end) | .queue.revision += 1
    elif $x.type=="queue_renew" then
      .queue.items |= map(if .id==$p.id and .status=="running" and .claim.token==$p.token and
        .claim.worker_pid==$p.worker_pid and .claim.worker_start_token==$p.worker_start_token then
        .claim.proof_fingerprint=$p.proof_fingerprint | .claim.expires_at=$p.expires_at
      else . end) | .queue.revision += 1
    elif $x.type=="queue_complete" then
      .queue.items |= map(if .id==$p.id and .status=="running" and .claim.token==$p.token and
        .claim.worker_pid==$p.worker_pid and .claim.worker_start_token==$p.worker_start_token
        then .status="complete" else . end) |
      .queue.revision += 1
    elif $x.type=="queue_fail" then
      .queue.items |= map(if .id==$p.id and .status=="running" and .claim.token==$p.token and
        .claim.worker_pid==$p.worker_pid and .claim.worker_start_token==$p.worker_start_token and
        $p.strategy_hash!=.strategy_hash then
        .status="scheduled" | .claim=null | .last_error=$p.reason |
        .strategy_hash=$p.strategy_hash | .next_action_at=$p.retry_at | .attempt += 1
      else . end) | .queue.revision += 1
    elif $x.type=="queue_reclaim" then
      reduce $p.claims[] as $r (.;
        .queue.items |= map(if .id==$r.id and .claim.token==$r.token then
          .status="scheduled" | .claim=null | .last_error=$r.reason |
          .next_action_at=$p.requeue_at | .attempt += 1
        else . end)) | .queue.revision += 1
    elif $x.type=="provider_state" then .queue.provider_state=$p.state | .queue.revision += 1
    else . end' "$state" >"$output"
}
```

The generic `--payload-file` parser, payload hash, event-chain validation, and mode-0400
publication are already complete in Action 2.5. Apply only these reducer hunks; typed
dispatch occurs after the generic reducer has verified `payload_sha256` and the event chain:

```diff
--- a/core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-reducer.sh
+++ b/core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-reducer.sh
@@
-      leases:{},queue_summary:{running:0,scheduled:0},recovery:null,max_unexplained_idle_gap:0}' \
+      leases:{},queue:{revision:0,provider_state:"available",items:[]},
+      queue_summary:{running:0,scheduled:0},recovery:null,max_unexplained_idle_gap:0}' \
@@
 runtime_apply_event() {
@@
+  case $(jq -r '.type' "$event") in
+    queue_*|provider_state)
+      . "$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd -P)/polylane-queue-reducer.sh"
+      polylane_queue_apply_event "$state" "$event" "$output.queue"
+      jq --slurpfile e "$event" '($e[0]) as $x |
+        .last_event_sequence=$x.sequence | .last_event_hash=$x.event_hash' \
+        "$output.queue" >"$output"
+      rm -f "$output.queue"
+      return
+      ;;
+  esac
```
