- Create: `core/tests/helpers/controller-contract.sh`
- Modify: `core/scripts/polylane-runtime.sh`, `core/scripts/polylane-queue.sh`,
  `core/scripts/polylane-run.sh`, `core/scripts/polylane-supervisor.sh`
- Modify: `codex/SKILL.md`, `codex/references/codex-runtime.md`

**Interfaces:**
- `polylane-guardian.sh --runtime <dir> --state <controller-state> -- <controller-argv>`
  runs without a terminal restart cap.
- `polylane-guardian.sh check-once --runtime <dir> --state <controller-state>
  [--now <epoch> (test mode only)]` executes the same reduce/audit/select/reserve/dispatch/recover tick used
  by production.
- `polylane-keeper.sh start|run --project <root> --loop-id <id> --launch <json>` owns one
  detached external generation, recreates a lost owned tmux session, and is mutually
  supervised by the tmux sentinel.
- `polylane-terminal.sh complete <controller-state> <perfection-state>
  <final-suggestions> <final-delivery>` validates perfection, the final informational set,
  and its pending durable delivery before writing a `COMPLETE` transition request for the
  reducer.
- `polylane-terminal.sh needs-input <controller-state> <needs-input.json>
  <condition-evidence.json>` validates an allowed reason and a finalized, typed production
  observation of that condition before writing a `WAITING_FOR_USER` transaction.
- `polylane-terminal.sh answer <controller-state> <needs-input.json> <answer.json>` validates
  request/loop identity, archives the resolved exchange, and requests the same loop return
  to `WORKING` with an immediate `state_reconcile` job.
- `polylane-codex-loop.sh <project-root> [--resume]` starts or resumes the stable session.
- `polylane-codex-controller.sh <project-root> <loop-id>` runs the stdin-based Codex
  controller turn loop. Codex produces only schema-validated proposals; the core turn
  driver owns every queue/stage/git/result/cleanup mutation.
- `polylane-controller-turn.sh prepare|apply|step ...` creates immutable hashed turn
  evidence, atomically applies a typed proposal at expected runtime/queue revisions, and
  reconciles one durable cycle effect at a time.
- `polylane-command-policy.sh compile-controller <project-root> <proposal.json>
  <compiled.json>` converts model-proposed argv to a mechanically safe, checkout-relative,
  executable-identity-bound policy. `verify-spec <project-root> <command-spec.json>`
  revalidates the executable path, inode, mode, and hash immediately before and after use.
- `polylane-finalizer.sh run --runtime <dir> --shutdown-token <token>` is a detached,
  non-agent terminal teardown peer that mutually supervises the keeper until owned tmux and
  actor resources are gone, then records `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE` and exits.
- `polylane-finalizer.sh emergency-cleanup --project <root> --loop-id <id>
  --generation <n>` is enabled only by `POLYLANE_TEST_MODE=1`; it validates every ownership
  token, fences owned actors/keepers, disarms the scoped hook, and removes only the tagged
  test session.
- `polylane-process.sh adopt-or-fence <runtime> <actor> <expected-token>` adopts exactly one
  live PID/start-token with a valid lease, or TERM/KILLs its tmux pane and captured
  descendant tree before a replacement generation may start.
- `polylane-process.sh assert-current <runtime> <actor> <generation-token>` gates every
  shared-state, queue, result, cleanup, and base-promotion mutation.

**Contract — terminal-state and shutdown-stage tests:**

Attempt terminal transitions for `FAILED`, `HALTED`, `BUDGET`, `TIMEOUT`, `NO_GO`,
`RETRY_EXHAUSTED`, `COMPLETE`, and `WAITING_FOR_USER`. All but the latter two must exit 5.
`COMPLETE` without two clean certifications, a valid final 30-suggestion file, and a
matching pending delivery record must fail.
`WAITING_FOR_USER` without both a valid input file and genuine allowlisted condition
evidence must fail. A mode-0400 JSON file by itself is never authority for either terminal
transition.

Allowed input reasons are exactly:

```text
credential billing account_permission core_strategy_decision irreversible_external_action external_resource_conflict
```

`needs-input.json` requires `loop_id`, `reason_code`, one concrete question, a recommended
answer, impact, `request_id`, and `created_at`. Transient network/provider/rate-limit
reasons are invalid. A valid answer requires the same `request_id`/`loop_id`, nonempty
answer text, and timestamp. Test answer application clears the active request, archives it
under `docs/polylane/input-history/`, renews keeper/guardian ownership, resumes the same
session without discovery, and never changes `loop_id`.

Every new COMPLETE/WAITING transaction is signed with HMAC-SHA256 over its canonical
unsigned bytes and binds all four immutable hashes: body, sealed request, transaction, and
the current terminal-reducer actor record. The actor record binds PID, process start token,
strictly increasing generation, generation token, and registration time; the reducer
requires that exact generation to remain current and live. HMAC verification uses
constant-time comparison. Reducer replay is allowed only when the already-applied record
has the same transaction id *and exact transaction hash*; replay never re-authorizes a
different body and is the only path that may succeed after the producing actor exits.
Tests reject an unsigned transaction, a forged tag, one-byte mutations of every bound
artifact, a stale actor generation, a dead actor PID, a reused id with different bytes, and
an otherwise valid old transaction at a newer runtime revision.

WAITING condition evidence is a sealed mode-0400 artifact that binds request id, reason,
and the id/hash of one finalized `input_condition_observed` event emitted by its allowlisted
production source. The reducer resolves and rehashes that event and checks the configured
reason/source/type mapping; a caller-authored assertion, generic event, stale observation,
wrong request, or mismatched reason fails. COMPLETE similarly binds the exact sealed
final-suggestion artifact, not merely a sha256-shaped string: canonical artifact bytes must
equal `convergence.finalization.suggestions`, contain ranks 1..30 exactly once, and hash to
all transaction, finalization, and delivery references.

Apply an answer as one reducer-owned idempotent transaction keyed by request id and expected
runtime revision. Its published snapshot simultaneously records input history, removes the
active request, requests WORKING, renews the appropriate keeper/guardian ownership, and
enqueues immediate `state_reconcile`; docs files are repairable projections. Inject crashes
before/after event, snapshot, each projection, and wake. Replay must never yield WAITING with
no request or WORKING with a stale active request, and duplicate answers append no history.

Both terminal helpers must emit a validated transition event carrying the expected current
revision; they may not edit controller or operational state directly. Inject a reducer
crash after publishing the runtime generation but before refreshing
`docs/polylane/controller-state.json`; resume must rebuild the projection from the current
snapshot and preserve exactly one terminal transition.

`COMPLETE` starts an idempotent shutdown stage machine:

```text
TERMINAL_PUBLISHED -> CLAIMS_DRAINED -> ACTORS_FENCED -> HOOK_DISARMED ->
FINALIZER_ARMED -> SESSION_CLOSED -> TEARDOWN_COMPLETE
```

`WAITING_FOR_USER` never enters it. Ordinary input reasons retain keeper/guardian/session
plus a scheduled input observer. `external_resource_conflict` retains only the detached
keeper/input observer because the exact owned tmux session cannot exist; watch output is
suppressed until ownership is restored. After the user frees the name and answers/re-enters,
the keeper recreates sentinel/guardian under the same loop id before returning to WORKING.
Inject a crash before/after every shutdown stage, kill keeper once and finalizer
once, and assert the surviving peer resumes. After COMPLETE, no new claim/controller may
start, the pane-death hook is disarmed, all actor tokens are fenced, the owned session stays
absent, and no keeper/sentinel/guardian/finalizer PID remains. Pending final chat delivery is
durable and does not require the background runtime to stay alive.

The finalizer is double-forked and reparented before the keeper records it, and the test
asserts its PPID is not the keeper. Before any owned session kill, it seals one inventory
containing the keeper owner, sentinel heartbeat, every guardian/controller/worker actor,
every lane identity, and every tmux pane root. Each live identity is fenced only by the
shared exact sequence `STOP -> descendant snapshot until stable -> CONT all -> TERM ->
wait -> KILL survivors -> wait -> verify absent`. The keeper remains live as the recovery
peer through `SESSION_CLOSED`; the finalizer then fences it with that same sequence and
publishes `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE`. The finalizer records its own handoff identity, publishes,
exits normally, and foreground independently verifies that exact PID/start-token absent—no
process ever signals itself. Emergency cleanup uses the identical sealed inventory and
sequence. A direct `tmux kill-session` is forbidden until every inventoried pane root and
descendant is absent.

**Contract — guardian, keeper, sentinel, fencing, and lane-surface fault tests:**

Fixtures cover controller clean exit while incomplete, exit 75 recovery, killed PID,
expired lease with live heartbeat, duplicate output fingerprints, and guardian restart.
Assert each incomplete case persists diagnostics, claims or reconstructs a recovery item,
and starts a new controller generation. Also freeze the guardian heartbeat while its PID
remains alive; the sentinel must replace it. Kill the sentinel foreground process; the
tmux pane-death hook must respawn it. No restart count terminates the loop.

Start two simultaneous `--resume` launchers and assert an atomic keeper-generation claim
allows exactly one keeper/guardian/controller. Kill the detached keeper and assert the tmux
sentinel replaces it. Kill the entire owned tmux session and assert the keeper recreates and
re-arms the exact same session name without user action. Before a new loop id is persisted,
an unrelated collision makes the loop-id generator choose another unique id. After a run is
already persisted, create an unrelated same-name session during the recreation race; keeper
must leave it untouched, record `session_recover`, keep visible diagnostics/probes scheduled,
and preserve the public name. If the collision remains after internal rechecks, it emits a
concrete `external_resource_conflict` request identifying the unrelated session and asking
the user to rename or remove that exact conflicting tmux session. The persisted `loop_id`
never changes. It must never silently use a suffixed name.

Kill a guardian while its controller child and one offline worker remain alive. A new
guardian may adopt a child only when PID, process start token, actor generation token, claim,
and progress lease all match; otherwise it first fences the recorded tmux pane plus the
recursively captured descendant PID tree, waits for their exit, and only then starts a new
generation. A stale process that survives TERM/KILL must be unable to mutate anything:
all helpers reject its old generation token, lane worktrees are quarantined, and a new
attempt gets a distinct worktree. Assert exactly one current controller and one current
worker after guardian, sentinel, and session recovery, with no two valid mutation tokens.

Call runtime emit/reduce, queue reserve/activate/complete/fail, cycle-result write,
cleanup, marker acceptance, and base-promotion entrypoints with a stale token and assert
each exits 6 before mutation. Repeat with the current token and assert the intended single
mutation. This focused matrix prevents a process helper that is correct while an older
direct mutation path remains unfenced.

During a real mock-agent active cycle, delete one builder pane and then the entire `lanes`
window. The supervisor/guardian persists `lane_pane_lost` or `lanes_window_lost`, recreates
the missing surface with the same cycle `run_id`, and relaunches only incomplete lanes
through the ordinary selected adapter. Current valid DONE markers remain accepted, stale
markers are ignored, and exactly one current attempt exists per lane after recovery.

Abort an incomplete test run and invoke `emergency-cleanup`; assert keeper cannot recreate
the session and no owned PID remains. Wrong project/loop/generation must exit before any
signal or tmux mutation, and the helper must contain no broad `pkill` or unscoped session
kill.

**Contract — resume, cycle transaction, and safe controller argv tests:**

Use a mock Codex controller that completes one queue item, exits, then on resume reads the
persisted cycle/action and completes another. Assert no discovery/interview restart,
controller generation increments, `loop_id` and tmux session remain stable, and the exact
watch line prints once for each launcher invocation.

Drive the authoritative cycle stage machine through `PLANNED`, `LANES_RUNNING`,
`INTEGRATING`, `PROMOTION_PREPARED`, `PROMOTED`, `RESULT_RECORDED`, `CLEANUP`, and
`NEXT_ENQUEUED`. Inject a crash before/after every transition, including after the git base
ref moves but before state, after result but before cleanup, and after cleanup but before
next enqueue. Resume must reconcile git refs plus nonce-tagged result, perform each effect
at most once, and always reach the next runnable action.

Start two promoters with the same expected base and two different integration commits;
exactly one compare-and-swap may move the base. A stale or externally advanced base must
leave the ref untouched and enqueue reintegration/reconciliation with both commit ids.

The fail-closed loop test rejects a controller state with `agent:claude`, a missing loop id,
or project mismatch before tmux creation.

Test controller override parsing with an absolute executable whose path contains spaces and
shell metacharacters; it must arrive as one argv element and the metacharacters must never
execute. `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD` accepts exactly one canonical absolute executable path.
For multiple arguments, `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_ARGV_FILE` is a canonical absolute path to a
mode-0600 JSON array of nonempty strings whose first element is an absolute executable;
the variables are mutually exclusive. Reject relative paths, non-arrays, non-string/null
members, unsafe permissions, and a changed file hash between validation and launch. Persist
the validated argv array and hash in `controller-launch.json`, then restart from that array
without `eval` or reparsing shell text.

```json
{"schema_version":1,"allowed":["account_permission","billing","core_strategy_decision","credential","external_resource_conflict","irreversible_external_action"]}
```

Create `core/config/polylane-shutdown-stages-v1.json`:

```json
{"schema_version":1,"stages":["TERMINAL_PUBLISHED","CLAIMS_DRAINED","ACTORS_FENCED","HOOK_DISARMED","FINALIZER_ARMED","SESSION_CLOSED","TEARDOWN_COMPLETE"]}
```

Create `core/config/polylane-controller-proposal-v1.json`:

```json
{
  "$schema":"https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "type":"object",
  "additionalProperties":false,
  "required":["schema_version","turn_id","input_hash","action","goal_ref","rationale","builders","integrator","certification"],
  "properties":{
    "schema_version":{"const":1},
    "turn_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[A-Za-z0-9._:-]{1,160}$"},
    "input_hash":{"type":"string","pattern":"^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$"},
    "action":{"const":"START_CYCLE"},
    "goal_ref":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":160},
    "rationale":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":4000},
    "builders":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"maxItems":6,"items":{"$ref":"#/$defs/builder"}},
    "integrator":{"$ref":"#/$defs/integrator"},
    "certification":{"$ref":"#/$defs/certification"}
  },
  "$defs":{"builder":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":false,
    "required":["id","brief","activities","ownership_globs","verification_argv"],
    "properties":{
      "id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$"},
      "brief":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":4000},
      "activities":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":1000},"uniqueItems":true},
      "ownership_globs":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","minLength":1},"uniqueItems":true},
      "verification_argv":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv"}
    }},
    "integrator":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":false,
      "required":["id","brief","activities","ownership_globs","verification_argv"],
      "properties":{
        "id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$"},
        "brief":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":4000},
        "activities":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","minLength":1,"maxLength":1000},"uniqueItems":true},
        "ownership_globs":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"items":{"type":"string","minLength":1},"uniqueItems":true},
        "verification_argv":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv"}
      }
    },
    "argv":{"type":"array","minItems":1,"prefixItems":[{"type":"string","pattern":"^/"}],
      "items":{"type":"string","minLength":1}},
    "argv_spec":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":false,"required":["argv"],
      "properties":{"argv":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv"}}},
    "certification":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":false,
      "required":["deadline_seconds","checks","fresh_checkout"],
      "properties":{"deadline_seconds":{"type":"integer","minimum":1,"maximum":86400},
        "checks":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":false,
          "required":["accessibility","broken_flows","documentation","functional","performance","regression","security","stubs"],
          "properties":{"accessibility":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},"broken_flows":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},
            "documentation":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},"functional":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},
            "performance":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},"regression":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},
            "security":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},"stubs":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"}}},
        "fresh_checkout":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":false,"required":["boot","build","install"],
          "properties":{"boot":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},"build":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"},
            "install":{"$ref":"#/$defs/argv_spec"}}}}}
  }
}
```

Run:

```bash

jq -e '.stages|length==7 and .[-1]=="TEARDOWN_COMPLETE"' core/config/polylane-shutdown-stages-v1.json
jq -e '.properties.action.const=="START_CYCLE" and
  (.["$defs"].builder.properties|has("selected_skills")|not) and
  .["$defs"].builder.properties.activities.minItems==1 and
  (.["$defs"].integrator.properties|has("selected_skills")|not) and
  .["$defs"].builder.properties.verification_argv.["$ref"]=="#/$defs/argv" and
  .["$defs"].integrator.properties.verification_argv.["$ref"]=="#/$defs/argv" and
  (.["$defs"].certification.properties.checks.required|length)==8 and
  .["$defs"].certification.properties.fresh_checkout.required==["boot","build","install"]' \
  core/config/polylane-controller-proposal-v1.json
```

Expected: both commands print `true` and exit 0.

- [ ] **Action 4.1a (2–5 minutes): Compile all proposed commands through a hostile-input policy**

Create `core/scripts/polylane-command-policy.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
umask 077
sha_file() {
  if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then sha256sum "$1" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'
  else shasum -a 256 "$1" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; fi
}
canonical_project() { [ -d "$1" ] && [ ! -L "$1" ] && (cd "$1" && pwd -P); }
reject_user_home_path() {
  local candidate home
  candidate=$1; home=$(cd ~ && pwd -P) || return 5
  case $candidate in "$home"|"$home"/*) return 5;; *) return 0;; esac
}
canonical_launch() {
  local path directory
  path=$1; [ "${path#/}" != "$path" ] && [ -e "$path" ] || return 5
  directory=$(cd "$(dirname "$path")" && pwd -P) || return 5
  path=$directory/$(basename "$path"); [ -f "$path" ] && [ -x "$path" ] || return 5
  printf '%s\n' "$path"
}
physical_executable() {
  local launch physical directory
  launch=$(canonical_launch "$1") || return 5
  if [ -L "$launch" ]; then command -v realpath >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 5; physical=$(realpath "$launch")
  else physical=$launch; fi
  directory=$(cd "$(dirname "$physical")" && pwd -P) || return 5
  physical=$directory/$(basename "$physical")
  [ -f "$physical" ] && [ ! -L "$physical" ] && [ -x "$physical" ] || return 5
  printf '%s\n' "$physical"
}
identity_json() {
  local launch physical device inode mode
  launch=$(canonical_launch "$1") || return 5; physical=$(physical_executable "$launch") || return 5
  device=$(stat -f %d "$physical" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %d "$physical")
  inode=$(stat -f %i "$physical" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %i "$physical")
  mode=$(stat -f %Lp "$physical" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %a "$physical")
  jq -n --arg launch_path "$launch" --arg physical_path "$physical" --arg hash "$(sha_file "$physical")" \
    --arg device "$device" --arg inode "$inode" --arg mode "$mode" \
    '{launch_path:$launch_path,physical_path:$physical_path,sha256:$hash,
      device:$device,inode:$inode,mode:$mode}'
}
interpreter_json() {
  local launch physical first shebang interpreter runtime ignored directory
  launch=$1; physical=$(physical_executable "$launch") || return 5
  IFS= read -r first <"$physical" || first=''
  case $first in '#!'*) shebang=${first#\#!};; *) printf 'null\n'; return 0;; esac
  IFS=' ' read -r interpreter runtime ignored <<EOF
$shebang
EOF
  [ -n "$interpreter" ] || return 5
  if [ "$(basename "$interpreter")" = env ]; then
    case $runtime in ''|*/*|*[!A-Za-z0-9._+-]*) return 5;; esac
    interpreter=''
    for directory in "$(cd "$(dirname "$launch")" && pwd -P)" \
      "$(cd "$(dirname "$physical")" && pwd -P)" /opt/homebrew/bin /usr/local/bin /usr/bin /bin; do
      [ -x "$directory/$runtime" ] || continue; interpreter=$directory/$runtime; break
    done
    [ -n "$interpreter" ] || return 5
  fi
  interpreter=$(canonical_launch "$interpreter") || return 5
  case $(basename "$interpreter") in sh|bash|dash|zsh|fish|csh|tcsh) return 5;; esac
  identity_json "$interpreter"
}
path_entries_json() {
  local project launch physical interpreter allow_local launch_dir physical_dir temporary
  project=$1 launch=$2 physical=$3 interpreter=$4 allow_local=$5
  launch_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "$launch")" && pwd -P)
  physical_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "$physical")" && pwd -P)
  temporary=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-path-entries.XXXXXX")
  jq -n --arg launch "$launch_dir" --arg physical "$physical_dir" --argjson interpreter "$interpreter" \
    '[{kind:"host",path:$launch},{kind:"host",path:$physical},
      {kind:"host",path:"/usr/bin"},{kind:"host",path:"/bin"}] +
      (if $interpreter==null then [] else
        [{kind:"host",path:($interpreter.launch_path|split("/")|.[0:-1]|join("/")|if .=="" then "/" else . end)},
         {kind:"host",path:($interpreter.physical_path|split("/")|.[0:-1]|join("/")|if .=="" then "/" else . end)}] end) |
      unique_by(.kind+":"+.path)' >"$temporary"
  if [ "$allow_local" = 1 ] && [ -f "$project/package.json" ]; then
    jq '. + [{kind:"checkout_relative",path:"node_modules/.bin",required_if_present:true}]' \
      "$temporary"
  else cat "$temporary"; fi
  rm -f "$temporary"
}
validate_tool_verb() {
  local project tool argv verb script
  project=$1 tool=$2 argv=$3
  case $tool in
    sh|bash|dash|zsh|fish|csh|tcsh|env|xargs|sudo|doas|su|ssh|scp|curl|wget|nc|ncat|socat|rm|rmdir|dd|kill|pkill)
      return 5 ;;
    true|test|pytest|pytest-*|ctest|rspec|swift|xcodebuild) : ;;
    git)
      verb=$(jq -r '.[1] // ""' "$argv")
      case $verb in diff|status|rev-parse|ls-files) :;; *) return 5;; esac ;;
    go)
      verb=$(jq -r '.[1] // ""' "$argv"); case $verb in test|build|vet) :;; *) return 5;; esac ;;
    cargo)
      verb=$(jq -r '.[1] // ""' "$argv"); case $verb in test|build|check|clippy|doc) :;; *) return 5;; esac
      jq -e 'index("--offline")!=null' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5 ;;
    npm|pnpm|yarn|bun)
      verb=$(jq -r '.[1] // ""' "$argv")
      case $verb in test|run) :;; ci|install) jq -e 'index("--offline")!=null' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5;; *) return 5;; esac
      if [ "$verb" = run ]; then
        script=$(jq -r '.[2] // ""' "$argv"); [ -n "$script" ] || return 5
        [ -f "$project/package.json" ] && jq -e --arg script "$script" '.scripts[$script]|type=="string" and length>0' \
          "$project/package.json" >/dev/null || return 5
      fi ;;
    make|gmake)
      [ -f "$project/Makefile" ] || [ -f "$project/makefile" ] || return 5
      jq -e 'all(.[1:][]?; test("^(clean|distclean|install|deploy|publish|push|release)$")|not)' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5 ;;
    cmake)
      jq -e '.[1]=="--build" and all(.[2:][]?; .!="--install")' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5 ;;
    python|python[0-9]|python[0-9].*)
      jq -e 'all(.[1:][]?; .!="-c" and .!="--command") and
        ((.[1]=="-m" and .[2]=="pytest") or (.[1]|type=="string" and test("^[^-].*\\.py$")))' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5 ;;
    node|deno|ruby)
      jq -e 'all(.[1:][]?; .!="-e" and .!="--eval" and .!="-p" and .!="--print") and
        (.[1]|type=="string" and test("^[^-]"))' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5 ;;
    dotnet)
      verb=$(jq -r '.[1] // ""' "$argv"); case $verb in test|build) :;; *) return 5;; esac ;;
    mvn|mvnw|gradle|gradlew)
      jq -e 'all(.[1:][]?; test("^(deploy|publish|release|clean)$")|not) and index("--offline")!=null' \
        "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5 ;;
    *) return 5 ;;
  esac
}
compile_argv() {
  local project argv output allow_local requested launch physical tool normalized identity interpreter paths
  project=$1 argv=$2 output=$3 allow_local=${4:-1}
  jq -e 'type=="array" and length>0 and all(.[];type=="string" and length>0 and
    (contains("\u0000")|not) and (test("[\\r\\n]")|not))' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5
  requested=$(jq -r '.[0]' "$argv"); launch=$(canonical_launch "$requested") || return 5
  physical=$(physical_executable "$launch") || return 5; tool=$(basename "$launch")
  reject_user_home_path "$launch" && reject_user_home_path "$physical" || return 5
  jq -e 'all(.[1:][]?;
    (test("(^|[=:])https?://")|not) and
    (test("^(--?(force|delete|deploy|publish|push|release|registry|network|online)|-(rf|fr))($|=)")|not) and
    (contains("`")|not) and (contains("$(")|not))' "$argv" >/dev/null || return 5
  normalized=$output.argv
  jq --arg project "$project" --arg executable "$physical" '
    [$executable] + [.[1:][] | if startswith("/") then
      if .==$project then "." elif startswith($project+"/") then .[($project|length)+1:] else error("outside project") end
    else if .==".." or startswith("../") or contains("/../") then error("path traversal") else . end end]' \
    "$argv" >"$normalized" || { rm -f "$normalized"; return 5; }
  validate_tool_verb "$project" "$tool" "$normalized" || { rm -f "$normalized"; return 5; }
  identity=$(identity_json "$launch"); interpreter=$(interpreter_json "$launch") || return 5
  if [ "$interpreter" != null ]; then
    reject_user_home_path "$(printf '%s\n' "$interpreter" | jq -r .launch_path)" &&
      reject_user_home_path "$(printf '%s\n' "$interpreter" | jq -r .physical_path)" || return 5
  fi
  paths=$(path_entries_json "$project" "$launch" "$physical" "$interpreter" "$allow_local")
  jq -n --arg tool "$tool" --argjson argv "$(jq -c . "$normalized")" \
    --argjson executable "$identity" --argjson interpreter "$interpreter" --argjson paths "$paths" \
    '{argv:$argv,tool:$tool,executable:$executable,interpreter:$interpreter,path_entries:$paths}' >"$output"
  rm -f "$normalized"
}
compile_controller() {
  local project proposal output tmp compiled count index group key allow_local
  project=$(canonical_project "$1") || return 5; proposal=$2 output=$3
  [ -f "$proposal" ] && [ ! -L "$proposal" ] && jq -e . "$proposal" >/dev/null || return 5
  tmp=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-command-policy.XXXXXX"); trap 'rm -rf "$tmp"' EXIT
  compiled=$tmp/compiled.json
  jq -n --arg proposal_hash "$(sha_file "$proposal")" --slurpfile p "$proposal" \
    '{schema_version:1,proposal_hash:$proposal_hash,builders:[],integrator:null,
      certification:{deadline_seconds:$p[0].certification.deadline_seconds,checks:{},fresh_checkout:{}}}' >"$compiled"
  count=$(jq '.builders|length' "$proposal"); index=0
  while [ "$index" -lt "$count" ]; do
    jq ".builders[$index].verification_argv" "$proposal" >"$tmp/argv"
    compile_argv "$project" "$tmp/argv" "$tmp/spec"
    jq --arg id "$(jq -r ".builders[$index].id" "$proposal")" --slurpfile s "$tmp/spec" \
      '.builders += [{id:$id,verification_argv:$s[0].argv,verification_command:$s[0]}]' \
      "$compiled" >"$compiled.next"; mv "$compiled.next" "$compiled"; index=$((index+1))
  done
  jq '.integrator.verification_argv' "$proposal" >"$tmp/argv"; compile_argv "$project" "$tmp/argv" "$tmp/spec"
  jq --arg id "$(jq -r .integrator.id "$proposal")" --slurpfile s "$tmp/spec" \
    '.integrator={id:$id,verification_argv:$s[0].argv,verification_command:$s[0]}' \
    "$compiled" >"$compiled.next"; mv "$compiled.next" "$compiled"
  for group in checks fresh_checkout; do
    for key in $(jq -r ".certification.$group|keys[]" "$proposal"); do
      jq --arg group "$group" --arg key "$key" '.certification[$group][$key].argv' \
        "$proposal" >"$tmp/argv"
      if [ "$group:$key" = fresh_checkout:install ]; then allow_local=0; else allow_local=1; fi
      compile_argv "$project" "$tmp/argv" "$tmp/spec" "$allow_local"
      jq --arg group "$group" --arg key "$key" --slurpfile s "$tmp/spec" \
        '.certification[$group][$key]=$s[0]' "$compiled" >"$compiled.next"; mv "$compiled.next" "$compiled"
    done
  done
  chmod 400 "$compiled"; mv "$compiled" "$output"
  rm -rf "$tmp"; trap - EXIT
}
verify_spec() {
  local project spec path actual actual_interpreter expected_hosts kind entry cursor old_ifs component tmp rc
  project=$(canonical_project "$1") || return 5; spec=$2
  [ -f "$spec" ] && [ ! -L "$spec" ] || return 5
  jq -e '(keys|sort)==["argv","executable","interpreter","path_entries","tool"] and
    (.argv|type=="array" and length>0) and
    (.executable|keys|sort)==["device","inode","launch_path","mode","physical_path","sha256"] and
    (.interpreter==null or (.interpreter|keys|sort)==["device","inode","launch_path","mode","physical_path","sha256"]) and
    (.path_entries|type=="array" and length>=2 and all(.[];
      ((keys|sort)==["kind","path"] and .kind=="host") or
      ((keys|sort)==["kind","path","required_if_present"] and .kind=="checkout_relative" and .required_if_present==true)))' \
    "$spec" >/dev/null || return 5
  path=$(canonical_launch "$(jq -r .executable.launch_path "$spec")") || return 5
  reject_user_home_path "$path" || return 5
  [ "$(physical_executable "$path")" = "$(jq -r .argv[0] "$spec")" ] || return 5
  reject_user_home_path "$(jq -r .argv[0] "$spec")" || return 5
  [ "$(basename "$path")" = "$(jq -r .tool "$spec")" ] || return 5
  actual=$(identity_json "$path")
  jq -e --argjson actual "$actual" '.executable==$actual' "$spec" >/dev/null || return 5
  actual_interpreter=$(interpreter_json "$path") || return 5
  if [ "$actual_interpreter" != null ]; then
    reject_user_home_path "$(printf '%s\n' "$actual_interpreter" | jq -r .launch_path)" &&
      reject_user_home_path "$(printf '%s\n' "$actual_interpreter" | jq -r .physical_path)" || return 5
  fi
  jq -e --argjson actual "$actual_interpreter" '.interpreter==$actual' "$spec" >/dev/null || return 5
  expected_hosts=$(path_entries_json "$project" "$path" "$(physical_executable "$path")" \
    "$actual_interpreter" 0)
  jq -e --argjson expected "$expected_hosts" '
    ([.path_entries[]|select(.kind=="host")]==$expected) and
    ([.path_entries[]|select(.kind=="checkout_relative")]|length<=1) and
    all(.path_entries[]|select(.kind=="checkout_relative");
      .=={kind:"checkout_relative",path:"node_modules/.bin",required_if_present:true})' \
    "$spec" >/dev/null || return 5
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r kind entry; do
    if [ "$kind" = host ]; then
      [ -d "$entry" ] && [ ! -L "$entry" ] && [ "$(cd "$entry" && pwd -P)" = "$entry" ] || return 5
    else
      case $entry in ''|/*|..|../*|*/../*) return 5;; esac
      cursor=$project; old_ifs=$IFS; IFS=/
      for component in $entry; do cursor=$cursor/$component; [ ! -L "$cursor" ] || { IFS=$old_ifs; return 5; }; done
      IFS=$old_ifs; [ ! -e "$cursor" ] || [ -d "$cursor" ] || return 5
      [ "$entry" != node_modules/.bin ] || [ -f "$project/package.json" ] || return 5
    fi
  done < <(jq -r '.path_entries[]|[.kind,.path]|@tsv' "$spec")
  tmp=$(mktemp); jq '.argv' "$spec" >"$tmp"
  validate_tool_verb "$project" "$(jq -r .tool "$spec")" "$tmp"; rc=$?; rm -f "$tmp"; return "$rc"
}
case ${1:-} in
  compile-controller) [ "$#" -eq 4 ] || exit 2; compile_controller "$2" "$3" "$4" ;;
  verify-spec) [ "$#" -eq 3 ] || exit 2; verify_spec "$2" "$3" ;;
  *) exit 2 ;;
esac
```

The compiler itself never executes a proposed command. It rejects unknown tools, shells,
privilege/process/network programs, interpreter inline code, deploy/publish/destructive
verbs and flags, NUL/newline-bearing arguments, traversal, and absolute operands outside
the project. Every accepted absolute project operand is made checkout-relative. Package
install commands require offline mode. Its immutable command spec binds logical launch path,
physical executable and shebang-interpreter paths, device, inode, mode, and SHA-256. It also
freezes a minimal `path_entries` array containing only canonical host directories required by
those identities, `/usr/bin`, `/bin`, and manifest-approved checkout-relative tool directories.
The eventual runner uses `env -i` with only fixed `HOME`, compiled `PATH`, `TMPDIR`, `LANG`,
`LC_ALL`, `TZ`, and `CI`, and calls `verify-spec` both
immediately before spawn and after wait. Missing or unsafe commands route typed bounded
`unsafe_command_policy` recovery; only a genuine product-strategy ambiguity may request a
`core_strategy_decision`.

The executable placed in `argv[0]` is the frozen physical path, never the logical launch
symlink. Task 6 may retain the logical path only as evidence; it executes the physical path
inside the fail-closed isolation boundary described there. A logical-link swap after
compilation therefore cannot redirect execution, and any link/physical identity change is
still rejected by the immediate verifier.

Add hostile contract cases for `/bin/rm`, `/bin/sh -c`, `sudo`, `curl`, `npm publish`,
`npm ci` without `--offline`, `python -c`, a newline argument, `../../escape`, an absolute
outside path, a swapped executable inode, and a changed executable hash. All exit 5 without
spawning a child. A repository-declared `npm run test`, offline install, `/usr/bin/test`, and
project-relative test script compile successfully. Add a Homebrew-style `npm` symlink with an
`/usr/bin/env node` shebang, a Linux system-tool fixture, and hostile inherited/proposal PATH
entries; the first two compile and run from the compiled path entries while PATH injection
is absent or rejected. Also swap the logical symlink between compile/verify/exec and assert
the broker either runs the originally frozen physical identity or exits 5 before spawn; it
must never execute the replacement.

Run:

```bash
chmod +x core/scripts/polylane-command-policy.sh
bash -n core/scripts/polylane-command-policy.sh
```

Expected: exit 0 with no output.

- [ ] **Action 4.2 (2–5 minutes): Add the complete controller-runtime contract driver**

Create `core/tests/helpers/controller-contract.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
scenario=${1:?scenario required}
REPO=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../../.." && pwd -P)
BIN=$REPO/core/scripts
AUTONOMY_TMP=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-controller-test.XXXXXX")
export AUTONOMY_TMP POLYLANE_NOW_EPOCH=1000 POLYLANE_TEST_MODE=1
trap 'if [ -f "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime/session.json" ]; then loop=$(jq -r .session "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime/session.json" | sed "s/^polylane-//"); generation=$(jq -r .generation "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime/session.json"); "$BIN/polylane-finalizer.sh" emergency-cleanup --project "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project" --loop-id "$loop" --generation "$generation" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi; rm -rf "$AUTONOMY_TMP"' EXIT
. "$REPO/core/tests/helpers/autonomy-contract.sh"
project=$AUTONOMY_TMP/project
runtime=$project/.polylane/runtime
state=$project/docs/polylane/controller-state.json
mkdir -p "$runtime" "$(dirname "$state")"
"$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" init --loop-id pl1 --agent codex --project "$project"
jq -n --arg project "$project" '{schema_version:1,revision:1,runtime_revision:1,loop_id:"pl1",agent:"codex",project_root:$project,
  phase:"WORKING",terminal:null,stage:"PLANNED",cycle:1,cycle_run_id:"pl1-c0001-a01",current_action_id:"bootstrap",
  last_progress_at:1000,next_action_at:null,session:{name:"polylane-pl1",controller_window:"controller",lanes_window:"lanes",
  watch_command:"tmux attach -t polylane-pl1"},provider:{state:"available",last_probe_at:null,next_probe_at:null},
  recovery:{attempt:0,reason_code:null,strategy_hash:null},terminal_evidence:null}' >"$state"
make_launch_spec() {
  kind=$1 destination=$2 argv_file=$3
  hash=$(jq -Sc . "$argv_file" | shasum -a 256 | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
  mkdir -p "$runtime/launch-specs"; chmod 700 "$runtime/launch-specs"
  content_tmp=$runtime/launch-specs/.fixture.$$
  jq -n --arg kind "$kind" --arg project "$project" --arg hash "$hash" --slurpfile argv "$argv_file" \
    '{schema_version:1,kind:$kind,project_root:$project,loop_id:"pl1",generation:1,
      argv:$argv[0],argv_sha256:$hash}' >"$content_tmp"
  content_hash=$(shasum -a 256 "$content_tmp" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
  content=$runtime/launch-specs/${content_hash#sha256:}.json
  chmod 400 "$content_tmp"; mv "$content_tmp" "$content"
  jq -n --arg path "$content" --arg hash "$content_hash" \
    '{schema_version:1,content:{path:$path,hash:$hash}}' >"$destination"
  chmod 600 "$destination"
}
make_condition_evidence() {
  request=$1 condition_type=$2 evidence=$3
  action="condition:$(jq -r .request_id "$request")"
  payload=$runtime/condition-payload.$$.json
  jq -n --arg loop "$(jq -r .loop_id "$request")" --arg request_id "$(jq -r .request_id "$request")" \
    --arg reason "$(jq -r .reason_code "$request")" --arg type "$condition_type" \
    '{loop_id:$loop,request_id:$request_id,reason_code:$reason,condition_type:$type}' >"$payload"
  "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" emit controller input_condition_observed \
    "$action" --payload-file "$payload"
  "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" reduce
  event=$(find "$runtime/events" -type f -name '*.json' -exec jq -er \
    --arg action "$action" 'select(.actor=="controller" and
      .type=="input_condition_observed" and .action_id==$action)|input_filename' {} + | tail -1)
  [ -f "$event" ] || autonomy_fail missing-condition-event
  file_hash=$(shasum -a 256 "$event" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
  jq -n --arg loop "$(jq -r .loop_id "$request")" --arg request_id "$(jq -r .request_id "$request")" \
    --arg reason "$(jq -r .reason_code "$request")" --arg type "$condition_type" \
    --arg event "$event" --arg file_hash "$file_hash" --arg hash "$(jq -r .event_hash "$event")" \
    --argjson id "$(jq -r .sequence "$event")" --argjson observed "$(jq -r .at "$event")" \
    '{schema_version:1,loop_id:$loop,request_id:$request_id,reason_code:$reason,
      condition_type:$type,source_actor:"controller",observed_at:$observed,
      event:{id:$id,hash:$hash,path:$event,file_hash:$file_hash}}' >"$evidence"
  rm -f "$payload"
}

case $scenario in
  terminal)
    perfection=$project/docs/polylane/perfection.json
    final=$project/docs/polylane/final-suggestions.json
    delivery=$project/docs/polylane/final-delivery.json
    printf '%s\n' '{"consecutive_clean":0,"records":[]}' >"$perfection"
    printf '%s\n' '{"loop_id":"pl1","suggestions":[]}' >"$final"
    printf '%s\n' '{"loop_id":"pl1","status":"pending_receipt"}' >"$delivery"
    for terminal in FAILED HALTED BUDGET TIMEOUT NO_GO RETRY_EXHAUSTED; do
      assert_rc "terminal-$terminal" 5 "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" request "$state" "$terminal"
    done
    assert_rc incomplete-complete 5 "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" complete "$state" "$perfection" "$final" "$delivery"
    request=$project/docs/polylane/needs-input.json
    jq -n '{loop_id:"pl1",reason_code:"rate_limit",question:"Retry?",recommended_answer:"yes",impact:"delay",request_id:"r1",created_at:1000}' >"$request"
    assert_rc invalid-input-reason 5 "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" needs-input "$state" "$request" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/missing-evidence"
    jq '.reason_code="credential"' "$request" >"$request.next" && mv "$request.next" "$request"
    evidence=$AUTONOMY_TMP/condition-evidence.json
    make_condition_evidence "$request" credential_missing "$evidence"
    direct=$AUTONOMY_TMP/direct-terminal.json
    jq -n --slurpfile request "$request" '{request:$request[0]}' >"$direct"
    assert_rc direct-complete-forbidden 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" \
      emit terminal terminal_complete direct-complete --payload-file "$direct"
    assert_rc direct-waiting-forbidden 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" \
      emit terminal terminal_waiting direct-waiting --payload-file "$direct"
    assert_rc generic-transaction-forbidden 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" \
      emit terminal terminal_transaction direct-transaction --payload-file "$direct"
    "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" needs-input "$state" "$request" "$evidence"
    "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    assert_json waiting '.phase' WAITING_FOR_USER "$runtime/current/state.json"
    transaction=$(find "$runtime/terminal-staging" -name '*.json' -type f | LC_ALL=C sort | tail -1)
    "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" terminal-transaction "$transaction"
    assert_json replay-idempotent '[.terminal_transactions|to_entries[]]|length' 1 \
      "$runtime/current/state.json"
    forged=$runtime/terminal-staging/forged-waiting.json
    jq '.body.request.question="forged"' "$transaction" >"$forged"; chmod 400 "$forged"
    assert_rc forged-terminal-hmac 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" \
      terminal-transaction "$forged"
    dead_base=$runtime/terminal-staging/.dead-actor-base.json
    jq -Sc 'del(.auth_tag,.transaction_hash)|
      .transaction_id="terminal:waiting:999:aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa"' \
      "$transaction" >"$dead_base"
    dead_hash=$(shasum -a 256 "$dead_base" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
    jq -Sc --arg hash "$dead_hash" '.+{transaction_hash:$hash}' "$dead_base" >"$dead_base.signed"
    dead_tag=$("$BIN/polylane-hmac.py" sign "$runtime/terminal-auth.key" "$dead_base.signed")
    dead=$runtime/terminal-staging/dead-actor.json
    jq -Sc --arg tag "$dead_tag" '.+{auth_tag:$tag}' "$dead_base.signed" >"$dead"; chmod 400 "$dead"
    assert_rc dead-terminal-actor 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" \
      terminal-transaction "$dead"
    rm -f "$dead_base" "$dead_base.signed"
    stale=$runtime/terminal-staging/stale-waiting.json
    jq '.transaction_id="terminal:waiting:stale"' "$transaction" >"$stale"; chmod 400 "$stale"
    assert_rc stale-terminal-transaction 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" \
      terminal-transaction "$stale"
    assert_json stale-no-transition '.terminal' WAITING_FOR_USER "$runtime/current/state.json"
    ;;
  input)
    request=$project/docs/polylane/needs-input.json
    answer=$AUTONOMY_TMP/answer.json
    jq -n '{loop_id:"pl1",reason_code:"core_strategy_decision",question:"Choose the durable key?",recommended_answer:"A",impact:"changes public schema",request_id:"r9",created_at:1000}' >"$request"
    evidence=$AUTONOMY_TMP/condition-evidence.json
    make_condition_evidence "$request" core_strategy_ambiguity "$evidence"
    "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" needs-input "$state" "$request" "$evidence"
    "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    mock=$AUTONOMY_TMP/resume-controller
    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\n: >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller-resumed"\nsleep 30\n' >"$mock"; chmod 700 "$mock"
    printf '["%s"]\n' "$mock" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller.argv"
    make_launch_spec controller "$runtime/controller-launch.json" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller.argv"
    sleep 30 & paused_controller=$!
    paused_token=$(ps -o lstart= -p "$paused_controller" | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')
    jq -n --argjson pid "$paused_controller" --arg token "$paused_token" \
      '{actor:"controller",pid:$pid,start_token:$token,generation_token:"controller-before-input",
        lease_valid:true,descendants:[]}' >"$runtime/actor-controller.json"
    "$BIN/polylane-guardian.sh" check-once --runtime "$runtime" --state "$state" --now 1000 \
      --controller-launch "$runtime/controller-launch.json"
    assert_eq waiting-fenced false "$(kill -0 "$paused_controller" 2>/dev/null && printf true || printf false)"
    assert_eq waiting-no-restart false "$([ -e "$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller-resumed" ] && printf true || printf false)"
    assert_json waiting-paused '.controller_paused' true "$runtime/current/state.json"
    jq -n '{loop_id:"pl1",request_id:"r9",answer:"A",answered_at:1001}' >"$answer"
    "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" answer "$state" "$request" "$answer"
    "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    assert_json working '.phase' WORKING "$runtime/current/state.json"
    "$BIN/polylane-guardian.sh" check-once --runtime "$runtime" --state "$state" --now 1001 \
      --controller-launch "$runtime/controller-launch.json"
    assert_eq answer-restarts true "$([ -e "$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller-resumed" ] && printf true || printf false)"
    assert_eq active-cleared false "$([ -e "$request" ] && printf true || printf false)"
    assert_eq one-history 1 "$(find "$project/docs/polylane/input-history" -name 'r9*.json' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
    "$BIN/polylane-terminal.sh" answer "$state" "$request" "$answer"
    assert_eq duplicate-history 1 "$(find "$project/docs/polylane/input-history" -name 'r9*.json' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
    ;;
  guardian)
    mock=$AUTONOMY_TMP/mock-controller
    cat >"$mock" <<'MOCK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
printf 'controller-start\n'
exit "${MOCK_CONTROLLER_RC:-75}"
MOCK
    chmod +x "$mock"
    export POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD="$mock"
    printf '["%s"]\n' "$mock" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller.argv"
    make_launch_spec controller "$runtime/controller-launch.json" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller.argv"
    "$BIN/polylane-guardian.sh" check-once --runtime "$runtime" --state "$state" --now 1000 \
      --controller-launch "$runtime/controller-launch.json" || true
    "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    assert_json recovering '.phase' RECOVERING "$runtime/current/state.json"
    assert_json recovery-queued '[.queue.items[]|select(.status=="ready" or .status=="scheduled")]|length>0' true "$runtime/current/state.json"
    printf '["/usr/bin/true"]\n' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/sentinel.argv"
    make_launch_spec sentinel "$runtime/sentinel-launch.json" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/sentinel.argv"
    printf '["/usr/bin/true"]\n' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/keeper.argv"
    make_launch_spec keeper "$runtime/keeper-launch.json" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/keeper.argv"
    for ignored in 1 2 3; do "$BIN/polylane-keeper.sh" start --project "$project" --loop-id pl1 \
      --sentinel-launch "$runtime/sentinel-launch.json" --keeper-launch "$runtime/keeper-launch.json"; done
    assert_eq one-keeper 1 "$(find "$runtime/keeper.lock" -name owner.json | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
    ;;
  fencing)
    sleep 30 & child=$!
    child_token=$(ps -o lstart= -p "$child" | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')
    jq -n --argjson pid "$child" --arg token "$child_token" '{actor:"controller",pid:$pid,start_token:$token,generation_token:"g1",lease_valid:true,descendants:[]}' >"$runtime/actor-controller.json"
    "$BIN/polylane-process.sh" adopt-or-fence "$runtime" controller g1
    assert_eq adopted true "$(kill -0 "$child" 2>/dev/null && printf true || printf false)"
    assert_rc stale-token 6 "$BIN/polylane-process.sh" assert-current "$runtime" controller old
    "$BIN/polylane-process.sh" adopt-or-fence "$runtime" controller g2
    assert_eq fenced false "$(kill -0 "$child" 2>/dev/null && printf true || printf false)"
    jq -n '{actor:"missing",pid:99999999,start_token:"",generation_token:"g1",lease_valid:true,descendants:[]}' >"$runtime/actor-missing.json"
    "$BIN/polylane-process.sh" adopt-or-fence "$runtime" missing g1
    assert_json nonexistent-never-adopted '.generation_token' fenced "$runtime/actor-missing.json"
    ;;
  shutdown)
    jq -n '{}' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/direct-terminal.json"
    assert_rc no-direct-complete 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" emit \
      terminal terminal_complete complete --payload-file "$AUTONOMY_TMP/direct-terminal.json"
    assert_rc no-direct-waiting 5 "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" emit \
      terminal terminal_waiting waiting --payload-file "$AUTONOMY_TMP/direct-terminal.json"
    # Crash/replay of authenticated COMPLETE and the finalizer is exercised after the real
    # two-certification finalization fixture in convergence-contract.sh delivery mode.
    ;;
  resume)
    mock="$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller path ; touch SHOULD_NOT_EXIST"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$mock")"
    printf '#!/usr/bin/env bash\nprintf "%%s\\n" "$0" "$@" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/argv"\n' >"$mock"
    chmod 700 "$mock"
    export POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD="$mock"
    "$REPO/codex/scripts/polylane-codex-loop.sh" "$project"
    [ ! -e "$project/SHOULD_NOT_EXIST" ] || autonomy_fail shell-metacharacter-executed
    controller_content=$(jq -r .content.path "$runtime/controller-launch.json")
    guardian_content=$(jq -r .content.path "$runtime/guardian-launch.json")
    sentinel_content=$(jq -r .content.path "$runtime/sentinel-launch.json")
    keeper_content=$(jq -r .content.path "$runtime/keeper-launch.json")
    jq -e --arg executable "$mock" '.kind=="controller" and .argv[0]==$executable and
      (.argv_sha256|startswith("sha256:"))' "$controller_content" >/dev/null || autonomy_fail argv-path-split
    jq -e --arg controller "$runtime/controller-launch.json" '.kind=="guardian" and
      (.argv[0]|endswith("polylane-guardian.sh")) and (.argv|index($controller)!=null)' "$guardian_content" >/dev/null || autonomy_fail guardian-chain
    jq -e --arg guardian "$runtime/guardian-launch.json" --arg keeper "$runtime/keeper-launch.json" '.kind=="sentinel" and
      (.argv[0]|endswith("polylane-sentinel.sh")) and (.argv|index($guardian)!=null) and (.argv|index($keeper)!=null)' \
      "$sentinel_content" >/dev/null || autonomy_fail sentinel-chain
    jq -e --arg sentinel "$runtime/sentinel-launch.json" --arg self "$runtime/keeper-launch.json" '.kind=="keeper" and
      (.argv[0]|endswith("polylane-keeper.sh")) and (.argv|index($sentinel)!=null) and (.argv|index($self)!=null)' \
      "$keeper_content" >/dev/null || autonomy_fail keeper-chain
    POLYLANE_EXPECTED_PROJECT=$project POLYLANE_EXPECTED_LOOP_ID=pl1 \
      "$BIN/polylane-exec-argv.sh" --spec "$runtime/controller-launch.json"
    [ ! -e "$project/SHOULD_NOT_EXIST" ] || autonomy_fail executor-shell-metacharacter
    printf '%s\n' '["/usr/bin/printf","safe argument"]' >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/argv.json"
    chmod 600 "$AUTONOMY_TMP/argv.json"
    unset POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD
    export POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_ARGV_FILE="$AUTONOMY_TMP/argv.json"
    "$REPO/codex/scripts/polylane-codex-loop.sh" "$project" --resume
    assert_json stable-loop '.loop_id' pl1 "$state"
    controller_content=$(jq -r .content.path "$runtime/controller-launch.json")
    assert_json argv-envelope '.argv[1]' 'safe argument' "$controller_content"
    ;;
  cycle)
    base=$(git -C "$project" init -q && git -C "$project" commit --allow-empty -qm base && git -C "$project" rev-parse HEAD)
    git -C "$project" commit --allow-empty -qm integration-a; a=$(git -C "$project" rev-parse HEAD)
    git -C "$project" reset --hard -q "$base"; git -C "$project" commit --allow-empty -qm integration-b; b=$(git -C "$project" rev-parse HEAD)
    git -C "$project" update-ref refs/heads/main "$base"
    ( git -C "$project" update-ref refs/heads/main "$a" "$base" ) & p1=$!
    ( git -C "$project" update-ref refs/heads/main "$b" "$base" ) & p2=$!
    set +e; wait "$p1"; r1=$?; wait "$p2"; r2=$?; set -e
    assert_eq one-promotion 1 "$(( (r1==0) + (r2==0) ))"
    ;;
  codex)
    export POLYLANE_TEST_FOREGROUND=1 POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD=/usr/bin/true
    output=$("$REPO/codex/scripts/polylane-codex-loop.sh" "$project")
    assert_eq one-attach 1 "$(printf '%s\n' "$output" | grep -c '^tmux attach -t polylane-')"
    assert_rc incomplete-shutdown 3 "$REPO/codex/scripts/polylane-codex-loop.sh" "$project" --resume --foreground-once
    ;;
  *) printf 'unknown controller scenario: %s\n' "$scenario" >&2; exit 2 ;;
esac

finish_autonomy_test "controller-$scenario"
```

bash core/tests/test-needs-input.sh
