```bash
bash core/tests/test-shutdown-replay.sh
bash core/tests/test-controller-resume.sh
bash core/tests/test-cycle-transactions.sh
bash codex/tests/test-codex-loop.sh
```

Expected RED: every test exits nonzero because its production helper is absent; no test may
pass using direct state edits or a mock-only shutdown path.

**Contract — durable controller projection:**

Initialize `docs/polylane/controller-state.json` atomically:

```json
{
  "schema_version":1,
  "revision":1,
  "loop_id":"pl-abc123",
  "agent":"codex",
  "project_root":"/absolute/project",
  "phase":"WORKING",
  "terminal":null,
  "stage":"PLANNED",
  "cycle":1,
  "cycle_run_id":"pl-abc123-c0001-a01",
  "current_action_id":"bootstrap",
  "last_progress_at":1000,
  "next_action_at":null,
  "session":{"name":"polylane-pl-abc123","controller_window":"controller","lanes_window":"lanes","watch_command":"tmux attach -t polylane-pl-abc123"},
  "provider":{"state":"available","last_probe_at":null,"next_probe_at":null},
  "recovery":{"attempt":0,"reason_code":null,"strategy_hash":null},
  "terminal_evidence":null
}
```

Persist before tmux launch. Resume validates `agent`, canonical project, and owned session.
The published runtime snapshot/event cursor and projection payload are authoritative. The docs controller file is
an atomic durable projection with `runtime_revision`; startup repairs it from the current
snapshot whenever revisions differ. Only the reducer applies phase/terminal transitions,
so operational and durable views cannot independently choose a state.

**Contract — keeper/sentinel/guardian/finalizer loops:**

`polylane-keeper.sh start` claims `.polylane/runtime/keeper.lock` by canonical project,
loop id, PID, and monotonically increasing generation, then detaches `run` with closed stdin
and an owned log. Concurrent starts join the live generation. The keeper ensures the tagged
session exists, recreates it when lost, and monitors the sentinel heartbeat. The controller
window runs `polylane-sentinel.sh` as its foreground process. The sentinel
starts the guardian child, checks its separate heartbeat every five seconds, and replaces a
dead or stale guardian; it also restarts a dead/stale keeper from the validated launch file.
Every sentinel external probe has a bounded timeout. If the
sentinel exits, the owned tmux `pane-died` hook respawns it from a validated
`.polylane/runtime/controller-launch.json` argv array; no `eval` or queue-provided command
is used.

The guardian starts the Codex controller as a child, records PID/generation, observes controller
log fingerprint plus runtime events, reduces state immediately on wake, and checks at most
every five seconds. Lease expiry captures the log tail, process command, queue state, and
git summary; it checkpoints and routes recovery before replacing the child. The guardian
itself contains no Codex/API call and can recover a dead or falsely-heartbeating controller.

Every actor record includes PID, process start token, tmux pane id when applicable,
generation token, and the captured descendant set. On guardian takeover, adopt a still
valid actor instead of duplicating it; fence an invalid actor before replacement. Runtime,
queue, terminal, cycle-result, cleanup, and promotion entrypoints call `assert-current`
under their mutation lock. Codex lanes can write only their isolated attempt worktree;
acceptance ignores a marker/commit whose attempt token is stale. This token fencing is the
final safety boundary even if an OS process ignores termination.

On reducer-approved COMPLETE, keeper and a detached finalizer claim the same shutdown token
and mutually check liveness until teardown is durable. They stop new reservations, drain or
fence current workers/controller, disable sentinel restart, disarm only the owned tmux hook,
and close only the tagged session. Either peer can replay every stage; neither exits until a
published snapshot says `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE` and verifies all owned PIDs/session are absent.
WAITING_FOR_USER bypasses terminal shutdown; the collision exception uses its dedicated
keeper-only input state rather than pretending an owned session is active.

Each wait ends at the earliest actor/claim deadline, due `next_action_at`, or five-second
fallback. At a boundary the guardian atomically emits the recovery/claim transition before
publishing an expired/idle snapshot. Audit derives covered and uncovered timestamp intervals
from ordered events and deadlines; it does not infer zero idle from five-second samples.

Before any timed wait it records `next_action_at`. A signal trap writes a resumable
diagnostic and leaves controller/goal state intact.

**Contract — Codex loop/controller wrappers and cycle transaction:**

`polylane-codex-loop.sh` creates a cryptographically random or git/time-derived safe
`loop_id`, invokes session `ensure`, writes/announces the watch command, and seeds the
`controller` window through `arm-controller`, so the sentinel and tmux death hook guard the
guardian command. It defines `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD` as an explicit test/expert override
for one absolute executable path and `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_ARGV_FILE` for a validated JSON
argv array; neither value is treated as shell text and production defaults to the Codex
controller adapter. It resolves controller model
by passing `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_MODEL` (if set) to the installed
`polylane-codex-model.sh resolve-model`; effort similarly uses the shared resolver with
`POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_EFFORT` and default `xhigh`. On resume it validates and reuses state,
keeper generation, and session.

`polylane-codex-controller.sh` runs the deterministic prepare/apply/step loop. Only its
Codex-owned turn job renders the compact immutable planner prompt and executes the exact
argv below; stdout is a private JSONL event log, stderr is a separate private diagnostic,
and the proposal comes only from `--output-last-message`:

```text
codex exec --ephemeral --sandbox read-only -C <project> -c approval_policy=never \
  --model <controller-model> -c model_reasoning_effort=<effort> \
  --output-schema <immutable-schema> -o <private-proposal-file> --json - \
  < <immutable-controller-prompt> > <private-events.jsonl> 2> <private-stderr.log>
```

The turn job rejects malformed JSONL, a failed/error event, or a stream whose final event
is not `turn.completed`; it then validates the separate proposal file and only the core
driver may apply it. Mixed stdout/stderr and prose-directed file writes are never proposal
transport.

It observes output and work artifacts as progress fingerprints. Each cycle manifest has
stable `loop_id`, numeric `cycle`, and a fresh nonce `run_id`. The controller atomically
enqueues exactly one `cycle` item containing the validated manifest path/hash; guardian
dispatch invokes the Codex fail-closed cycle launcher, whose single runner owns concurrent
builders and the dependent integrator in `lanes`. No per-builder queue item may start a
competing runner. The nonce-valid cycle result completes that one queue claim. A controller
exit while incomplete is recovery, not success. Before starting a quiet Codex child it declares the child PID and an explicit
maximum quiet deadline (default 300 seconds, configurable); any changed output returns the
actor to the ordinary 90-second renewable lease, while the hard quiet deadline never
extends without new proof.

Before every irreversible cycle effect, emit the next stage with an idempotency key and
expected base commit. On resume, reconcile the current git base, integrator ancestry,
cycle-result nonce, cleanup ownership, and queued next action against the stage. Promotion,
result recording, cleanup, and next enqueue are each safe to repeat after a crash but are
recorded exactly once. Base promotion uses a mutation lock plus
`git update-ref <base-ref> <promoted-commit> <expected-base>` compare-and-swap; a mismatch
never overwrites concurrent work and becomes typed reconciliation before any worktree sync.

**Contract — foreground Codex skill, attach-line deduplication, follow cursor, and final gate:**

Codex `SKILL.md` launches `polylane-codex-loop.sh`, reads `needs-input.json` on re-entry,
and never re-interviews a resumed run. It surfaces the watch command line whenever launch
or resume returns it. On every skill entry it calls global
`polylane-state.sh --watch --all` and displays one standalone command for every active owned run;
keeper recreation events are reannounced on the next available chat interaction.
The foreground skill unions launcher-returned and global-discovery records by owned
`(session, generation)`, sorts them, and renders each distinct attach line exactly once in
that response. This response-local deduplication never suppresses an announcement in a
later chat turn. `codex/tests/test-codex-loop.sh` covers a new launch, a one-run resume,
same-launch recreation where both sources report the current command, and multiple active
runs; each case requires one sorted standalone line per active session and no duplicate for
the resumed run.

After launch, the foreground Codex turn repeatedly calls `polylane-state.sh follow` with a
maximum 30-second wait, publishes concise progress/cycle/GitHub-skill suggestion updates,
and advances its durable chat cursor while the detached loop keeps working. It does not end
the original task merely because a cycle finished. On COMPLETE it renders the deterministic
30-item pending delivery only after shutdown reaches `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE` and the foreground
has independently verified the owned session and every keeper/sentinel/guardian/finalizer/
controller/worker PID are absent. `COMPLETE` at `TERMINAL_PUBLISHED` remains a nonfinal
progress update. On WAITING it asks the one persisted question. If the Codex host turn is
interrupted, the next skill entry resumes from the cursor and replays every unacknowledged
deterministic message id. `codex/tests/test-codex-loop.sh` proves the foreground does not
return during any intermediate shutdown stage.

- [ ] **Action 4.4b (2–5 minutes): Add the shared constant-time HMAC primitive**

Create `core/scripts/polylane-hmac.py` with this complete body:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import hashlib
import hmac
import os
import re
import stat
import sys

TAG = re.compile(r"hmac-sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}\Z")


def die(code: int = 5) -> None:
    raise SystemExit(code)


def read_regular(path: str, *, key: bool = False) -> bytes:
    flags = os.O_RDONLY | getattr(os, "O_NOFOLLOW", 0)
    try:
        fd = os.open(path, flags)
    except OSError:
        die()
    try:
        info = os.fstat(fd)
        if not stat.S_ISREG(info.st_mode):
            die()
        if key and stat.S_IMODE(info.st_mode) != 0o400:
            die()
        chunks = []
        while True:
            block = os.read(fd, 1024 * 1024)
            if not block:
                break
            chunks.append(block)
        value = b"".join(chunks)
    finally:
        os.close(fd)
    if key and len(value) != 32:
        die()
    return value


def keygen(path: str) -> None:
    flags = os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | getattr(os, "O_NOFOLLOW", 0)
    try:
        fd = os.open(path, flags, 0o400)
    except OSError:
        die()
    try:
        value = os.urandom(32)
        if os.write(fd, value) != len(value):
            die()
        os.fchmod(fd, 0o400)
        os.fsync(fd)
    finally:
        os.close(fd)


def calculate(key_path: str, message_path: str) -> str:
    key = read_regular(key_path, key=True)
    message = read_regular(message_path)
    return "hmac-sha256:" + hmac.new(key, message, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()


def main() -> None:
    if len(sys.argv) == 3 and sys.argv[1] == "keygen":
        keygen(sys.argv[2])
        return
    if len(sys.argv) == 4 and sys.argv[1] == "sign":
        print(calculate(sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]))
        return
    if len(sys.argv) == 5 and sys.argv[1] == "verify":
        supplied = sys.argv[4]
        if TAG.fullmatch(supplied) is None:
            die()
        expected = calculate(sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3])
        if not hmac.compare_digest(expected, supplied):
            die()
        return
    die(2)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Create `core/scripts/polylane-detach.py` with this complete body. It is the only finalizer
launcher: the second fork is reparented before the helper returns, so the finalizer can
fence the keeper's complete descendant tree without ever targeting itself.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import os
import sys


def main() -> None:
    if len(sys.argv) < 5 or sys.argv[1] != "--log" or "--" not in sys.argv[3:]:
        raise SystemExit(2)
    log = os.path.abspath(sys.argv[2])
    split = sys.argv.index("--")
    argv = sys.argv[split + 1 :]
    if not argv or not os.path.isabs(argv[0]):
        raise SystemExit(2)
    read_fd, write_fd = os.pipe()
    first = os.fork()
    if first:
        os.close(write_fd)
        data = b""
        while not data.endswith(b"\n"):
            block = os.read(read_fd, 64)
            if not block:
                break
            data += block
        os.close(read_fd)
        _, status = os.waitpid(first, 0)
        if status != 0 or not data.rstrip().isdigit():
            raise SystemExit(5)
        print(data.decode("ascii").strip())
        return
    os.close(read_fd)
    os.setsid()
    second = os.fork()
    if second:
        os.write(write_fd, (str(second) + "\n").encode("ascii"))
        os.close(write_fd)
        os._exit(0)
    os.close(write_fd)
    devnull = os.open(os.devnull, os.O_RDONLY)
    logfile = os.open(log, os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
    os.dup2(devnull, 0)
    os.dup2(logfile, 1)
    os.dup2(logfile, 2)
    os.close(devnull)
    os.close(logfile)
    os.execv(argv[0], argv)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Create `core/config/polylane-input-conditions-v1.json`:

```json
{"schema_version":1,"max_age_seconds":300,"conditions":[
  {"reason_code":"credential","actor":"controller","condition_type":"credential_missing"},
  {"reason_code":"billing","actor":"controller","condition_type":"billing_required"},
  {"reason_code":"account_permission","actor":"controller","condition_type":"account_permission_required"},
  {"reason_code":"core_strategy_decision","actor":"controller","condition_type":"core_strategy_ambiguity"},
  {"reason_code":"irreversible_external_action","actor":"policy","condition_type":"irreversible_action_pending"},
  {"reason_code":"external_resource_conflict","actor":"keeper","condition_type":"owned_session_name_conflict"}
]}
```

In `runtime_init`, create mode-0700 `terminal-staging`, `terminal-requests`,
`terminal-suggestions`, `terminal-condition-evidence`, and `terminal-actors` directories;
run `polylane-hmac.py keygen "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-auth.key"`; create
`terminal-actor-generation` containing `0` mode 0600. Initialization must fail if any of
these paths is a symlink or if the key already exists unexpectedly. Never serialize or log
the key. The same helper is used for terminal transactions and soak injections; no shell
concatenation or ordinary SHA-256 is an authentication tag.

Run:

```bash
chmod +x core/scripts/polylane-hmac.py core/scripts/polylane-detach.py
python3 -m py_compile core/scripts/polylane-hmac.py
python3 -m py_compile core/scripts/polylane-detach.py
jq -e '.schema_version==1 and (.conditions|length)==6 and
  ([.conditions[].reason_code]|unique|length)==6' \
  core/config/polylane-input-conditions-v1.json
```

- [ ] **Action 4.5 (2–5 minutes): Add reducer-owned terminal/input transactions**

Create `core/scripts/lib/polylane-terminal-reducer.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
terminal_hash_json() { jq -Sc . "$1" | runtime_sha256_stdin; }
terminal_reject() {
  local state event output reason
  state=$1 event=$2 output=$3 reason=$4
  jq --arg reason "$reason" --slurpfile event "$event" '
    ($event[0]) as $x |
    .terminal_transactions=(.terminal_transactions//{}) |
    .terminal_results=(.terminal_results//{}) |
    .terminal_results[$x.action_id]={status:"rejected",reason:$reason,
      event_id:$x.sequence,event_hash:$x.event_hash} |
    .last_event_sequence=$x.sequence | .last_event_hash=$x.event_hash' "$state" >"$output"
}
terminal_mode() { stat -f '%Lp' "$1" 2>/dev/null || stat -c '%a' "$1"; }
terminal_file_under() {
  local root file parent
  root=$(cd "$1" && pwd -P) || return 5; file=$2
  [ -f "$file" ] && [ ! -L "$file" ] || return 5
  parent=$(cd "$(dirname "$file")" && pwd -P) || return 5
  [ "$parent" = "$root" ] || return 5
  printf '%s/%s\n' "$parent" "$(basename "$file")"
}
terminal_validate_actor() {
  local payload actor path hash current pid start actor_unsigned
  payload=$1 actor=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-actor-verify.$$.json
  jq -Sc .actor "$payload" >"$actor"
  path=$(jq -er .path "$actor"); hash=$(jq -er .hash "$actor")
  path=$(terminal_file_under "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-actors" "$path") || { rm -f "$actor"; return 5; }
  [ "$(terminal_mode "$path")" = 400 ] && [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$path")" = "$hash" ] || {
    rm -f "$actor"; return 5; }
  jq -e --slurpfile actor "$actor" '
    (keys|sort)==["actor","generation","generation_token","pid","registered_at","start_token"] and
    .actor=="terminal-reducer" and .pid==$actor[0].pid and
    .start_token==$actor[0].start_token and .generation==$actor[0].generation and
    .generation_token==$actor[0].generation_token' "$path" >/dev/null || { rm -f "$actor"; return 5; }
  current=$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-actor-current.json
  [ -f "$current" ] && [ ! -L "$current" ] && [ "$(terminal_mode "$current")" = 600 ] &&
    jq -e --arg path "$path" --arg hash "$hash" --argjson generation "$(jq -r .generation "$actor")" '
      .path==$path and .hash==$hash and .generation==$generation' "$current" >/dev/null || {
        rm -f "$actor"; return 5; }
  pid=$(jq -r .pid "$actor"); start=$(jq -r .start_token "$actor")
  [ "$(runtime_start_token "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true)" = "$start" ] || { rm -f "$actor"; return 5; }
  actor_unsigned=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-actor-unsigned.$$.json
  jq -Sc 'del(.generation_token)' "$path" >"$actor_unsigned"
  "$TERMINAL_BIN/polylane-hmac.py" verify "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-auth.key" \
    "$actor_unsigned" "$(jq -r .generation_token "$path")" || {
      rm -f "$actor" "$actor_unsigned"; return 5; }
  rm -f "$actor" "$actor_unsigned"
}
terminal_validate_payload() {
  local state payload body request base signed
  state=$1 payload=$2
  jq -e '
    (keys|sort)==(["actor","auth_tag","body","body_hash",
      "expected_convergence_revision","expected_queue_revision",
      "expected_runtime_revision","kind","request","schema_version",
      "transaction_hash","transaction_id"]|sort) and .schema_version==2 and
    (.kind|IN("complete","waiting")) and
    (.transaction_id|type=="string" and test("^terminal:(complete|waiting):[0-9]+:[0-9a-f]{24}$")) and
    (.body_hash|test("^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$")) and
    (.transaction_hash|test("^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$")) and
    (.auth_tag|test("^hmac-sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$")) and
    (.expected_runtime_revision|type=="number" and floor==.) and
    (.expected_queue_revision|type=="number" and floor==.) and
    (.expected_convergence_revision|type=="number" and floor==.) and
    (.request|keys|sort)==["hash","path"] and
    (.request.hash|test("^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$"))' "$payload" >/dev/null || return 5
  body=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-body.$$.json
  jq -Sc .body "$payload" >"$body"
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$body")" = "$(jq -r .body_hash "$payload")" ] || {
    rm -f "$body"; return 5; }
  request=$(terminal_file_under "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-requests" "$(jq -r .request.path "$payload")") || {
    rm -f "$body"; return 5; }
  [ "$(terminal_mode "$request")" = 400 ] &&
    [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$request")" = "$(jq -r .request.hash "$payload")" ] &&
    cmp -s "$request" "$body" || { rm -f "$body"; return 5; }
  base=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-base.$$.json; signed=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-signed.$$.json
  jq -Sc 'del(.transaction_hash,.auth_tag)' "$payload" >"$base"
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$base")" = "$(jq -r .transaction_hash "$payload")" ] || {
    rm -f "$body" "$base"; return 5; }
  jq -Sc 'del(.auth_tag)' "$payload" >"$signed"
  "$TERMINAL_BIN/polylane-hmac.py" verify "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-auth.key" "$signed" \
    "$(jq -r .auth_tag "$payload")" || { rm -f "$body" "$base" "$signed"; return 5; }
  terminal_validate_actor "$payload" || { rm -f "$body" "$base" "$signed"; return 5; }
  rm -f "$body" "$base" "$signed"
}
terminal_validate_event() {
  local state event payload rc
  state=$1 event=$2
  jq -e '.actor=="terminal" and .type=="terminal_transaction"' "$event" >/dev/null || return 5
  payload=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-payload.$$.json
  jq -Sc .payload "$event" >"$payload"
  terminal_validate_payload "$state" "$payload"
  rc=$?; rm -f "$payload"; return "$rc"
}
terminal_validate_revisions() {
  local state event
  state=$1 event=$2
  jq -e --slurpfile event "$event" '
    .revision==$event[0].payload.expected_runtime_revision and
    (.queue.revision//0)==$event[0].payload.expected_queue_revision and
    (.convergence.transaction_revision//0)==
      $event[0].payload.expected_convergence_revision' "$state" >/dev/null
}
terminal_validate_complete() {
  local state event body project goal goal_hash commit delivery_tmp suggestion_path
  state=$1 event=$2
  body=$(jq -c .payload.body "$event")
  project=$(jq -er .project_root "$state") || return 5
  goal=$project/docs/polylane/max-state.json
  [ -f "$goal" ] && [ ! -L "$goal" ] || return 5
  goal_hash=$(jq -Sc . "$goal" | runtime_sha256_stdin)
  commit=$(git -C "$project" rev-parse HEAD) || return 5
  [ "$goal_hash" = "$(printf '%s\n' "$body" | jq -r .goal_state_hash)" ] &&
    [ "$commit" = "$(printf '%s\n' "$body" | jq -r .commit)" ] || return 5
  jq -e --arg goal "$goal_hash" --arg commit "$commit" --argjson body "$body" '
    ($body|keys|sort)==["certification_hashes","check_config_hash","commit","delivery",
      "delivery_hash","goal_state_hash","shutdown_token","suggestion_artifact","suggestion_hash"] and
    (.terminal==null) and .convergence.consecutive_clean>=2 and
    (.convergence.certifications[-2:]|length)==2 and
    ([.convergence.certifications[-2:].attempt_id]|unique|length)==2 and
    ([.convergence.certifications[-2:].certification_id]|unique|length)==2 and
    ([.convergence.certifications[-2:].checkout_id]|unique|length)==2 and
    ([.convergence.certifications[-2:].council.council_id]|unique|length)==2 and
    ([.convergence.certifications[-2:].check_config_hash]|unique|length)==1 and
    [.convergence.certifications[-2:].certification_hash]==$body.certification_hashes and
    all(.convergence.certifications[-2:];
      .commit==$commit and .goal_state_hash==$goal and
      .check_config_hash==$body.check_config_hash and .product_clean==true and
      (.findings|length)==0 and .council.mode=="exhaustive") and
    .convergence.finalization!=null and
    (.convergence.finalization.suggestions.suggestions|length)==30 and
    ([.convergence.finalization.suggestions.suggestions[].rank]|sort)==[range(1;31)] and
    ([.convergence.finalization.suggestions.suggestions[].id]|unique|length)==30 and
    all(.convergence.finalization.suggestions.suggestions[];
      .scope=="beyond_locked" and .execution=="informational" and .starts_new_run==true) and
    .convergence.finalization.suggestion_hash==$body.suggestion_hash and
    $body.suggestion_artifact.hash==$body.suggestion_hash and
    .convergence.finalization.delivery==$body.delivery and
    $body.delivery.content_hash==$body.suggestion_hash and
    $body.delivery.status=="pending_receipt" and
    ($body.delivery.require_receipt_before_shutdown//false)==false and
    ($body.delivery.receipt//null)==null' "$state" >/dev/null || return 5
  delivery_tmp=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-delivery.$$.json
  printf '%s\n' "$body" | jq -Sc .delivery >"$delivery_tmp"
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$delivery_tmp")" = "$(printf '%s\n' "$body" | jq -r .delivery_hash)" ] || {
    rm -f "$delivery_tmp"; return 5; }
  rm -f "$delivery_tmp"
  suggestion_path=$(printf '%s\n' "$body" | jq -r .suggestion_artifact.path)
  suggestion_path=$(terminal_file_under "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-suggestions" "$suggestion_path") || return 5
  [ "$(terminal_mode "$suggestion_path")" = 400 ] &&
    [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$suggestion_path")" = "$(printf '%s\n' "$body" | jq -r .suggestion_hash)" ] || return 5
  jq -e --slurpfile suggestions "$suggestion_path" '
    .convergence.finalization.suggestions==$suggestions[0] and
    ($suggestions[0].suggestions|length)==30 and
    ([$suggestions[0].suggestions[].rank]|sort)==[range(1;31)] and
    ([$suggestions[0].suggestions[].id]|unique|length)==30' "$state" >/dev/null || return 5
  "$TERMINAL_BIN/polylane-certify.sh" validate-complete-state \
    "$RUNTIME_DIR" "$state" "$goal" "$project"
}
terminal_validate_waiting() {
  local state event body request reason evidence_path evidence_hash condition_type source_actor
  local event_path max_age observed rc
  state=$1 event=$2 body=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-waiting-body.$$.json
  jq -Sc .payload.body "$event" >"$body"
  jq -e '
    (keys|sort)==["condition_evidence","request","request_hash"] and
    (.request|keys|sort)==["created_at","impact","loop_id","question","reason_code",
      "recommended_answer","request_id"] and
    (.request.request_id|type=="string" and length>0) and
    (.request.question|type=="string" and length>0)' "$body" >/dev/null || {
      rm -f "$body"; return 5; }
  request=$RUNTIME_DIR/.terminal-request.$$.json
  jq -Sc .request "$body" >"$request"
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$request")" = "$(jq -r .request_hash "$body")" ] || {
    rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
  reason=$(jq -r .reason_code "$request")
  jq -e --arg reason "$reason" '.allowed|index($reason)!=null' \
    "$TERMINAL_ROOT/config/polylane-input-reasons-v1.json" >/dev/null || {
      rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
  evidence_path=$(jq -r .condition_evidence.path "$body")
  evidence_hash=$(jq -r .condition_evidence.hash "$body")
  evidence_path=$(terminal_file_under "$RUNTIME_DIR/terminal-condition-evidence" "$evidence_path") || {
    rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
  [ "$(terminal_mode "$evidence_path")" = 400 ] &&
    [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$evidence_path")" = "$evidence_hash" ] || {
      rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
  condition_type=$(jq -er .condition_type "$evidence_path") || return 5
  source_actor=$(jq -er .source_actor "$evidence_path") || return 5
  jq -e --arg reason "$reason" --arg type "$condition_type" --arg actor "$source_actor" '
    any(.conditions[];.reason_code==$reason and .condition_type==$type and .actor==$actor)' \
    "$TERMINAL_ROOT/config/polylane-input-conditions-v1.json" >/dev/null || {
      rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
  event_path=$(jq -er .event.path "$evidence_path") || return 5
  case $event_path in "$RUNTIME_DIR"/events/*.json|"$RUNTIME_DIR"/pinned-events/*.json) :;; *) return 5;; esac
  [ -f "$event_path" ] && [ ! -L "$event_path" ] || return 5
  [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$event_path")" = "$(jq -r .event.file_hash "$evidence_path")" ] &&
    jq -e --slurpfile evidence "$evidence_path" --slurpfile request "$request" '
      .sequence==$evidence[0].event.id and .event_hash==$evidence[0].event.hash and
      .actor==$evidence[0].source_actor and .type=="input_condition_observed" and
      .at==$evidence[0].observed_at and
      .payload.loop_id==$request[0].loop_id and
      .payload.request_id==$request[0].request_id and
      .payload.reason_code==$request[0].reason_code and
      .payload.condition_type==$evidence[0].condition_type' "$event_path" >/dev/null || {
        rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
  max_age=$(jq -r .max_age_seconds "$TERMINAL_ROOT/config/polylane-input-conditions-v1.json")
  observed=$(jq -r .observed_at "$evidence_path")
  [ "$observed" -le "$(runtime_now)" ] && [ $(( $(runtime_now) - observed )) -le "$max_age" ] || {
    rm -f "$body" "$request"; return 5; }
    jq -e --slurpfile request "$request" '
      .terminal==null and .active_input==null and
      .loop_id==$request[0].loop_id and .shutdown==null' "$state" >/dev/null
  rc=$?; rm -f "$body" "$request"; return "$rc"
}
polylane_terminal_apply_event() {
  local state event output type id hash transaction_hash prior rc kind
  state=$1 event=$2 output=$3
  type=$(jq -r .type "$event")
  case $type in
    terminal_complete|terminal_waiting)
      terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" forbidden_direct_terminal_event
      return;;
    terminal_transaction) :;;
    *) cp "$state" "$output"; return;;
  esac
  jq -e '.actor=="terminal" and .type=="terminal_transaction" and
    (.payload.transaction_id|type=="string" and length>0) and
    (.payload.transaction_hash|test("^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$"))' "$event" >/dev/null || {
      terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" malformed_terminal_transaction; return; }
  id=$(jq -r .payload.transaction_id "$event")
  hash=$(jq -r .payload.body_hash "$event")
  transaction_hash=$(jq -r .payload.transaction_hash "$event")
  prior=$(jq -c --arg id "$id" '.terminal_transactions[$id]//null' "$state")
  if [ "$prior" != null ]; then
    [ "$(printf '%s\n' "$prior" | jq -r .transaction_hash)" = "$transaction_hash" ] || {
      terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" transaction_id_conflict; return; }
    jq --arg id "$id" --slurpfile e "$event" '
      .terminal_results[$id]={status:"applied",reason:"idempotent_replay",
        event_id:$e[0].sequence,event_hash:$e[0].event_hash} |
      .last_event_sequence=$e[0].sequence | .last_event_hash=$e[0].event_hash' \
      "$state" >"$output"
    return
  fi
  terminal_validate_event "$state" "$event" || {
    rc=$?; terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" "invalid_or_stale_terminal_transaction:$rc"; return; }
  terminal_validate_revisions "$state" "$event" || {
    terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" stale_terminal_revision; return; }
  kind=$(jq -r .payload.kind "$event")
  case $kind in
    complete) terminal_validate_complete "$state" "$event" || {
      terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" invalid_complete_evidence; return; };;
    waiting) terminal_validate_waiting "$state" "$event" || {
      terminal_reject "$state" "$event" "$output" invalid_waiting_request; return; };;
  esac
  jq --arg id "$id" --arg hash "$hash" --arg transaction_hash "$transaction_hash" \
    --arg kind "$kind" --slurpfile e "$event" '
    ($e[0]) as $x | ($x.payload) as $p |
    .terminal_transactions=(.terminal_transactions//{}) |
    .terminal_results=(.terminal_results//{}) |
    .terminal_transactions[$id]={kind:$kind,body_hash:$hash,
      request_hash:$p.request.hash,actor_hash:$p.actor.hash,
      transaction_hash:$transaction_hash,event_id:$x.sequence,event_hash:$x.event_hash} |
    .terminal_results[$id]={status:"applied",event_id:$x.sequence,event_hash:$x.event_hash} |
    if $kind=="complete" then
      .phase="COMPLETE" | .terminal="COMPLETE" |
      .shutdown={token:$p.body.shutdown_token,stage:"TERMINAL_PUBLISHED"} |
      .terminal_evidence={certification_hashes:$p.body.certification_hashes,
        suggestion_hash:$p.body.suggestion_hash,
        suggestion_artifact:$p.body.suggestion_artifact,delivery_hash:$p.body.delivery_hash,
        delivery:$p.body.delivery}
    else
      .phase="WAITING_FOR_USER" | .terminal="WAITING_FOR_USER" |
      .active_input=$p.body.request
    end |
    .last_event_sequence=$x.sequence | .last_event_hash=$x.event_hash' "$state" >"$output"
}
polylane_terminal_apply_control_event() {
  local state event output
  state=$1 event=$2 output=$3
  jq --slurpfile e "$event" '
    ($e[0]) as $x | ($x.payload) as $p |
    if $x.type=="input_pause" then
      if .terminal=="WAITING_FOR_USER" and .active_input.request_id==$p.request_id then
        .queue.items |= map(if (.status=="starting" or .status=="running") then
          .status="scheduled" | .next_action_at=$p.paused_at | .claim=null |
          .last_error={reason:"input_pause",retry_at:$p.paused_at} else . end) |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller_paused=true | .input_pause=$p
      else . end
    elif $x.type=="input_answer" then
      if .active_input.request_id==$p.request_id then
        .phase="WORKING" | .terminal=null | .active_input=null |
        .controller_paused=false | .input_history=((.input_history // []) + [$p])
      else . end
    elif $x.type=="shutdown_advance" then
      ["TERMINAL_PUBLISHED","CLAIMS_DRAINED","ACTORS_FENCED","HOOK_DISARMED",
       "FINALIZER_ARMED","SESSION_CLOSED","TEARDOWN_COMPLETE"] as $stages | .shutdown.stage as $current |
      if .shutdown.token!=$p.shutdown_token then .
      elif .shutdown.stage==$p.stage then .
      elif ($stages|index($p.expected_stage))==($stages|index($current)) and
           ($stages|index($p.stage))==(($stages|index($current))+1)
      then .shutdown.stage=$p.stage else . end
    elif $x.type=="cycle_stage" then
      if (.controller.stage_revision // 0)==$p.expected_stage_revision then
        .controller.stage=$p.stage | .controller.stage_revision=($p.expected_stage_revision+1) |
        .controller.stage_key=$p.idempotency_key | .controller.expected_base=$p.expected_base
      else . end
    else . end' "$state" >"$output"
}
```

Create `core/scripts/lib/polylane-controller-reducer.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
polylane_controller_apply_event() {
  local state event output
  state=$1 event=$2 output=$3
  jq --slurpfile e "$event" '
    ($e[0]) as $x | ($x.payload) as $p |
    .controller=(.controller // {stage:"PLANNED",stage_revision:0,cycle:1,run_id:null,
      pending_turn:null,applied_transactions:[],last_conflict:null,evidence:{}}) |
    .queue=(.queue // {revision:0,provider_state:"available",items:[]}) |
    if $x.type=="controller_turn_start" then
      if .controller.pending_turn!=null and .controller.pending_turn.turn_id==$p.turn_id then .
      elif .controller.pending_turn==null and .revision==$p.expected_runtime_revision and
           .controller.stage=="PLANNED" then .controller.pending_turn=$p
      else .controller.last_conflict={type:"turn_start",turn_id:$p.turn_id,event_sequence:$x.sequence} end
    elif $x.type=="controller_transaction" then
      if (.controller.applied_transactions|index($p.transaction_id))!=null then .
      # Guardian/lease heartbeats advance the global revision while a controller compiles
      # its immutable policy. Controller CAS is therefore guarded by its own stage revision,
      # stage, pending turn, and (where mutated) queue revision—not unrelated heartbeats.
      elif .controller.stage_revision!=$p.expected_stage_revision or
           .controller.stage!=$p.expected_stage then
        .controller.last_conflict={type:"revision_or_stage",transaction_id:$p.transaction_id,
          event_sequence:$x.sequence,expected_runtime_revision:$p.expected_runtime_revision,
          actual_runtime_revision:.revision,expected_stage:$p.expected_stage,actual_stage:.controller.stage}
      elif $p.kind=="plan_cycle" and .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           .controller.pending_turn.turn_id==$p.turn_id and
           ((.certification_policy // null)==null or .certification_policy==$p.certification_policy) and
           (any(.queue.items[];.id==$p.queue_item.id)|not) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_item + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller.stage="LANES_RUNNING" |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.run_id=$p.run_id |
        .controller.manifest=$p.manifest | .controller.pending_turn=null |
        .certification_policy=(.certification_policy // $p.certification_policy) |
        .controller.recovery_item_id=null |
        .controller.evidence.plan=$p.evidence |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="reject_proposal" and .controller.pending_turn.turn_id==$p.turn_id then
        .controller.pending_turn=null |
        .controller.proposal_rejections=((.controller.proposal_rejections // []) +
          [{turn_id:$p.turn_id,reason_code:$p.reason_code,evidence:$p.evidence,event_sequence:$x.sequence}]) |
        .controller.proposal_rejections=(.controller.proposal_rejections[-8:]) |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="advance_stage" and
        (["LANES_RUNNING:INTEGRATING","INTEGRATING:PROMOTION_PREPARED",
          "PROMOTION_PREPARED:PROMOTED","PROMOTED:RESULT_RECORDED",
          "RESULT_RECORDED:CLEANUP"] | index($p.expected_stage+":"+$p.next_stage))!=null then
        .controller.stage=$p.next_stage | .controller.stage_revision += 1 |
        .controller.evidence[$p.next_stage]=$p.evidence |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="enqueue_followups" and $p.expected_stage=="CLEANUP" and
           .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           (([.queue.items[].id]+[$p.queue_items[].id]) as $ids |
             ($ids|length)==($ids|unique|length)) and
           (([$p.queue_items[].id]|sort)==(($p.critical_ids+$p.sidecar_ids)|sort)) and
           (($p.critical_ids+$p.sidecar_ids|length)==($p.critical_ids+$p.sidecar_ids|unique|length)) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_items[] + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller.stage="NEXT_ENQUEUED" |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.followup_ids=$p.critical_ids |
        .controller.sidecar_ids=((.controller.sidecar_ids // [])+$p.sidecar_ids|unique) |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="route_council" and $p.expected_stage=="NEXT_ENQUEUED" and
           ($p.council.verdict!="GO" or $p.council.mode=="exhaustive") and
           .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           all(.queue.items[];.id!=$p.queue_item.id) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_item + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller.stage="COUNCIL_ROUTED" |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.council=$p.council |
        .controller.followup_ids=[$p.queue_item.id] |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="escalate_council" and $p.expected_stage=="NEXT_ENQUEUED" and
           .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           all(.queue.items[];.id!=$p.queue_item.id) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_item + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller.stage="EXHAUSTIVE_COUNCIL" |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.followup_ids=[$p.queue_item.id] |
        .controller.fast_council=$p.fast_council |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="route_council" and $p.expected_stage=="EXHAUSTIVE_COUNCIL" and
           $p.council.mode=="exhaustive" and .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           all(.queue.items[];.id!=$p.queue_item.id) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_item + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller.stage="COUNCIL_ROUTED" |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.council=$p.council |
        .controller.followup_ids=[$p.queue_item.id] |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="enqueue_council_successor" and $p.expected_stage=="COUNCIL_ROUTED" and
           .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           (. as $state | all($p.queue_items[];
             . as $new | all($state.queue.items[];.id!=$new.id))) and
           (([$p.queue_items[].id]|sort)==(($p.critical_ids+$p.sidecar_ids)|sort)) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_items[] + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 |
        .controller.stage=(if ($p.critical_ids|length)==0 then "PLANNED" else "SUCCESSOR_ENQUEUED" end) |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.followup_ids=$p.critical_ids |
        .controller.sidecar_ids=((.controller.sidecar_ids // [])+$p.sidecar_ids|unique) |
        .controller.next_goal_ref=$p.next_goal_ref |
        .controller.cycle+=(if ($p.critical_ids|length)==0 then 1 else 0 end) |
        .controller.run_id=(if ($p.critical_ids|length)==0 then null else .controller.run_id end) |
        .controller.manifest=(if ($p.critical_ids|length)==0 then null else .controller.manifest end) |
        .controller.council=(if ($p.critical_ids|length)==0 then null else .controller.council end) |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="open_next_cycle" and $p.expected_stage=="SUCCESSOR_ENQUEUED" then
        .controller.stage="PLANNED" | .controller.stage_revision += 1 |
        .controller.cycle += 1 | .controller.run_id=null | .controller.manifest=null |
        .controller.followup_ids=[] | .controller.council=null |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      elif $p.kind=="route_recovery" and .queue.revision==$p.expected_queue_revision and
           all(.queue.items[];.id!=$p.queue_item.id) then
        .queue.items += [$p.queue_item + {created_sequence:$x.sequence}] |
        .queue.revision += 1 | .controller.stage="PLANNED" |
        .controller.stage_revision += 1 | .controller.run_id=null | .controller.manifest=null |
        .controller.pending_turn=null | .controller.recovery_item_id=$p.queue_item.id |
        .controller.applied_transactions += [$p.transaction_id]
      else .controller.last_conflict={type:"invalid_effect",transaction_id:$p.transaction_id,
        event_sequence:$x.sequence} end
    else . end' "$state" >"$output"
}
```

```diff
@@
   case $(jq -r '.type' "$event") in

+      rm -f "$output.typed"
+      return
+      ;;
```

Create `core/scripts/polylane-terminal.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
umask 077
BIN=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd -P)
ROOT=$(cd "$BIN/.." && pwd -P)
RUNTIME=$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh
. "$BIN/lib/polylane-runtime-common.sh"
HMAC=$BIN/polylane-hmac.py
atomic_json() { destination=$1; temporary=$destination.tmp.$$; cat >"$temporary"; jq -e . "$temporary" >/dev/null; chmod 600 "$temporary"; mv -f "$temporary" "$destination"; }
runtime_for_state() { project=$(jq -er '.project_root' "$1"); printf '%s/.polylane/runtime' "$project"; }
seal_terminal_json() {
  root=$1 source=$2
  [ -d "$root" ] && [ ! -L "$root" ] || return 5
  canonical=$root/.seal.$$.json; jq -Sc . "$source" >"$canonical"
  hash=$(runtime_sha256 "$canonical"); destination=$root/${hash#sha256:}.json
  if [ -e "$destination" ]; then
    [ -f "$destination" ] && [ ! -L "$destination" ] &&
      [ "$(runtime_sha256 "$destination")" = "$hash" ] || { rm -f "$canonical"; return 5; }
    rm -f "$canonical"
  else chmod 400 "$canonical"; mv -f "$canonical" "$destination"; fi
  printf '%s\t%s\n' "$destination" "$hash"
}
register_terminal_actor() {
  runtime=$1; key=$runtime/terminal-auth.key
  [ -f "$key" ] && [ ! -L "$key" ] || return 5
  generation=$(( $(cat "$runtime/terminal-actor-generation") + 1 ))
  temporary=$runtime/.terminal-generation.$$
  printf '%s\n' "$generation" >"$temporary"; chmod 600 "$temporary"
  mv -f "$temporary" "$runtime/terminal-actor-generation"
  actor_base=$runtime/.terminal-actor-base.$$.json
  jq -Scn --argjson pid "$$" --arg start_token "$(runtime_start_token $$)" \
    --argjson generation "$generation" --argjson registered_at "$(runtime_now)" \
    '{actor:"terminal-reducer",pid:$pid,start_token:$start_token,generation:$generation,
      registered_at:$registered_at}' >"$actor_base"
  generation_token=$("$HMAC" sign "$key" "$actor_base")
  actor_source=$runtime/.terminal-actor-source.$$.json
  jq -Sc --arg generation_token "$generation_token" \
    '.+{generation_token:$generation_token}' "$actor_base" >"$actor_source"
  IFS=$'\t' read -r TERMINAL_ACTOR_PATH TERMINAL_ACTOR_HASH <<EOF
$(seal_terminal_json "$runtime/terminal-actors" "$actor_source")
EOF
  current=$runtime/.terminal-actor-current.$$.json
  jq -Scn --arg path "$TERMINAL_ACTOR_PATH" --arg hash "$TERMINAL_ACTOR_HASH" \
    --argjson generation "$generation" '{path:$path,hash:$hash,generation:$generation}' >"$current"
  chmod 600 "$current"; mv -f "$current" "$runtime/terminal-actor-current.json"
  TERMINAL_ACTOR_GENERATION=$generation
  rm -f "$actor_base" "$actor_source"
}
emit_control_payload() {
  state=$1 type=$2 action=$3 payload=$4; runtime=$(runtime_for_state "$state")
  "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" emit terminal "$type" "$action" --payload-file "$payload"
  "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" wake
}
submit_terminal() {
  state_file=$1 kind=$2 body=$3; runtime=$(runtime_for_state "$state_file")
  RUNTIME_DIR=$runtime
  actor_lock=$runtime/terminal-actor.lock
  runtime_lock_acquire "$actor_lock" || return $?
  trap 'runtime_lock_release "$actor_lock" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true' RETURN
  "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" reduce
  snapshot=$runtime/current/state.json
  IFS=$'\t' read -r request_path request_hash <<EOF
$(seal_terminal_json "$runtime/terminal-requests" "$body")
EOF
  body_hash=$request_hash
  register_terminal_actor "$runtime" || return 5
  actor_ref=$runtime/.terminal-actor-ref.$$.json
  jq -Scn --arg path "$TERMINAL_ACTOR_PATH" --arg hash "$TERMINAL_ACTOR_HASH" \
    --slurpfile actor "$TERMINAL_ACTOR_PATH" '$actor[0]+{path:$path,hash:$hash}' >"$actor_ref"
  id="terminal:$kind:$TERMINAL_ACTOR_GENERATION:$(printf '%s' "$body_hash" | cut -c8-31)"
  staging=$runtime/terminal-staging; [ -d "$staging" ] && [ ! -L "$staging" ] || return 5
  base=$staging/.transaction-base.$$.json
  jq -Scn --arg id "$id" --arg kind "$kind" --arg hash "$body_hash" \
    --argjson runtime_revision "$(jq -r .revision "$snapshot")" \
    --argjson queue_revision "$(jq -r '.queue.revision//0' "$snapshot")" \
    --argjson convergence_revision "$(jq -r '.convergence.transaction_revision//0' "$snapshot")" \
    --arg request_path "$request_path" --arg request_hash "$request_hash" \
    --slurpfile actor "$actor_ref" --slurpfile body "$body" '
    {schema_version:2,transaction_id:$id,kind:$kind,
      expected_runtime_revision:$runtime_revision,expected_queue_revision:$queue_revision,
      expected_convergence_revision:$convergence_revision,body_hash:$hash,
      request:{path:$request_path,hash:$request_hash},actor:$actor[0],body:$body[0]}' >"$base"
  transaction_hash=$(runtime_sha256 "$base")
  signed=$staging/.transaction-signed.$$.json
  jq -Sc --arg transaction_hash "$transaction_hash" \
    '.+{transaction_hash:$transaction_hash}' "$base" >"$signed"
  auth_tag=$("$HMAC" sign "$runtime/terminal-auth.key" "$signed")
  temporary=$staging/.transaction.$$.json
  jq -Sc --arg auth_tag "$auth_tag" '.+{auth_tag:$auth_tag}' "$signed" >"$temporary"
  destination=$staging/${transaction_hash#sha256:}.json
  chmod 400 "$temporary"
  if [ -e "$destination" ]; then
    [ -f "$destination" ] && [ ! -L "$destination" ] &&
      cmp -s "$temporary" "$destination" || { rm -f "$temporary"; return 7; }
    rm -f "$temporary"
  else
    ln "$temporary" "$destination" || { rm -f "$temporary"; return 5; }
    rm -f "$temporary"
  fi
  rm -f "$base" "$signed" "$actor_ref"
  "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" terminal-transaction "$destination"
  "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" wake
  runtime_lock_release "$actor_lock"; trap - RETURN
}
validate_request() {
  state=$1 request=$2
  loop=$(jq -er '.loop_id' "$state")
  jq -e --arg loop "$loop" 'type=="object" and .loop_id==$loop and
    (.reason_code|type=="string") and (.question|type=="string" and length>0) and
    (.recommended_answer|type=="string" and length>0) and (.impact|type=="string" and length>0) and
    (.request_id|type=="string" and length>0) and (.created_at|type=="number")' "$request" >/dev/null || return 5
  reason=$(jq -r '.reason_code' "$request")
  jq -e --arg reason "$reason" '.allowed|index($reason)!=null' "$ROOT/config/polylane-input-reasons-v1.json" >/dev/null || return 5
}
case ${1:-} in
  request) exit 5 ;;
  needs-input)
    [ "$#" -eq 4 ] || exit 2
    state=$2 request=$3 evidence=$4; validate_request "$state" "$request"
    jq -e --arg loop "$(jq -r .loop_id "$request")" --arg request "$(jq -r .request_id "$request")" \
      --arg reason "$(jq -r .reason_code "$request")" '
      (keys|sort)==["condition_type","event","loop_id","observed_at","reason_code",
        "request_id","schema_version","source_actor"] and .schema_version==1 and
      .loop_id==$loop and .request_id==$request and .reason_code==$reason and
      (.condition_type|type=="string" and length>0) and
      (.source_actor|type=="string" and length>0) and
      (.observed_at|type=="number" and floor==.) and
      (.event|keys|sort)==["file_hash","hash","id","path"]' "$evidence" >/dev/null || exit 5
    runtime=$(runtime_for_state "$state"); RUNTIME_DIR=$runtime
    IFS=$'\t' read -r evidence_path evidence_hash <<EOF
$(seal_terminal_json "$runtime/terminal-condition-evidence" "$evidence")
EOF
    body=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-input.XXXXXX")
    request_hash=$(jq -Sc . "$request" | if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      sha256sum | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; else shasum -a 256 | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; fi)
    jq -n --arg hash "$request_hash" --arg evidence_path "$evidence_path" \
      --arg evidence_hash "$evidence_hash" --slurpfile request "$request" \
      '{request:$request[0],request_hash:$hash,
        condition_evidence:{path:$evidence_path,hash:$evidence_hash}}' >"$body"
    submit_terminal "$state" waiting "$body"
    rm -f "$body"
    ;;
  answer)
    state=$2 request=$3 answer=$4
    project=$(jq -er '.project_root' "$state")
    request_id=$(jq -r '.request_id' "$answer")
    history=$project/docs/polylane/input-history/$request_id.json
    if [ ! -f "$request" ]; then [ -f "$history" ] && exit 0; exit 5; fi
    validate_request "$state" "$request"
    loop=$(jq -r '.loop_id' "$request")
    jq -e --arg loop "$loop" --arg id "$(jq -r '.request_id' "$request")" '
      .loop_id==$loop and .request_id==$id and (.answer|type=="string" and length>0) and (.answered_at|type=="number")' "$answer" >/dev/null || exit 5
    payload=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-answer.XXXXXX")
    jq -n --slurpfile request "$request" --slurpfile answer "$answer" \
      '{loop_id:$request[0].loop_id,request_id:$request[0].request_id,request:$request[0],answer:$answer[0]}' >"$payload"
    emit_control_payload "$state" input_answer "$request_id" "$payload"
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$(runtime_for_state "$state")" reduce
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$history")"
    cat "$payload" | atomic_json "$history"
    rm -f "$request" "$payload"
    goal=$project/docs/polylane/resume-goal.json
    jq -n --arg loop "$loop" '{loop_id:$loop,complete:false,next_goal_ref:"input-resume"}' >"$goal"
    "$BIN/polylane-queue.sh" --dir "$(runtime_for_state "$state")" ensure-progress "$goal"
    ;;
  complete)
    state=$2 perfection=$3 suggestions=$4 delivery=$5
    [ -x "$BIN/polylane-certify.sh" ] && [ -x "$BIN/polylane-suggestions.sh" ] || exit 5
    project=$(jq -er '.project_root' "$state")
    runtime=$(runtime_for_state "$state")
    "$BIN/polylane-certify.sh" repair "$runtime" "$project"
    "$BIN/polylane-certify.sh" complete "$runtime" "$project/docs/polylane/max-state.json" "$project"
    "$BIN/polylane-suggestions.sh" validate-final "$suggestions" "$(jq -r .loop_id "$state")"
    "$RUNTIME" --dir "$runtime" reduce
    snapshot=$runtime/current/state.json
    RUNTIME_DIR=$runtime
    IFS=$'\t' read -r suggestion_path suggestion_hash <<EOF
$(seal_terminal_json "$runtime/terminal-suggestions" "$suggestions")
EOF
    delivery_hash=$(jq -Sc . "$delivery" | if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      sha256sum | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; else shasum -a 256 | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; fi)
    jq -e --arg suggestion "$suggestion_hash" --argjson delivery "$(jq -c . "$delivery")" '
      .convergence.finalization.suggestion_hash==$suggestion and
      .convergence.finalization.delivery==$delivery' "$snapshot" >/dev/null || exit 5
    goal_hash=$(jq -Sc . "$project/docs/polylane/max-state.json" | if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      sha256sum | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; else shasum -a 256 | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'; fi)
    commit=$(git -C "$project" rev-parse HEAD)
    check_config=$(jq -r '.convergence.certifications[-1].check_config_hash' "$snapshot")
    certs=$(jq -c '[.convergence.certifications[-2:].certification_hash]' "$snapshot")
    token=$(printf '%s\n%s\n%s\n' "$(jq -r .loop_id "$snapshot")" "$commit" \
      "$suggestion_hash" | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}')
    body=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-complete.XXXXXX")
    jq -n --arg token "$token" --arg goal "$goal_hash" --arg commit "$commit" \
      --arg config "$check_config" --argjson certs "$certs" --arg suggestion "$suggestion_hash" \
      --arg suggestion_path "$suggestion_path" --arg delivery_hash "$delivery_hash" \
      --slurpfile delivery "$delivery" '
      {shutdown_token:$token,goal_state_hash:$goal,commit:$commit,
        check_config_hash:$config,certification_hashes:$certs,suggestion_hash:$suggestion,
        suggestion_artifact:{path:$suggestion_path,hash:$suggestion},
        delivery_hash:$delivery_hash,delivery:$delivery[0]}' >"$body"
    submit_terminal "$state" complete "$body"
    rm -f "$body"
    ;;
  *) exit 2 ;;
esac
```

```bash
bash -n core/scripts/lib/polylane-terminal-reducer.sh
bash -n core/scripts/polylane-terminal.sh
```

- [ ] **Action 4.6 (2–5 minutes): Add actor identity adoption and fencing**

Create `core/scripts/polylane-process.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
start_token() {
  process_state=$(ps -o stat= -p "$1" 2>/dev/null) || return 1
  case $process_state in *Z*) return 1;; esac
  row=$(ps -o lstart= -p "$1" 2>/dev/null) || return 1
  [ -n "$(printf '%s' "$row" | tr -d '[:space:]')" ] || return 1
  printf '%s\n' "$row" | cksum | awk '{print $1":"$2}'
}
actor_file() { printf '%s/actor-%s.json' "$1" "$2"; }
signal_identity() {
  pid=$1 token=$2 signal=$3
  [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" = "$token" ] || return 0
  kill -"$signal" "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
}
descendant_snapshot() {
  root=$1; table=$(ps -axo pid=,ppid=)
  frontier=("$root"); depths=(0); seen=" $root "; index=0
  while [ "$index" -lt "${#frontier[@]}" ]; do
    parent=${frontier[$index]}; depth=${depths[$index]}; index=$((index + 1))
    while IFS= read -r child; do
      [ -n "$child" ] || continue
      case $seen in *" $child "*) continue;; esac
      seen="$seen$child "; child_depth=$((depth + 1)); token=$(start_token "$child" || true)
      [ -z "$token" ] || printf '%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$child_depth" "$child" "$token"
      frontier[${#frontier[@]}]=$child; depths[${#depths[@]}]=$child_depth
    done < <(printf '%s\n' "$table" | awk -v parent="$parent" '$2==parent {print $1}')
  done
}
fence_pid_tree() {
  pid=$1 recorded=$2
  [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" = "$recorded" ] || return 0
  # Freeze the root first, then repeatedly discover-and-freeze descendants until the exact
  # PID/start-token set is stable. A frozen member cannot fork after it enters the snapshot.
  signal_identity "$pid" "$recorded" STOP
  snapshot=$(printf '0\t%s\t%s\n' "$pid" "$recorded")
  stable=0
  for ignored in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    discovered=$(descendant_snapshot "$pid" || true)
    while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth child child_token; do
      [ -n "$child" ] || continue
      signal_identity "$child" "$child_token" STOP
    done < <(printf '%s\n' "$discovered")
    merged=$(printf '%s\n%s\n' "$snapshot" "$discovered" | sed '/^$/d' | LC_ALL=C sort -u)
    if [ "$merged" = "$snapshot" ]; then stable=1; break; fi
    snapshot=$merged
  done
  [ "$stable" -eq 1 ] || return 75
  # Every process we STOPped is resumed before TERM. This includes a root that was already
  # STOPped by the fault under repair, preventing an unkillable frozen survivor.
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth member member_token; do
    [ -n "$member" ] || continue
    signal_identity "$member" "$member_token" CONT
  done < <(printf '%s\n' "$snapshot" | LC_ALL=C sort -t $'\t' -k1,1nr -k2,2nr)
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth member member_token; do
    [ -n "$member" ] || continue
    signal_identity "$member" "$member_token" TERM
  done < <(printf '%s\n' "$snapshot" | LC_ALL=C sort -t $'\t' -k1,1nr -k2,2nr)
  for ignored in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    live=0
    while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth member member_token; do
      [ "$(start_token "$member" || true)" != "$member_token" ] || live=1
    done < <(printf '%s\n' "$snapshot")
    [ "$live" -eq 0 ] && break
    sleep 0.1
  done
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth member member_token; do
    [ -n "$member" ] || continue
    [ "$(start_token "$member" || true)" != "$member_token" ] ||
      signal_identity "$member" "$member_token" KILL
  done < <(printf '%s\n' "$snapshot" | LC_ALL=C sort -t $'\t' -k1,1nr -k2,2nr)
  for ignored in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    live=0
    while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth member member_token; do
      [ "$(start_token "$member" || true)" != "$member_token" ] || live=1
    done < <(printf '%s\n' "$snapshot")
    [ "$live" -eq 0 ] && break
    sleep 0.1
  done
  while IFS=$'\t' read -r depth member member_token; do
    [ "$(start_token "$member" || true)" != "$member_token" ] || return 75
  done < <(printf '%s\n' "$snapshot")
  [ -z "$(descendant_snapshot "$pid" || true)" ] || return 75
}
case ${1:-} in
  assert-current)
    runtime=$2 actor=$3 expected=$4 file=$(actor_file "$runtime" "$actor")
    [ -f "$file" ] || exit 6
    actual=$(jq -er '.generation_token' "$file") || exit 6; pid=$(jq -er .pid "$file"); token=$(jq -er .start_token "$file")
    [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && [ "$(jq -r .lease_valid "$file")" = true ] &&
      [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" = "$token" ] || exit 6
    ;;
  adopt-or-fence)
    runtime=$2 actor=$3 expected=$4 file=$(actor_file "$runtime" "$actor")
    [ -f "$file" ] || exit 6
    pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$file"); recorded=$(jq -r '.start_token' "$file")
    actual=$(start_token "$pid" || true); generation=$(jq -r '.generation_token' "$file")
    valid=$(jq -r '.lease_valid' "$file")
    if [ "$expected" != shutdown ] && [ -n "$actual" ] && [ "$actual" = "$recorded" ] && [ "$generation" = "$expected" ] && [ "$valid" = true ]; then exit 0; fi
    # Freeze the root before discovery so it cannot create an unrecorded descendant during
    # fencing. Discover the live tree at fence time and signal deepest-first.
    fence_pid_tree "$pid" "$recorded"
    jq '.lease_valid=false | .generation_token="fenced"' "$file" >"$file.tmp.$$" && mv -f "$file.tmp.$$" "$file"
    ;;
  fence-identity)
    runtime=$2 identity=$3 expected_hash=$4
    case $identity in "$runtime"/lane-identities/*.json) :;; *) exit 5;; esac
    [ -f "$identity" ] && [ ! -L "$identity" ] &&
      [ "$(stat -f '%Lp' "$identity" 2>/dev/null || stat -c '%a' "$identity")" = 400 ] || exit 5
    if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then actual_hash=$(sha256sum "$identity" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
    else actual_hash=$(shasum -a 256 "$identity" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'); fi
    [ "$actual_hash" = "$expected_hash" ] || exit 5
    jq -e --slurpfile state "$runtime/current/state.json" '
      (keys|sort)==["cycle","generation","lane_id","loop_id","pid","role","run_id","schema_version","start_token"] and
      .schema_version==1 and .role=="builder" and (.generation|type=="number" and .>=1) and
      (.pid|type=="number" and .>1) and (.start_token|type=="string" and length>0) and
      .loop_id==$state[0].loop_id and .run_id==$state[0].controller.run_id and
      .cycle==$state[0].controller.cycle' "$identity" >/dev/null || exit 5
    pid=$(jq -r .pid "$identity"); recorded=$(jq -r .start_token "$identity")
    [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" = "$recorded" ] || exit 6
    fence_pid_tree "$pid" "$recorded"
    ;;
  fence-sealed-identity)
    runtime=$2 identity=$3 expected_hash=$4
    root=$runtime/shutdown-identities
    [ -d "$root" ] && [ ! -L "$root" ] || exit 5
    parent=$(cd "$(dirname "$identity")" && pwd -P) || exit 5
    [ "$parent" = "$(cd "$root" && pwd -P)" ] && [ -f "$identity" ] &&
      [ ! -L "$identity" ] || exit 5
    [ "$(stat -f '%Lp' "$identity" 2>/dev/null || stat -c '%a' "$identity")" = 400 ] || exit 5
    if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      actual_hash=$(sha256sum "$identity" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
    else actual_hash=$(shasum -a 256 "$identity" | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}'); fi
    [ "$actual_hash" = "$expected_hash" ] &&
      [ "$(basename "$identity" .json)" = "${expected_hash#sha256:}" ] || exit 5
    jq -e '(keys|sort)==["pid","role","source","start_token"] and
      (.role|IN("keeper","sentinel","guardian","controller","worker","lane","pane","finalizer")) and
      (.pid|type=="number" and .>1) and (.start_token|type=="string" and length>0) and
      (.source|type=="string" and startswith("/"))' "$identity" >/dev/null || exit 5
    pid=$(jq -r .pid "$identity"); recorded=$(jq -r .start_token "$identity")
    [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" != "$recorded" ] || fence_pid_tree "$pid" "$recorded"
    [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" != "$recorded" ] || exit 75
    ;;
  verify-absent)
    runtime=$2; failed=0
    for file in "$runtime"/actor-*.json; do
      [ -f "$file" ] || continue
      pid=$(jq -r .pid "$file"); token=$(jq -r .start_token "$file")
      [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" != "$token" ] || { printf '%s\n' "$file" >&2; failed=1; }
      while IFS=$'\t' read -r child child_token; do
        [ "$(start_token "$child" || true)" != "$child_token" ] || { printf '%s:%s\n' "$file" "$child" >&2; failed=1; }
      done < <(jq -r '.descendants[]?|[.pid,.start_token]|@tsv' "$file")
    done
    for file in "$runtime/keeper.lock/owner.json" "$runtime/sentinel-heartbeat.json"; do
      [ -f "$file" ] && [ ! -L "$file" ] || continue
      pid=$(jq -r '.pid//0' "$file"); token=$(jq -r '.start_token//""' "$file")
      [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" != "$token" ] || {
        printf '%s\n' "$file" >&2; failed=1; }
    done
    manifest=$runtime/shutdown-identity-manifest.json
    if [ -f "$manifest" ] && [ ! -L "$manifest" ]; then
      while IFS=$'\t' read -r pid token role; do
        [ "$(start_token "$pid" || true)" != "$token" ] || {
          printf '%s:%s\n' "$role" "$pid" >&2; failed=1; }
      done < <(jq -r '.identities[]|[.pid,.start_token,.role]|@tsv' "$manifest")
      self_pid=$(jq -r '.finalizer_handoff.pid//0' "$manifest")
      self_token=$(jq -r '.finalizer_handoff.start_token//""' "$manifest")
      [ "$(start_token "$self_pid" || true)" != "$self_token" ] || {
        printf 'finalizer-handoff:%s\n' "$self_pid" >&2; failed=1; }
    fi
    [ "$failed" -eq 0 ] || exit 75
    ;;
  *) exit 2 ;;
esac
```
