reconcile_item() {
  local output=$1 generation=$2
  jq -n --argjson generation "$generation" '
    {schema_version:1,id:("reconcile-"+($generation|tostring)),kind:"state_reconcile",
      mode:"offline",status:"ready",priority:100,goal_ref:"goal-reconcile",
      args:{goal_ref:"goal-reconcile",reconcile_generation:$generation},depends_on:[],
      next_action_at:10,deadline_at:310,attempt:1,
      strategy_hash:("strategy-"+($generation|tostring)),ownership_globs:[],
      resource_locks:["runtime:reducer"],claim:null,last_error:null}' >"$output"
}
reset_case state_reconcile
item=$runtime/reconcile-item.json; reconcile_item "$item" 1
enqueue_item "$item"
jq -e 'any(.queue.items[];.id=="reconcile-1" and
  .args.reconcile_generation==1)' "$runtime/current/state.json" >/dev/null ||
  fail reconcile-production-reducer
[ "$(jq '.audit.recovery_pairs|length' "$runtime/current/state.json")" -eq 1 ] ||
  fail reconcile-production-recovery

reset_case state_reconcile
item=$runtime/reconcile-item.json; reconcile_item "$item" 1
FIXTURE_STALE=1
expect_rc 5 enqueue_item "$item"
unset FIXTURE_STALE
provider_negative_clean reconcile-stale

replay_outputs() {
  local recovery=$1 destination=$2 observation=$runtime/replay-observation.json
  mkdir -p "$runtime/snapshots/1"
  cp "$runtime/current/state.json" "$runtime/snapshots/1/state.json"
  if [ "$recovery" = event_replay ]; then
    replay_payload=$runtime/replay-source.json; jq -n '{source:"real-reducer"}' >"$replay_payload"
    publish_event controller replay_source replay-source "$replay_payload"
    runtime_reduce
  fi
  capture_replay_observation "$runtime" "$runtime/current/state.json" "$observation"
  jq -n --slurpfile observation "$observation" \
    '{replay_observation:$observation[0]}' >"$destination"
}
reset_case event_replay
outputs=$runtime/outputs.json; replay_outputs event_replay "$outputs"
close_native recovery event_replay recovery recovery recovery_action q1 "$outputs"
[ "$(jq '.audit.recovery_pairs|length' "$runtime/current/state.json")" -eq 1 ] ||
  fail replay-production-positive

reset_case boundary_reconcile
outputs=$runtime/outputs.json; replay_outputs boundary_reconcile "$outputs"
close_native recovery boundary_reconcile recovery recovery recovery_action q1 "$outputs"
[ "$(jq '.audit.recovery_pairs|length' "$runtime/current/state.json")" -eq 1 ] ||
  fail boundary-zero-production-positive

reset_case event_replay
outputs=$runtime/outputs.json; replay_outputs event_replay "$outputs"
jq 'del(.replay_observation.replayed_event_hash)' "$outputs" >"$outputs.bad"
mv -f "$outputs.bad" "$outputs"
expect_rc 5 close_native recovery event_replay recovery recovery recovery_action q1 "$outputs"
provider_negative_clean replay-schema

FIXTURE_TAMPER_OUTPUT_NAME=replay_observation
expect_rc 5 close_native recovery event_replay recovery recovery recovery_action q1 "$outputs"

reset_case event_replay

FIXTURE_STALE=1

jq -e 'any(.audit.recovery_results[];.status=="rejected" and
  .reason=="conflicting_recovery_evidence") and (.audit.recovery_pairs|length)==1' \

~~~

Install the receipt-convergent final body for `core/tests/test-task7-sequential-plan.sh`:

~~~bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
REPO=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd -P)
PLAN=${POLYLANE_PLAN:-$REPO/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-16-codex-persistent-autonomy.md}
TMP=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-task7-final-bodies.XXXXXX")
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT
fail() { printf 'FAIL %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }
extract_after() {
  local marker output
  marker=$1 output=$2
  awk -v marker="$marker" '
    index($0,marker){seen=1;next}
    seen && ($0=="~~~bash" || $0=="```bash") {
      closing=substr($0,1,3);body=1;next}
    body && $0==closing {exit}
    body {print}
  ' "$PLAN" >"$output"
  [ -s "$output" ] || fail "empty extraction: $marker"
}
files='core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-audit-reducer.sh
codex/scripts/polylane-codex-controller.sh
core/scripts/lib/polylane-controller-reducer.sh
core/scripts/polylane-controller-turn.sh
core/scripts/polylane-state.sh
core/tests/helpers/fake-tmux.sh
core/scripts/polylane-guardian.sh
core/scripts/polylane-queue.sh
core/tests/test-autonomy-soak.sh
core/tests/helpers/mock-runtime-actors.sh
core/scripts/lib/polylane-queue-reducer.sh
core/scripts/polylane-worker.sh
core/scripts/polylane-keeper.sh
core/scripts/polylane-recovery.sh
core/scripts/polylane-sentinel.sh
core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-audit-hooks.sh
core/scripts/lib/polylane-runtime-audit.sh
core/tests/helpers/focused-audit-publish-contract.sh
core/tests/test-audit-handler-receipts.sh'
while IFS= read -r file; do
  body=$TMP/body
  extract_after "Install the receipt-convergent final body for \`$file\`:" "$body"
  cmp -s "$body" "$REPO/$file" || fail "final body differs: $file"
done <<EOF
$files
EOF
printf 'PASS task7-final-bodies\n'
~~~

~~~bash

  core/scripts/polylane-recovery.sh core/tests/helpers/focused-audit-publish-contract.sh \
  core/tests/test-task7-sequential-plan.sh
for mode in pre post duplicate wake successor; do
  bash core/tests/helpers/focused-audit-publish-contract.sh "$mode" </dev/null
done
bash core/tests/test-task7-sequential-plan.sh
perl -e "alarm 900; exec @ARGV" bash core/tests/test-autonomy-soak.sh
~~~

`POLYLANE_NOW_EPOCH` unset is due at logical 0 with deadline 300; the guardian and keeper
retain their exact live generations across logical event jumps; the full soak recovers all
20 faults with no unexplained idle gap or out-of-horizon non-bootstrap queue timestamp; and
the final-body comparator is byte-identical for all 19 runtime/test artifacts.

### Task 8: Run Real Two-Cycle Continuity, Install, and Certify

**Files:**
- Create: `codex/scripts/polylane-codex-continuity.sh`
- Create: `codex/scripts/polylane-codex-skill-forward.sh`
- Create: `codex/scripts/polylane-continuity-fixture-controller.sh`
- Create: `codex/tests/test-codex-continuity.sh`
- Create: `codex/tests/test-codex-skill-forward.sh`
- Modify: `codex/references/codex-runtime.md`, `README.md`, `.polylane/SCHEMA.md`
- Installed output: `${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/skills/polylane/`

**Interfaces:**
- Gated real run:
  `POLYLANE_CODEX_CONTINUITY=1 POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL=<id>
   codex/scripts/polylane-codex-continuity.sh`.
- Hermetic test uses `POLYLANE_AGENT_CMD` plus a single-path `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD` mock
  (and separately tests `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_ARGV_FILE`) but exercises
  real tmux, stable session, guardian, two cycle promotions, recovery, and certification.
- Both hermetic and real fault injection kill an owned cycle-two builder process after
  launch; every initial and replacement builder command is generated by the ordinary
  selected agent adapter. No canary-only Codex command wrapper exists.
- Gated installed-skill forward test:
  `POLYLANE_CODEX_SKILL_FORWARD=1 POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL=<id>
  codex/scripts/polylane-codex-skill-forward.sh <installed-skill-root>`.

**Contract — hermetic continuity and installed-skill-forward tests:**

Without the opt-in flag, assert output `codex-continuity-gated-off` and exit 0. With mocks,
assert:

```text
same loop_id and tmux session for both cycles
windows controller and lanes exist during the run
cycle run_id values differ
one injected transient failure event
one automatic recovery event
two nonce-valid GO promotions
two clean exhaustive certification records
terminal COMPLETE
watch command unchanged and exact
max_unexplained_idle_gap=0
exactly 30 final suggestions, none queued
```

Real mode rejects nonempty inherited `POLYLANE_AGENT_CMD` or
`POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD` or `POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_ARGV_FILE`; add a negative fixture proving
an ambient override cannot satisfy
the live gate. Recorded controller, initial builder, replacement builder, and integrator argv
must all resolve inside the temporary installed package and contain `codex exec`,
`--sandbox workspace-write`, `approval_policy=never`, selected model, effort, and stdin
prompt marker. Hermetic mode is the only mode allowed to use overrides.

The test trap first calls ownership-validated `emergency-cleanup`, verifies no owned PID or
session remains, and only then removes its temporary repository. It never directly kills a
session while a live keeper could recreate it.

`test-codex-skill-forward.sh` checks gated-off behavior and uses a fake *outer* Codex CLI to
capture a user-like prompt without receiving this plan or expected answer. It requires the
literal `$polylane` trigger, `approval_policy=never`, disposable `-C` project, installed
skill root, state-follow monitoring, final-delivery validation, and emergency-cleanup trap.

- [ ] **Action 8.1 (2–5 minutes): Add the complete two-cycle continuity test**

Create `codex/tests/test-codex-continuity.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
REPO=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd -P)
ROOT=${POLYLANE_TEST_PACKAGE_ROOT:-$REPO/codex}
HARNESS=$ROOT/scripts/polylane-codex-continuity.sh
AUTONOMY_TMP=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-continuity-test.XXXXXX")
export AUTONOMY_TMP
cleanup() {
  original_rc=$?
  cleanup_rc=0
  if [ -f "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime/session.json" ]; then
    loop=$(jq -r '.session|sub("^polylane-";"")' "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime/session.json")
    generation=$(jq -r .generation "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime/session.json")
    POLYLANE_TEST_MODE=1 "$ROOT/scripts/polylane-finalizer.sh" emergency-cleanup \
      --project "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project" --loop-id "$loop" --generation "$generation" || cleanup_rc=$?
    if [ "$cleanup_rc" -eq 0 ]; then
      "$ROOT/scripts/polylane-process.sh" verify-absent \
        "$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/.polylane/runtime" || cleanup_rc=$?
      tmux has-session -t "polylane-$loop" 2>/dev/null && cleanup_rc=75
    fi
  fi
  trap - EXIT
  if [ "$cleanup_rc" -eq 0 ]; then rm -rf "$AUTONOMY_TMP"
  else
    printf 'continuity cleanup failed; retained fixture: %s\n' "$AUTONOMY_TMP" >&2
    exit "$cleanup_rc"
  fi
  exit "$original_rc"
}
trap cleanup EXIT
. "$REPO/core/tests/helpers/autonomy-contract.sh"
gated=$("$HARNESS")
assert_eq gated codex-continuity-gated-off "$gated"
mkdir -p "$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin"
cat >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin/mock-agent" <<'MOCK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
printf '%s\0' "$@" >>"$POLYLANE_COMMAND_LOG"
cat >/dev/null
MOCK
cat >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin/mock-controller" <<'MOCK'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
printf '%s\0' "$@" >>"$POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_LOG"
exec "$POLYLANE_PACKAGE_ROOT/scripts/polylane-continuity-fixture-controller.sh" "$@"
MOCK
chmod +x "$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin/mock-agent" "$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin/mock-controller"
export POLYLANE_COMMAND_LOG=$AUTONOMY_TMP/agent.argv POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_LOG=$AUTONOMY_TMP/controller.argv
export POLYLANE_PACKAGE_ROOT=$ROOT POLYLANE_AGENT_CMD="$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin/mock-agent {model} {prompt} {effort}"
export POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD=$AUTONOMY_TMP/bin/mock-controller POLYLANE_HERMETIC=1 POLYLANE_TEST_MODE=1
output=$(POLYLANE_CODEX_CONTINUITY=1 POLYLANE_CONTINUITY_PROJECT="$AUTONOMY_TMP/project" "$HARNESS")
assert_contains attach 'tmux attach -t polylane-' "$output"
assert_contains go CODEX-CONTINUITY-GO "$output"
result=$AUTONOMY_TMP/project/docs/polylane/continuity-result.json
assert_json same-loop '.cycle_1.loop_id==.cycle_2.loop_id' true "$result"
assert_json different-runs '.cycle_1.run_id!=.cycle_2.run_id' true "$result"
assert_json promotions '.go_promotions' 2 "$result"
assert_json promotions-derived '.go_promotions==(.promotion_evidence|length)' true "$result"
assert_json recovery '.automatic_recoveries' 1 "$result"
assert_json recovery-derived '.automatic_recoveries==(.recovery_evidence|length)' true "$result"
assert_json certifications '.clean_certifications' 2 "$result"
assert_json certifications-derived '.clean_certifications==(.certification_evidence|length)' true "$result"
assert_json terminal '.terminal' COMPLETE "$result"
assert_json suggestions '.final_suggestions' 30 "$result"
assert_json not-queued '.final_suggestions_queued' 0 "$result"
assert_json idle '.max_unexplained_idle_gap' 0 "$result"
assert_json teardown '.shutdown_stage' TEARDOWN_COMPLETE "$result"
set +e
POLYLANE_HERMETIC=0 POLYLANE_AGENT_CMD=ambient POLYLANE_CODEX_CONTINUITY=1 "$HARNESS" >"$AUTONOMY_TMP/live.out" 2>"$AUTONOMY_TMP/live.err"
rc=$?
set -e
assert_eq live-rejects-override 5 "$rc"
finish_autonomy_test test-codex-continuity.sh
```

- [ ] **Action 8.3 (2–5 minutes): Run both harness tests and capture RED**

```bash
bash codex/tests/test-codex-continuity.sh
bash codex/tests/test-codex-skill-forward.sh
```

Expected RED: both exit nonzero because their harness scripts do not exist. The tests must
not invoke a real Codex CLI.

**Contract — one-shot external lane fault injection:**

After the cycle-two builder pane is tagged with the owned loop id, run nonce, lane id, role,
generation, and the path/hash of its runner-authored immutable child identity (PID plus
process start token), the harness validates every tag, atomically records
`fault-injected-cycle-2`, emits an `external_fault_injected` runtime event, and sends TERM
then KILL only to that child if needed. It never replaces the pane command, never builds a
`codex exec` argv, and never targets the controller or integrator. The guardian must detect
the dead child, enqueue recovery, and relaunch through `polylane-run.sh` plus the normal
Codex adapter. Tests inspect both command records and fail if either contains a canary-only
wrapper or bypasses `polylane-codex-agent.sh`.

**Contract — two-cycle throwaway continuity project:**

Create an initialized git repository with a locked goal that requires two independent
small changes in two cycles. Start the real persistent Codex loop. Cycle 1 builder creates
and tests `feature-one.txt`; its integrator promotes GO. Cycle 2 builder creates and tests
`feature-two.txt`; the harness kills its first owned process, the guardian recovers and
relaunches it through the normal adapter, and the integrator promotes GO. Then run two
exhaustive fresh-checkout certifications and reach `COMPLETE`.

The harness watches structured runtime state with a test timeout only; timeout is a failed
test, never a runtime terminal state. It validates all assertions from Step 1, including
the two different cycle nonces and zero unexplained idle gap.

- [ ] **Action 8.4 (2–5 minutes): Add the deterministic hermetic controller fixture**

Create `codex/scripts/polylane-continuity-fixture-controller.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
SELF=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd -P)
if [ "${1:-}" = exec ]; then
  output=''; schema=''; project=''
  args=("$@"); for ((i=0;i<${#args[@]};i++)); do
    case ${args[$i]} in
      -o|--output-last-message) i=$((i+1)); output=${args[$i]};;
      --output-schema) i=$((i+1)); schema=${args[$i]};;
      -C|--cd) i=$((i+1)); project=${args[$i]};;
    esac
  done
  [ -n "$output" ] && [ -f "$schema" ] && [ -d "$project" ] || exit 5
  prompt=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-continuity-prompt.XXXXXX")
  trap 'rm -f "$prompt"' EXIT
  cat >"$prompt"
  turn=$(jq -er .turn_id "$prompt"); input_hash=$(jq -er .input_hash "$prompt")
  cycle=$(jq -er .immutable_input.cycle "$prompt")
  goal=$(jq -er '.immutable_input.goal_state.next_goal_ref //
    [.immutable_input.goal_state.goal_tree.items[]?|select((.status//"open")!="complete")][0].id //
    [.immutable_input.goal_state.milestones[]?|select((.status//"open")!="complete")][0].id' "$prompt")
  feature=feature-$([ "$cycle" -eq 1 ] && printf one || printf two).txt
  jq -n --arg turn "$turn" --arg input_hash "$input_hash" --arg goal "$goal" \
    --arg feature "$feature" \
    '{schema_version:1,turn_id:$turn,input_hash:$input_hash,action:"START_CYCLE",goal_ref:$goal,
      rationale:("hermetic cycle for "+$feature),builders:[{id:"builder",brief:("create and verify "+$feature),
        activities:[("create "+$feature),"run the declared verification"],
        ownership_globs:[$feature],verification_argv:["/usr/bin/test","-f",$feature]}],
      integrator:{id:"integrator",brief:"integrate and verify the authenticated builder commit",
        activities:["merge the authenticated builder commit","run integration verification"],
        ownership_globs:[$feature],verification_argv:["/usr/bin/test","-f",$feature]},
      certification:{deadline_seconds:300,
        checks:{accessibility:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},broken_flows:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},
          documentation:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},functional:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},
          performance:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},regression:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},
          security:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},stubs:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]}},
        fresh_checkout:{boot:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},build:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]},
          install:{argv:["/usr/bin/true"]}}}}' >"$output"
  printf '%s\n' '{"type":"turn.completed"}'
  exit 0
fi
[ "$#" -eq 2 ] || exit 2
project=$(cd "$1" && pwd -P); loop=$2
export POLYLANE_CODEX_CLI=$SELF/polylane-continuity-fixture-controller.sh
exec "$SELF/polylane-codex-controller.sh" "$project" "$loop"
```

Run: `chmod +x codex/scripts/polylane-continuity-fixture-controller.sh && bash -n codex/scripts/polylane-continuity-fixture-controller.sh`

Expected: exit 0 with no output.

- [ ] **Action 8.5 (2–5 minutes): Create the gated continuity harness and owned fault injection**

Create `codex/scripts/polylane-codex-continuity.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
[ "${POLYLANE_CODEX_CONTINUITY:-0}" = 1 ] || { printf 'codex-continuity-gated-off\n'; exit 0; }
ROOT=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd -P); BIN=$ROOT/scripts
hermetic=${POLYLANE_HERMETIC:-0}
if [ "$hermetic" != 1 ] && { [ -n "${POLYLANE_AGENT_CMD:-}" ] || [ -n "${POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_CMD:-}" ] || [ -n "${POLYLANE_CONTROLLER_ARGV_FILE:-}" ]; }; then
  printf 'live continuity rejects command overrides\n' >&2; exit 5
fi
if [ "$hermetic" = 1 ]; then model=${POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL:-test-model}
else
  [ -z "${POLYLANE_CONTINUITY_PROJECT:-}" ] || { printf 'live continuity always owns a fresh project\n' >&2; exit 5; }
  [ -f "$ROOT/SKILL.md" ] && [ -x "$BIN/polylane-codex-loop.sh" ] || exit 5
  "$BIN/polylane-dispatch.sh" audit "$ROOT"
  codex_cli=$(command -v codex); codex_cli=$(cd "$(dirname "$codex_cli")" && pwd -P)/$(basename "$codex_cli")
  if [ -L "$codex_cli" ]; then command -v realpath >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 5; codex_physical=$(realpath "$codex_cli")
  else codex_physical=$codex_cli; fi
  [ -f "$codex_physical" ] && [ ! -L "$codex_physical" ] && [ -x "$codex_physical" ] || exit 5
  model=$("$BIN/polylane-codex-model.sh" resolve-model "${POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL:-}")
fi
if [ "$hermetic" = 1 ]; then project=${POLYLANE_CONTINUITY_PROJECT:-$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-continuity.XXXXXX")}
else project=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-continuity-live.XXXXXX"); fi
mkdir -p "$project"; project=$(cd "$project" && pwd -P)
created=0; [ -d "$project/.git" ] || { created=1; git -C "$project" init -q; git -C "$project" config user.email canary@example.invalid; git -C "$project" config user.name canary; }
cat >"$project/GOAL.md" <<'GOAL'
Build two independently verified cycles. Cycle one creates feature-one.txt containing one.
Cycle two creates feature-two.txt containing two. Promote each only after its check passes.
GOAL
if [ "$created" -eq 1 ]; then git -C "$project" add GOAL.md; git -C "$project" commit -qm goal; fi
mkdir -p "$project/docs/polylane" "$project/.polylane/runtime/skill-inventory" \
  "$project/.polylane/runtime/skill-snapshots"
inventory=$project/.polylane/runtime/skill-inventory/inventory.json
POLYLANE_CODEX_SKILL_ROOTS="$ROOT/bundled-skills" \
  "$BIN/polylane-codex-skills.sh" inventory "$inventory" \
  "$project/.polylane/runtime/skill-snapshots"
chmod 400 "$inventory"
inventory_hash=$(if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then sha256sum "$inventory"; \
  else shasum -a 256 "$inventory"; fi | awk '{print "sha256:"$1}')
jq -n --arg inventory "$inventory" --arg inventory_hash "$inventory_hash" \
  '{schema_version:1,complete:false,next_goal_ref:"two-features",
    milestones:[{id:"two-features",status:"open"}],
    goal_tree:{items:[{id:"two-features",status:"open",weight:100}]},
    skill_inventory:{inventory_path:$inventory,inventory_hash:$inventory_hash}}' \
  >"$project/docs/polylane/max-state.json"
export POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL=$model POLYLANE_BUILDER_MODEL=$model POLYLANE_INTEGRATOR_MODEL=$model
"$BIN/polylane-codex-loop.sh" "$project"
runtime=$project/.polylane/runtime; state=$project/docs/polylane/controller-state.json
RUNTIME_DIR=$runtime; export RUNTIME_DIR
. "$BIN/lib/polylane-runtime-common.sh"
. "$BIN/lib/polylane-runtime-events.sh"
. "$BIN/lib/polylane-runtime-audit.sh"
. "$BIN/lib/polylane-runtime-retention.sh"
auth_file=$runtime/audit-auth.key
if [ ! -e "$auth_file" ]; then
  "$BIN/polylane-hmac.py" keygen "$auth_file"
fi
loop=$(jq -r .loop_id "$state"); session=polylane-$loop; watch="tmux attach -t $session"
printf '%s\n' "$watch"
deadline=$(( $(date +%s) + ${POLYLANE_CONTINUITY_TIMEOUT:-900} )); injected=0
last_observed_at=$(runtime_now)
inject_cycle_two() {
  [ "$injected" -eq 0 ] || return 0
  pane=$(tmux list-panes -t "$session:lanes" -F '#{pane_id} #{@polylane-loop-id} #{@polylane-run-id} #{@polylane-lane-id} #{@polylane-role} #{@polylane-generation} #{@polylane-identity-path} #{@polylane-identity-hash}' |
    awk -v loop="$loop" '$2==loop && $3!="" && $4!="" && $5=="builder" && $6 ~ /^[0-9]+$/ {print; exit}')
  [ -n "$pane" ] || return 0
  set -- $pane; pane_id=$1; run_id=$3; lane=$4; role=$5; generation=$6; identity=$7; identity_hash=$8
  [ "$role" = builder ] && [ -n "$lane" ] && [ "$run_id" = "$(jq -r .controller.run_id "$runtime/current/state.json")" ] &&
    [ "$(jq -r .controller.cycle "$runtime/current/state.json")" = 2 ] || return 5
  case $identity in "$runtime"/lane-identities/*.json) :;; *) return 5;; esac
  jq -e --arg loop "$loop" --arg run "$run_id" --arg lane "$lane" --argjson generation "$generation" '
    .loop_id==$loop and .run_id==$run and .cycle==2 and .lane_id==$lane and .role=="builder" and
    .generation==$generation and (.pid|type=="number" and .>1) and (.start_token|type=="string" and length>0)' \
    "$identity" >/dev/null || return 5
  child=$(jq -r .pid "$identity"); child_token=$(jq -r .start_token "$identity")
  [ "$(runtime_start_token "$child" || true)" = "$child_token" ] || return 5
  marker=$runtime/fault-injected-cycle-2
  ( set -C; printf '%s %s %s\n' "$run_id" "$pane_id" "$child" >"$marker" ) 2>/dev/null || return 0
  fault=cycle-2-builder; recovery=same_nonce_lane_relaunch; at=$(runtime_now)
  nonce="continuity-$run_id-$child-$at"; prior=$(runtime_sha256 "$runtime/current/state.json")
  detail=$runtime/.continuity-detail.$$.json
  jq -n --arg run "$run_id" --arg pane "$pane_id" --arg lane "$lane" --arg role "$role" \
    --arg identity "$identity" --arg identity_hash "$identity_hash" --arg start_token "$child_token" \
    --argjson generation "$generation" --argjson pid "$child" \
    '{run_id:$run,pane_id:$pane,lane_id:$lane,role:$role,generation:$generation,
      identity:{path:$identity,hash:$identity_hash,pid:$pid,start_token:$start_token}}' >"$detail"
  detail_hash=$(runtime_sha256 "$detail"); unsigned=$runtime/.continuity-unsigned.$$.json
  jq -n --arg fault "$fault" --arg recovery "$recovery" --arg nonce "$nonce" \
    --argjson at "$at" --arg prior "$prior" --arg detail_hash "$detail_hash" \
    '{schema_version:2,fault_id:$fault,recovery:$recovery,nonce:$nonce,at:$at,
      prior_state_hash:$prior,detail_hash:$detail_hash}' >"$unsigned"
  unsigned_hash=$(jq -Sc . "$unsigned" | runtime_sha256_stdin)
  artifact_source=$runtime/.continuity-artifact.$$.json
  jq --arg hash "$unsigned_hash" '.+{unsigned_hash:$hash}' "$unsigned" >"$artifact_source"
  auth_tag=$(runtime_audit_auth_tag "$auth_file" "$artifact_source")
  jq --arg auth "$auth_tag" '.+{auth_tag:$auth}' "$artifact_source" >"$artifact_source.next"
  mv -f "$artifact_source.next" "$artifact_source"
  artifact=$(runtime_audit_seal "$runtime/audit/injections" "$artifact_source")
  artifact_hash=$(runtime_sha256 "$artifact"); payload=$runtime/.continuity-payload.$$.json
  jq -n --arg fault "$fault" --arg recovery "$recovery" --arg nonce "$nonce" --arg path "$artifact" \
    --arg hash "$artifact_hash" --arg auth "$auth_tag" \
    '{fault_id:$fault,recovery:$recovery,nonce:$nonce,
      artifact_path:$path,artifact_hash:$hash,auth_tag:$auth}' >"$payload"
  "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" emit continuity fault_injected "fault:$fault" \
    --fingerprint "$artifact_hash" --payload-file "$payload"
  "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" reduce
  rm -f "$detail" "$unsigned" "$artifact_source" "$payload"
  "$BIN/polylane-process.sh" fence-identity "$runtime" "$identity" "$identity_hash"
  [ "$(runtime_start_token "$child" || true)" != "$child_token" ] || return 75
  injected=1
}
while [ "$(date +%s)" -lt "$deadline" ]; do
  inject_cycle_two
  before_revision=$(jq -r .revision "$runtime/current/state.json")
  terminal=$(jq -r '.terminal // ""' "$runtime/current/state.json")
  stage=$(jq -r '.shutdown.stage // ""' "$runtime/current/state.json")
  [ "$terminal" != COMPLETE ] || { [ "$stage" = TEARDOWN_COMPLETE ] && break; }
  "$BIN/polylane-state.sh" follow --project "$project" --after-revision "$(jq -r .revision "$runtime/current/state.json")" --timeout 1 >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  now_observed=$(runtime_now)
  [ "$now_observed" -le "$last_observed_at" ] ||
    "$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" audit-interval --from "$last_observed_at" --to "$now_observed"
  after_revision=$(jq -r .revision "$runtime/current/state.json")
  if [ "$after_revision" = "$before_revision" ] && [ "$terminal" != COMPLETE ]; then
    jq -e --argjson now "$now_observed" '
      any(.queue.items[]?;(.status=="running" or .status=="starting") or
        ((.status=="ready" or .status=="scheduled") and .next_action_at>$now)) or
      any(.leases[]?;.deadline_at>$now) or .provider.state=="unavailable" or
      .terminal=="WAITING_FOR_USER"' "$runtime/current/state.json" >/dev/null || {
        printf 'continuity uncovered idle tick\n' >&2; exit 5; }
  fi
  last_observed_at=$now_observed
done
[ "$(jq -r '.shutdown.stage // ""' "$runtime/current/state.json")" = TEARDOWN_COMPLETE ] || { printf 'continuity timeout\n' >&2; exit 124; }
result=$project/docs/polylane/continuity-result.json
cycle1_matches=("$project"/docs/polylane/cycle-1-result-*.json)
cycle2_matches=("$project"/docs/polylane/cycle-2-result-*.json)
[ "${#cycle1_matches[@]}" -eq 1 ] && [ -f "${cycle1_matches[0]}" ] || exit 5
[ "${#cycle2_matches[@]}" -eq 1 ] && [ -f "${cycle2_matches[0]}" ] || exit 5
cycle1=${cycle1_matches[0]}; cycle2=${cycle2_matches[0]}
events=$runtime/.continuity-events.$$.jsonl; : >"$events"
runtime_verify_raw_chain
latest_checkpoint=$(runtime_latest_checkpoint || true)
[ -z "$latest_checkpoint" ] || runtime_verify_checkpoint "$latest_checkpoint"
seen=$runtime/.continuity-seen.$$.txt; : >"$seen"
for event in "$runtime"/events/*.json "$runtime"/pinned-events/*.json; do
  [ -f "$event" ] && [ ! -L "$event" ] || continue
  sequence=$(jq -r .sequence "$event"); previous=$(jq -r '.previous_event_hash//""' "$event")
  runtime_verify_event_file "$event" "$sequence" "$previous" || exit 5
  event_hash=$(jq -r .event_hash "$event")
  grep -Fqx -- "$event_hash" "$seen" && continue
  printf '%s\n' "$event_hash" >>"$seen"; jq -c . "$event" >>"$events"
done
promotions=$runtime/.continuity-promotions.$$.json
jq -s --slurpfile state "$runtime/current/state.json" '
  [.[] as $event | select($event.actor=="controller" and $event.type=="controller_transaction" and
    $event.payload.kind=="advance_stage" and $event.payload.next_stage=="PROMOTED" and
    ($state[0].controller.applied_transactions|index($event.payload.transaction_id)!=null)) |
    {transaction_id:$event.payload.transaction_id,event_id:$event.sequence,event_hash:$event.event_hash,
     evidence:$event.payload.evidence}] | unique_by(.transaction_id)' "$events" >"$promotions"
injection=$(jq -s -c '[.[]|select(.type=="fault_injected" and .payload.fault_id=="cycle-2-builder")]|unique_by(.event_hash)' "$events")
[ "$(printf '%s\n' "$injection" | jq length)" -eq 1 ] || exit 5
cause_hash=$(printf '%s\n' "$injection" | jq -r '.[0].event_hash')
recovery_pairs=$runtime/.continuity-recoveries.$$.json
jq --arg cause "$cause_hash" '[.audit.recovery_pairs|to_entries[]|
  select(.value.cause_event_hash==$cause)|.value]' "$runtime/current/state.json" >"$recovery_pairs"
while IFS= read -r evidence_path; do
  evidence_hash=$(jq -r --arg path "$evidence_path" '.[]|select(.evidence_path==$path)|.evidence_hash' "$recovery_pairs")
  evidence=$(runtime_audit_validate_sealed "$runtime/audit/recovery-evidence" "$evidence_path" "$evidence_hash")
  runtime_audit_validate_postcondition "$(jq -r --arg path "$evidence_path" '.[]|select(.evidence_path==$path)|.recovery' "$recovery_pairs")" "$evidence"
done < <(jq -r '.[].evidence_path' "$recovery_pairs")
certifications=$runtime/.continuity-certifications.$$.json
jq -s --slurpfile state "$runtime/current/state.json" '
  [.[] as $event | select($event.type=="convergence_transaction" and
    $event.payload.kind=="certification" and ($event.payload.body.record.findings|length)==0 and
    $state[0].convergence.applied_transactions[$event.payload.transaction_id].event_id==$event.sequence) |
    {transaction_id:$event.payload.transaction_id,event_id:$event.sequence,event_hash:$event.event_hash,
     attempt_id:$event.payload.body.record.attempt_id,
     certification_id:$event.payload.body.record.certification_id,
     checkout_id:$event.payload.body.record.checkout_id}] | unique_by(.transaction_id)' "$events" >"$certifications"
finalization=$runtime/.continuity-finalization.$$.json
jq -s --slurpfile state "$runtime/current/state.json" '
  [.[] as $event | select($event.type=="convergence_transaction" and
    $event.payload.kind=="finalization" and
    $state[0].convergence.applied_transactions[$event.payload.transaction_id].event_id==$event.sequence) |
    {transaction_id:$event.payload.transaction_id,event_id:$event.sequence,event_hash:$event.event_hash,
     suggestion_hash:$event.payload.body.suggestion_hash}] | unique_by(.transaction_id)' "$events" >"$finalization"
from=$(jq -s 'map(.at)|min' "$events"); to=$(jq -s 'map(.at)|max' "$events")
"$BIN/polylane-runtime.sh" --dir "$runtime" audit-interval --from "$from" --to "$to"
idle=$(jq '[.audit.intervals[]?.unexplained_gap]|max//0' "$runtime/current/state.json")
jq -n --slurpfile c1 "$cycle1" --slurpfile c2 "$cycle2" \
  --slurpfile promotions "$promotions" --slurpfile recoveries "$recovery_pairs" \
  --slurpfile certifications "$certifications" --slurpfile finalization "$finalization" \
  --slurpfile state "$runtime/current/state.json" --arg cause_hash "$cause_hash" --argjson idle "$idle" \
  '{cycle_1:$c1[0],cycle_2:$c2[0],promotion_evidence:$promotions[0],
    go_promotions:($promotions[0]|length),fault_cause_event_hash:$cause_hash,
    recovery_evidence:$recoveries[0],automatic_recoveries:($recoveries[0]|length),
    certification_evidence:$certifications[0],clean_certifications:($certifications[0]|length),
    finalization_evidence:$finalization[0],terminal:$state[0].terminal,
    shutdown_stage:$state[0].shutdown.stage,
    final_suggestions:($state[0].convergence.finalization.suggestions.suggestions|length),
    final_suggestions_queued:([$state[0].queue.items[]|select(.kind=="final_suggestions")]|length),
    max_unexplained_idle_gap:$idle}' >"$result"
rm -f "$events" "$seen" "$promotions" "$recovery_pairs" "$certifications" "$finalization"
jq -e '.cycle_1.loop_id==.cycle_2.loop_id and .cycle_1.run_id!=.cycle_2.run_id and
  .go_promotions==(.promotion_evidence|length) and .go_promotions==2 and
  .automatic_recoveries==(.recovery_evidence|length) and .automatic_recoveries==1 and
  .clean_certifications==(.certification_evidence|length) and .clean_certifications>=2 and
  (.finalization_evidence|length)==1 and .terminal=="COMPLETE" and
  .shutdown_stage=="TEARDOWN_COMPLETE" and .final_suggestions==30 and .final_suggestions_queued==0 and
  .max_unexplained_idle_gap==0' "$result" >/dev/null
printf 'CODEX-CONTINUITY-GO\n'
```

**Contract — installed-skill forward harness:**

The harness validates its installed-root argument, creates a disposable git project with a
small locked `GOAL.md`, then starts a fresh outer Codex CLI using the literal prompt shown in
Step 9. It captures outer output, discovers the owned loop only from validated project/tmux
state, follows it to a terminal state with test-only wall timeout, and verifies watch/final
delivery/teardown evidence. Its trap calls `emergency-cleanup` before deleting the project.
It never tells the fresh Codex how Polylane is implemented or what files to produce.

- [ ] **Action 8.7 (2–5 minutes): Create the gated installed-skill forward harness**

Create `codex/scripts/polylane-codex-skill-forward.sh` with this complete body:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
root=${1:-}
[ -n "$root" ] || exit 2
root=$(cd "$root" && pwd -P)
[ -f "$root/SKILL.md" ] && [ -x "$root/scripts/polylane-codex-loop.sh" ] || exit 5
[ "${POLYLANE_CODEX_SKILL_FORWARD:-0}" = 1 ] || { printf 'codex-skill-forward-gated-off\n'; exit 0; }
model=$($root/scripts/polylane-codex-model.sh resolve-model "${POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL:-}")
project=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-skill-forward.XXXXXX")
cleanup() {
  original_rc=$?
  cleanup_rc=0
  record=$project/.polylane/runtime/session.json
  if [ -f "$record" ]; then
    loop=$(jq -r '.session|sub("^polylane-";"")' "$record"); generation=$(jq -r .generation "$record")
    POLYLANE_TEST_MODE=1 "$root/scripts/polylane-finalizer.sh" emergency-cleanup \
      --project "$project" --loop-id "$loop" --generation "$generation" || cleanup_rc=$?
    if [ "$cleanup_rc" -eq 0 ]; then
      "$root/scripts/polylane-process.sh" verify-absent "$project/.polylane/runtime" || cleanup_rc=$?
      tmux has-session -t "polylane-$loop" 2>/dev/null && cleanup_rc=75
    fi
  fi
  trap - EXIT
  if [ "$cleanup_rc" -eq 0 ]; then rm -rf "$project"
  else
    printf 'skill-forward cleanup failed; retained fixture: %s\n' "$project" >&2
    exit "$cleanup_rc"
  fi
  exit "$original_rc"
}
trap cleanup EXIT
git -C "$project" init -q; git -C "$project" config user.email forward@example.invalid; git -C "$project" config user.name forward
printf 'Create verified hello.txt containing hello, then continue autonomously through completion.\n' >"$project/GOAL.md"
git -C "$project" add GOAL.md; git -C "$project" commit -qm goal
prompt='Use $polylane to build the project described in GOAL.md autonomously'
printf '%s\n' "$prompt" | codex exec -C "$project" --sandbox workspace-write -c approval_policy=never --model "$model" - >"$project/outer-codex.log"
if [ "${POLYLANE_HERMETIC:-0}" = 1 ]; then
  POLYLANE_CODEX_CONTINUITY=1 POLYLANE_HERMETIC=1 POLYLANE_CONTINUITY_PROJECT="$project" \
    "$root/scripts/polylane-codex-continuity.sh" >"$project/hermetic-loop.log"
fi
deadline=$(( $(date +%s) + ${POLYLANE_FORWARD_TIMEOUT:-900} ))
while [ "$(date +%s)" -lt "$deadline" ]; do
  state=$project/.polylane/runtime/current/state.json
  if [ -f "$state" ] && [ "$(jq -r '.shutdown.stage // ""' "$state")" = TEARDOWN_COMPLETE ]; then break; fi
  [ -f "$state" ] || { sleep 0.1; continue; }
  "$root/scripts/polylane-state.sh" follow --project "$project" --after-revision "$(jq -r .revision "$state")" --timeout 1 >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
done
state=$project/.polylane/runtime/current/state.json
[ -f "$state" ] && [ "$(jq -r .terminal "$state")" = COMPLETE ] && [ "$(jq -r .shutdown.stage "$state")" = TEARDOWN_COMPLETE ] || exit 124
final=$project/docs/polylane/final-suggestions.json; delivery=$project/docs/polylane/final-delivery.json
"$root/scripts/polylane-suggestions.sh" validate-final "$final" "$(jq -r .loop_id "$state")"
"$root/scripts/polylane-suggestions.sh" render-final "$final" "$delivery" >"$project/final-render.txt"
[ "$(grep -c '^- ' "$project/final-render.txt")" -eq 30 ] || exit 5
printf 'CODEX-SKILL-FORWARD-HERMETIC-GO\n'
```

**Contract — final runtime documentation:**

Document active phases, the two terminal states, progress leases, provider recovery,
input handoff, stable watch command, skill kits, perfection, final suggestions, and resume.
Schema documents every durable/runtime JSON field and the `loop_id` versus cycle `run_id`
distinction.

- [ ] **Action 8.11 (2–5 minutes): Apply exact final runtime documentation**

Append this exact section to `README.md`:

```markdown
## Persistent Codex autonomy

The Codex package runs one stable owned tmux session named `polylane-<loop_id>` with
`controller` and `lanes` windows. Every launch or resume returns the standalone command
`tmux attach -t polylane-<loop_id>`. `loop_id` stays fixed; every cycle receives a new
`run_id`. Work continues through typed recovery and provider probes until verified COMPLETE
or a persisted WAITING_FOR_USER question. COMPLETE requires two clean exhaustive
fresh-checkout certifications, then exactly 30 informational suggestions. The final response
waits for `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE` and remains deterministic at-least-once until a later receipt.
```

Append this exact section to `codex/references/codex-runtime.md`:

```markdown
## State and recovery reference

- Active phases: `WORKING`, `RECOVERING`.
- Terminal values: `COMPLETE`, `WAITING_FOR_USER` only.
- Default proof lease: 90 seconds; heartbeat and duplicate fingerprints do not renew it.
- Guardian fallback: five seconds; event wakes run sooner.
- Provider outages keep `provider_probe` scheduled and run typed offline work.
- Input answers resume the same `loop_id` transactionally.
- Ordinary councils have three independent members; risk/final councils have five including
  `ADVERSARY`.
- Completion requires two identical-current-product exhaustive certifications and exact 30
  beyond-scope informational suggestions.
- Foreground delivery waits for `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE`; a render attempt is not a receipt.
```

Append this exact schema block to `.polylane/SCHEMA.md`:

```markdown
## Persistent runtime schema v1

`docs/polylane/controller-state.json` fields: `schema_version`, `revision`,
`runtime_revision`, `loop_id`, `agent`, `project_root`, `phase`, `terminal`, `stage`, `cycle`,
`cycle_run_id`, `current_action_id`, `last_progress_at`, `next_action_at`, `session`,
`provider`, `recovery`, `terminal_evidence`.

`.polylane/runtime/current/state.json` additionally owns `last_event_sequence`, `guardian`,
`actors`, `leases`, `queue`, `queue_summary`, `active_input`, `input_history`, `shutdown`,
and `max_unexplained_idle_gap`. Queue claims include guardian generation, worker PID/start
token, opaque claim token, proof fingerprint, and deadline. `loop_id` identifies the full
multi-cycle task; cycle `run_id` is a fresh nonce and can never be reused for marker,
promotion, or recovery acceptance.
```

```bash
rg -n 'TEARDOWN_COMPLETE|run_id|WAITING_FOR_USER|90 seconds|ADVERSARY' README.md codex/references/codex-runtime.md .polylane/SCHEMA.md
```

Expected: all required terms appear in their relevant document.

- [ ] **Action 8.13 (2–5 minutes to launch): Activate, certify, or automatically roll back the user package**

```bash
set -e
model=$(codex/scripts/polylane-codex-model.sh \
  resolve-model "${POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL:-}")
active=$(./codex/install.sh --print-user-dest)
activation=$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-activation.XXXXXX")
tmp=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/polylane-final.XXXXXX")
certified=0
rollback_active() {
  rc=$?
  trap - EXIT INT TERM
  if [ "$certified" != 1 ] && [ -s "$activation" ]; then
    if core/scripts/polylane-package.sh rollback "$activation"; then
      core/scripts/polylane-package.sh wait-activation "$activation" || rc=1
    else
      rc=1
    fi
  fi
  rm -f "$activation"
  rm -rf "$tmp"
  exit "$rc"
}
trap rollback_active EXIT INT TERM
core/scripts/polylane-package.sh migration-preflight "$active"
./codex/install.sh --user --activation-record "$activation" \
  --certification-owner-pid "$$"
"$active/scripts/polylane-dispatch.sh" audit "$active"
POLYLANE_TEST_PACKAGE_ROOT="$active" bash codex/tests/test-codex-continuity.sh
python3 "${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}/skills/.system/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py" "$active"
POLYLANE_CODEX_SKILL_FORWARD=1 POLYLANE_CODEX_MODEL="$model" \
  codex/scripts/polylane-codex-skill-forward.sh "$active"
./codex/install.sh --dest "$tmp/polylane"
fresh_tree=$(cd -P "$tmp/polylane" && pwd)
active_tree=$(cd -P "$active" && pwd)
diff -qr "$fresh_tree" "$active_tree"
core/scripts/polylane-package.sh certify-activation "$activation"
core/scripts/polylane-package.sh wait-activation "$activation"
certified=1
rm -f "$activation"
rm -rf "$tmp"
trap - EXIT INT TERM
```

Expected: installer exits 0, the active installed skill passes its own hermetic tmux
continuity path and the Codex skill validator. The forward harness creates a disposable
repository containing only a user-style locked goal, starts a fresh real Codex CLI with the
prompt `Use $polylane to build the project described in GOAL.md autonomously`, and gives it
the installed skill artifact—not this plan or expected implementation. It asserts the skill
resolves helpers under `$active`, emits the owned attach command, launches normal tmux Codex
workers, reaches verified COMPLETE, renders the deterministic 30-item final delivery, and
leaves no owned process/session after cleanup. The complete package trees are then identical.
Any ordinary failure after the public pointer swap restores the previously active validated
package through the shell trap. If the publisher or certification shell receives SIGKILL,
the independently started pre-swap activation guard performs the same compare-and-swap
rollback. Commitment occurs only after every check and `certify-activation`; neither path
leaves an uncertified candidate active or overwrites a concurrent newer activation.
Both live gates use the same tested resolver as normal controller/lane launch. They require
user input only if neither an explicit override nor a valid configured Codex model exists;
an unset environment variable by itself never pauses execution.

Expected: clean worktree, all planned commits present, no continuity/canary session, and no
unmerged lane branch. Runtime teardown evidence must be `TEARDOWN_COMPLETE`, and no owned
keeper, sentinel, guardian, finalizer, controller, or worker PID may remain. Do not kill
unrelated tmux sessions. Create no empty verification
commit.

## Plan-Specific Spec Coverage

| Requirement | Task |
|---|---|
| Persistent controller, stable session, resume | 1 and 4 |
| Exact one-line tmux command | 1 |
| Only COMPLETE / WAITING_FOR_USER terminal | 4 |
| Five-second watchdog, 90-second progress lease | 2 |
| Heartbeat is not progress; quiet deadlines | 2 |
| Typed recovery ladder and no repeated tactic | 3 |
| Offline work and visible provider probes | 3 |
| Empty queue reconstruction | 3 and 7 |
| Fast event-driven cycles and concurrent work | 5 |
| Three-member ordinary / five-member final council | 5 |
| Informational cost ledger | 5 |
| Two consecutive clean exhaustive certifications | 6 |
| Per-cycle ranked suggestions and exact final 30 | 6 |
| Accelerated multi-hour injected-fault soak | 7 |
| Real two-cycle Codex/tmux recovery proof | 8 |
| Active verified Codex installation | 8 |
